The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Engineer to strictly adhere to the provisions and guidelines in the preparation of the ABC and perform a detailed review of the Program of Works.


The contractor refunded the said OCM mark-up on the Occupational Safety and Health Program.


Instructed the TWG for a thorough review of the submitted detail estimates of the bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC and the Local Development Council (LDC) to consider revisiting the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) of the municipality for the past years and determine the barangays that least benefited from funding for the implementation of PPAs, thereafter, consider the inclusion of the proposed projects of such barangays in the next year budget.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor directed the MPDC and the Local Development Council (LDC) to consider revisiting the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) of the municipality for the past years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
 
OIC-Municipal Engineer to regularly inspect the projects to monitor the adequately adhered to Occupational Safety and Health standards and prepare the Statement of Work Accomplished based on actual accomplishments; and

Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of directly deducting on the disbursement vouchers the OSH since there are proper authorities that impose such sanctions. 

Likewise, we encourage the Management to regularly convene with the Department of Labor and Employment concerning the identified violations of the OSH in accordance with Intergovernmental Coordination and Cooperation as prescribed in Section 31 of the IRR.
observations.managementAction:
The OIC-Municipal Engineer assured the Audit Team that guidelines are adhered to with regard to Occupational Safety and Health Standards.


The Municipal Accountant guaranteed to stop deducting on the DV the OSH for every project.


Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to review/re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures of the AICS Program and adhere to the guidelines outlined in the Manual of Operation. 
 

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all financial assistance under AICS will undergo the process of assessment by the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office personnel.
observations.managementAction:
Local Chief Executive directed the MSWDO to review/re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures of the AICS Program and adhere to the guidelines outlined in the Manual of Operation. 

The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office personnel assured that assessment for financial assistance under AICS is done according to the guidelines in their Manual of Operation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resources Officer to:
 
Revisit and study the organizational structure of the municipality, particularly the staffing pattern of all its offices, and consider abolishing items not regarded as vital, specifically those that have been left vacant for an extended period, and consider providing additional manpower complement to Municipal Health Office compliant with RA No. 7305; and

Fill-up all vacant positions that are still considered vital by hiring new employees or expediting the promotion of deserving job-order employees.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Mayor directed the Human Resources Officer to revisit and study the organizational structure of the municipality, particularly the staffing pattern of all its offices.



The LGU assured that vacant positions be filled-up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Health Officer to facilitate the certification requirements by the PhilHealth so that it could avail of the benefits package for its patients, which in turn, would help the municipality defraying expenses related thereto, and provide better services for its patients and a more conducive working environment to its isolation facility personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor initially enjoined the MHO to facilitate the said certification requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Cash in Bank, LCCA and other affected accounts pertaining to the misstatements; and 



The Municipal Treasurer to call the attention or inform the payees of those cancelled checks to surrender the stale checks to her so she could issue new checks.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Cash in Bank, LCCA and other affected accounts pertaining to the misstatements.


The Municipal Treasurer called the attention of the payees of those cancelled checks to surrender the stale checks to her so she could issue new checks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor 	direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

maintain list of contracts paid to all contractors of the municipality as basis to collect tax due on the ensuing year; and


collect the business taxes assessed on three contractors with corresponding interest, surcharges and penalties as stated on the Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction:
The BPLO maintained list of contracts paid to all contractors of the Municipality.



Collection of the unpaid taxes against the three contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Recognize in the General Fund books the PPEs totaling P5,681,540.00 purchased out of the Trust Fund in CY 2023 and thereafter record also the corresponding depreciation expenses to fairly present the affected PPE accounts; and b. Review the Trust Fund books in the previous years to check if there have been similar cases of unrecognized PPEs and recognize them also in the General Fund books, as well as the corresponding depreciation expenses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has been directed to take the necessary steps in the proper recognition of the PPEs purchased from the Trust Fund, and to review the Trust Fund books to verify any similar cases of unrecognized PPEs and recognize them properly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Ensure that all cash advances, except for petty cash fund, are fully liquidated at year-end and any unexpended balance shall be refunded by the accountable officer; and b. Require every official who will receive cash advance to strictly observe the prescribed period of liquidation of his/her cash advance to safeguard the cash from the risk of misuse or loss.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has been directed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned municipal officials to investigate the cause/s of the substantial decrease in the actual collections from Share from Economic Zones and propose specific measures to improve the financial performance of the Municipality, benchmarking from its performance in prior years, and to furnish the Audit Team with a written report thereon for validation purpose. Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds by limiting the disbursements within the total income realized during the year to prevent the recurrence of cash overdraft. It is likewise recommended that they take concrete and effective measures to cover the cash overdraft with the collections from the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has been instructed to look into the cause/s of the substantial decrease in the actual collections from the share from economic zone and propose measures to improve the financial performance of the Municipality. The Municipality have also requested assistance from the Philippine Economic Zone Authority (PEZA) for data which may help the former understand the cause of the decrease. The Management gave assurance that a more detailed report will be provided to the Audit Team as soon as they have processed the data that will be provided by the PEZA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Implement the procedures for the proper disposition of the dormant accounts totaling P5,181,600.34 and thereafter facilitate the filing of the request to write-off/derecognize from the books the dormant accounts pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008; and b. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of all dormant accounts in the books of the Municipality to ensure the fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has been directed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant to meticulously verify and ensure that the Performance Security and Warranty Security submitted by the winning bidders were dated and valid within the prescribed periods in order to fully protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and Municipal Accountant have been directed to check all the documents submitted by the winning bidders and ensure that the Performance Security and Warranty Security are dated accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer to: a. Submit the duly accomplished PIF to the Office of the Supervising Auditor or Audit Team Leader and the GIF of GSIS on or before April 30 of each year to be used as basis for the assessment of the general insurance coverage of all the Municipality’s insurable properties; and b. Insure with the GIF of GSIS all the Municipality’s insurable assets and properties with the appropriate insurance coverage to protect the Municipality thru indemnification in cases of damage or loss due to fortuitous events. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the annual budget the allocation of sufficient appropriation for the payment of insurance premiums in order to ensure that all the Municipality’s insurable properties will be properly insured with the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed compliance with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the status of the dormant bank account and take appropriate action thereon; and b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all the accounts maintained and prepare necessary adjusting entries to record all reconciling and unrecorded items.
observations.managementAction: The bank accounts was prepared and adjustments of valid reconciling items made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct: a) The planning process of the development projects of the LGU. b) The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to expedite the implementation of the PPAs and ensure that physical accomplishments are closely-monitored. c) MPDO and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the PPAs identified in its Annual Investment Plan have specific target dates of implementation, so as to guide the management in monitoring the status of implementation for each PPA.
observations.managementAction: LGU was trying to expedite the identification of the PPA to be implemented; Not the PPA to be implemented as officers such as the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to ensure enough preparation process for the procurement is also faulted on the delay of implementation of the identified PPAs under the 20% DF.
observations.reason: The tedious process of procurement is also faulted on the delay of implementation of the identified PPAs under the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to enforce full liquidation of cash advances of officers and employees and forward the same to COA for issuance of final demand.
observations.managementAction: Send demand letters to concerned employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PCF custodian to always observe the consistent availability of cash for the immediate assistance to individuals in crisis situations by practicing the replenishment when the disbursements reached at least 75% or as the need arises, of the total PCF set up.
observations.managementAction: The available cash for the PCF AICS is regularly monitored and ensured that AICS could be readily provided with cash assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that to the Local Chief Executive: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the reports submitted by the RCC with the daily remittance and/or Official Receipts of the AF used, the logbook for the released AF to the RCC and the remittances. b. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the report submitted by the Municipal Treasurer with the supporting documents and the reported AF used to ensure that all collections and revenues are completely recorded. c. Require both the Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records at least quarterly to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the Financial Reports.
observations.managementAction: The Liquidating Officer was instructed to validate the remittance to the ADGB every week.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to direct all collecting officers to regularly prepare Report of Collections and Deposits at the close of each business day and remit their collections daily, or when collection reached P5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Remittance to the ADGB was already made every week.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following suggested measures: a. Update the cashbooks regularly, which could be handled and reconciled daily, weekly or monthly whichever is practicable, based on the ROD and RCD. b. Maintain a separate cashbook for payroll fund and for operating expenses in each fund. c. Reconcile the cashbook balances with the accounting records every end of the month or at least quarterly. d. Immediately take up necessary adjustments on the books to reflect the correct cash balances and to reconcile her cashbooks with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The cashbook were updated and reconciled regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer issue an official receipt to taxpayers equal to the amount actually received/collected.
observations.managementAction: The difference of the money value from the AF was issued with an AF 51 to acknowledge receipt of the whole amount of payments received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of additional remuneration/compensation in the absence of legal basis.
observations.managementAction: The payment of additional to personnel was no longer granted, whereas, the designated MA was already designated as full pledged Municipal Agriculturist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: The Bids and Awards Committee to submit the contracts/Purchase Orders, together with the documents listed in COA Circular No. 2009-001, to the Audit Team within 5 days from execution for all types/modes of procurements. Also, require the committee to completely provide the necessary details in the Purchase Order in their succeeding transactions. The GSO or the Municipal Treasurer to promptly notify and furnish copies of delivery documents to the Audit Team on the deliveries made.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations
observations.reason: Lack of man power.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payrolls for salaries and wages should bear the required certification of the authorized officials and duly supported with complete and proper documentation.
We recommended that management secure a copy of the MOA for the aforementioned projects and furnish the same to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The MOA is attached in the FUR during liquidation of the fund transfer.
observations.reason: The copy of the MOA was not provided with the Accountant not Treasurer at time of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. Create an Oversight Committee to: (1) review the current fees and charges imposed by the LGU; (2) compute the appropriation rates for each service rendered by the LGU, and (3) submit to the Municipal Mayor the proposed revisions of the rates and charges. b. Require the appointed Chairperson of the Oversight Committee, to recommend to the SB the immediate approval of the proposed revisions of the LGU’s Municipal Revenue Code; and, c. Require the Municipal Vice-mayor, as the Presiding Officer of the SB to facilitate the immediate issuance of a local revenue ordinance for the adoption of the revised rates on fees and charges of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Revision of the Municipal Revenue Code is already in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and prepare a list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit the same to the Auditor to facilitate monitoring thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Report on Implementation of PPAs was prepared and submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. The MDRRMO to formulate an LDRRMF Plan which should consist of the specific details of the projects, programs and activities to be funded and furnish the same to the MPDC and MBO for incorporation in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget of the municipality; b. The MBO to ensure that charges against the LDRRM Fund are in line with its identified PPAs; and c. The Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF following the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: a. The MDRRMO prepared the plan and report. b. The MBO to ensure that charges against the LDRRM Fund are in line with its identified PPAs; and c. The Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to implement the use of a biometric machine for all the officers and personnel to log their attendance to work in order to ensure the correctness and accuracy of their time attendance.
observations.managementAction: Buy a Bundy clock

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Inventory Committee to expedite the taking and render a report thereon in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006. b. Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records and furnish the inventory committee a copy thereof to assist in the conduct of the physical count of the PPE. c. Require the Property Custodian to maintain records of all the LGU properties and monitor its storage. d. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE owned by the LGU and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor; e. Create an Appraisal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in order to prevent further deterioration and loss of value.
observations.managementAction: Each office was required to list all the supplies and equipment in their office. The reports were submitted to the Accountant for consolidation and validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting details/schedules and to provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements following the format prescribed in COA Circular 2016-004. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Reconciliation of the Surplus/Deficit from ordinary activities with the net cash flow from operating activities and present the same as either part of the Notes to Financial Statements or as an integral part of the Statement of Cash Flows.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations and prepare the necessary schedules. Prepare the reconciliation of surplus deficit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive: Consider the provision on the compensatory time/day-off, in lieu of the overtime pay or non-compensatory remuneration policy for overtime services pursuant to CSC and DBM JC No. 2; Require the HRMO to prepare a detailed and specific plan of activities per Department integrating the volume and frequency of regular and routine work to be performed and the expected output and deadline.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, the following documents and reports should be required on the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption: b. Monthly Report of Official Travels prepared by the driver concerned and Monthly Equipment Utilization Report prepared by the equipment custodian summarizing in chronological order his trips or use of equipment, respectively, for the month, duly certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer; and c. Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted monthly to the auditor for verification purposes. The report shall show the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the agency during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel consumed and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendation
observations.reason: Lack of manpower, the LGU does not have a permanent driver

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to look into the disposal and segregation of wastes in the MRF. It should supervise and consider the conduct of trainings and/or workshops on the proper handling and segregation of wastes to its Solid Waste Personnel.
observations.managementAction: Prepare the 10-year municipal solid waste management plan

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to: Impose the forfeiture of the performance security and/or reimburse from the contractor any amount that will be incurred to correct the defects/damages found within the defect liability period. Observe the period requirement provided in Section 62.2.3.2 in the imposition of the warranty security which is 2 to 15 years after final acceptance depending on the nature of the infrastructure project. Require the BAC to maintain a record and monitor the validity of posted securities so that replacement thereof at times of expiration can be demanded immediately.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action: Require the Property Custodian to maintain records of all the LGU properties and monitor its storage. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE owned by the LGU and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor; Create an Appraisal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in order to prevent further deterioration and loss of value.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct: The Municipal Engineer to create a well-planned detailed engineering and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all aspect of the construction/phases of the project to ensure that the POW shall cover at least a usable portion of the project and to see to it that no construction shall be started for portions of the project that are less than usable; The concerned offices to identify the reasons and address the hindrances in the commencement of the work and start of the construction works/phase for the completion of the project; and The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the Program of Work (POW), plans/drawings and technical specification with detailed breakdown of the cost up to the completion of the project, separate from the POW per phases, so as to appropriately allocate funds and to ensure continuity of the works.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the identified Persons Responsible and Liable in the Notices of Suspension and Notices of Disallowance, respectively to settle the amounts. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant send copy of the Notices to the Persons Responsible/Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Follow audit recommendations and settle the audit suspensions.
observations.reason: Collections not yet evaluated by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to: a. maintain property ledger cards and/or records of the composition of the PPE accounts, excel record to property ledger card. b. identify the recorded accumulated depreciation for each type of PPE, c. allocate depreciation separately on each items or part of the PPE accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustment thereto. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian be required to maintain the Stock Cards and require reconciliation between the records of the Accounting and the Property Custodian which shall be made regularly.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is on the process of transferring the excel record to property ledger. Identified each PPE and allocated their respective depreciation.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Part II, of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting details/schedules and to provide adequate and disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements following the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-004.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Reconciliation of the Surplus/Deficit from ordinary activities with the net cash flow from operating activities and present the same as either part of the Notes to Financial Statements or as an integral part of the Statement of Cash Flows.
observations.reason: PPE was not in accordance with the prescribed form.

observations.managementAction: The LGU has stopped granting overtime services to head of office. Overtime claims are supported with adequate documentation.
observations.reason: Provision of compensatory time/day-off has not yet been considered by the LGU in lieu of the overtime pay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor install control and monitoring mechanisms to ensure that the use of government vehicles and consumption of fuel shall strictly be for a valid public purpose. Henceforth, the following documents and reports should be required on the use of government vehicles and fuel consumption:
a. Serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets are duly accomplished and approved by the Municipal Mayor or his authorized representative to control the use of government vehicle;
b. Monthly Report of Official Travels prepared by the driver concerned and Monthly Equipment Utilization Report prepared by the equipment custodian in chronological order for the month, duly certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer; and
c. Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted monthly to the auditor for verification purposes. The report shall show the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the agency during the month, total distance travelled, total fuel consumed and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle.
observations.managementAction:
a) Serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets are duly accomplished.
b) The LGU did not prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels for CY 2022.
c) Report of Fuel Consumption were not prepared or submitted.
observations.reason: The LGU still did not prepare the monthly report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Impose the forfeiture of the performance security and/or reimburse from the contractor any amount that will be incurred to correct defects/damages found within the defect liability period.
b. Require the posting of the warranty security by the contractors after the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance by the agency be strictly enforced. To ensure that the warranty security is posted, the LGU may make it a requirement to observe the one (1) year period after completion of the project before returning the retention money after which the warranty period shall commence.
c. Observe the period requirement provided in Section 62.2.3.2 in the imposition of the warranty security which is 2 to 15 years after final acceptance depending on the nature of the infrastructure project.
d. Require the BAC to maintain a record and monitor the validity of posted securities so that replacement thereof at times of expiration can be demanded immediately.
observations.managementAction:
a) Not Implemented
b) Implemented
c) Not Implemented
d) Not Implemented
observations.reason: Has yet to impose the forfeiture for defects on property due to lack of personnel in the Engineering office. No reason provided for other not implemented items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a. Instruct the members of MIATF to ensure that environmental surfaces in the evacuation center (quarantine) facility are regularly and thoroughly cleaned, sanitized and maintained before, during and after the quarantine period of the occupants.
b. Require the MPDC to regularly monitor the status/condition of infrastructure projects of the LGU and recommend appropriate actions for its maintenance and/or repairs.
c. Require the Property Custodian to maintain records of all the LGU properties and monitor its storage.
d. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE owned by the LGU and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor;
e. Create an Appraisal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in order to prevent further deterioration and loss of value.
observations.managementAction:
a) Implemented - The quarantine facility is well maintained.
b) Implemented - Created Project Monitoring Team to regularly monitor the status of the projects.
c) Not Implemented - The MTO/Property Custodian still lack the required property records.
d) Not Implemented - The Inventory Committee did not conduct the physical count as at yearend.
e) Not Implemented - The LGU is yet to create an Appraisal Committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a. The Municipal Engineer to create a well-planned detailed engineering and review and evaluate program of works in all aspect of the construction/phases of the project to ensure that the POW shall cover at least a usable portion of the project and to see to it that no construction shall be started for portions of the project that are less than usable;
b. The concerned offices to identify the reasons and address the hindrances in the commencement of the work and start of the construction works/phase for the completion of the project; and
c. The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the Program of Work (POW), plans/drawings and technical specification with detailed breakdown of the cost up to the completion of the project, separate from the POW per phases, so as to appropriately allocate funds and to ensure continuity of the works.
observations.managementAction:
a) Not Implemented - Implementation is underway due to lack of manpower and transition of office.
b) Not Implemented - No reason provided.
c) Not Implemented - Delayed preparation of POW due to lack of adequate manpower.
observations.reason: Implementation is underway due to lack of manpower and transition of office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Regularly prepare a BRS for all the individual bank accounts maintained in the Authorized Government Depository Bank; and
b. Examine the reconciling items and prepare journal entry vouchers for the necessary adjustments in the books of the LGU.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Part II, finding no. 2, of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other local officials to:
a. Re-evaluate and re-assess the programmed priority PPAs embodied under the AIPs, and review if the AIPs are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the Municipality to ensure full utilization of the fund;
b. Maximize the utilization of the fund for CoViD-19-related PPAs to fully address surging issues on health, safety, and well-being of the residents of the Municipality; and
c. Implement immediately all unimplemented PPAs that are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the LGU for the attainment of socio-economic, and environmental management outcomes.
observations.reason: Implementation of the programs under the 20% DF was delayed due to lack of manpower and havoc brought about by the pandemic. CoVID-19 related funds are fully utilized as at yearend.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
a. The officers and employees concerned for the immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances;
b. The withholding of the salaries of the accountable employees until they make just the unliquidated amounts and stop the grant of cash advance; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the employees concerned and forward the same to COA for the issuance of final demand, and initiate the filing of cases pursuant to Section 128 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already started to deduct gradually unsettled amounts.
observations.reason: Several personnel with unsettled cash advances are no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant send copy of the Notices to the Persons Responsible/Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.reason: Substantive amount of NS, Disallowance and Notice of Charge are already not in service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the required submission of contracts to the Audit Team within five (5) days from their issuance or execution, for transactions to be reviewed as to legality and propriety prior to their payment.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Part II, finding no. 14.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a.) Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the status of the dormant
bank account and take appropriate action thereon; and
b.) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statement for all the accounts maintained and
prepare necessary adjusting entries to record all reconciling and
unrecorded items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct:
a) The planning officers such as the Municipal Engineer and Municipal
Planning and Development Officer to ensure that the identified priority
PPAs in its Annual Investment Plan are well planned and procurement-
and-implementation-ready in order to optimally contribute to the
attainment of desirable socio-economic targets of the LGU.
b) The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development
Officer to expedite the implementation of the PPAs and ensure that
physical accomplishments are closely-monitored.
c) MPDO and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the PPAs
identified in its Annual Investment Plan have specific target dates of
implementation, so as to guide the management in monitoring the
status of implementation for each PPA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all properties owned
by the municipality and prepare and submit the RPCPPE in prescribed
format not later than January 31 of each year; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property
Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following
courses of actions:
a. Require the Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to regularly
coordinate and reconcile their records in order to appropriately guide
the LCE in monitoring the utilization and status of the fund;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to make the
necessary adjustments in their utilization reports and records.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required Subsidiary
Ledgers to accurately reflect the unexpended balance of the LDRRM
Funds in the STF; and,
d. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
transfer the unexpended QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the Special
Trust Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at yearend and
make the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to issue demand letters to enforce full liquidation of cash
advances of officers and employees and forward the same to COA for
issuance of final demand. Henceforth, require all officials and employees to
observe strict adherence to the rules governing the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, otherwise, Section 5.1.3 of the rules should be
imposed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PCF custodian to
always observe the consistent availability of cash for the immediate
financial assistance to individuals in crisis situations by practicing the
replenishment when the disbursements reached at least 75% or as the need
arises, of the total PCF set up.

We recommended the following courses of actions to the Local Chief
Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the reports submitted by the
RCC with the Official Receipts used, the logbook for the released AF to
the RCC and conduct the physical count of the AF in the hands of the
collectors at least every turn over and/or end of the month to ensure
that all collections are turned over and completely reported in the RCD.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the report submitted by
the Municipal Treasurer with the supporting documents and the
reported AF used to ensure that all collections and revenues are
completely recorded.
c. Require both the Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile
their records at least quarterly to ensure the accuracy and reliability of
the Financial Reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to direct all collecting officers to regularly prepare Report of
Collections and Deposits at the close of each business day and remit their
collections daily, or when collection reached P5,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that the cashbooks are handled and maintained
accordingly by adopting the following suggested measures:
a. Update the cashbooks regularly, which could be daily, weekly or monthly
whichever is practicable, based on the ROD and RCD.
b. Maintain a separate cashbook for payroll fund and for operating
expenses in each fund.
c. Reconcile the cashbook balances with the accounting records every end
of the month or at least quarterly.
d. Immediately take up necessary adjustments on the books to reflect the
correct cash balances and to reconcile her cashbooks with the accounting
records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to issue an official receipt to taxpayers equal to the amount
actually received/collected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of additional
remuneration/compensation in the absence of legal basis.

We recommended that management prepare the Capacity Development
Agenda and the Local Chief Executive should ensure that seminars and
conventions attended is responsive to the organizational result and
development outcome, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce
financial resources of the LGU. Also, before approving the authority to
attend any seminar-workshop the authority and competencies of the
sponsoring organization/agency and its RPs should be accordingly verified.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee to submit the contracts/Purchase
Orders, together with the documents listed in COA Circular No. 2009-
001, to the Audit Team within 5 days from execution for all types/modes
of procurements. Also, require the committee to completely provide
the necessary details in the Purchase Order in their succeeding
transactions.
b. The GSO or the Municipal Treasurer to promptly notify and furnish
copies of delivery documents to the Audit Team on the deliveries made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payrolls for salaries
and wages should bear the required certification of the authorized officials
and duly supported with complete and proper documentation.

We recommended that management secure a copy of the MOA for the
aforementioned projects and furnish the same to the Audit Team.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:
a. Create an Oversight Committee to: (1) review the current fees and
charges imposed by the LGU; (2) compute the appropriation rates for
each service rendered by the LGU, and (3) submit to the Municipal
Mayor the proposed revisions of the rates and charges.
b. Require the appointed Chairperson of the Oversight Committee, to
recommend to the SB the immediate approval of the proposed revisions
of the LGU’s Municipal Revenue Code; and,
c. Require the Municipal Vice-mayor, as the Presiding Officer of the SB
to facilitate the immediate issuance of a local revenue ordinance for the
adoption of the revised rates on fees and charges of the LGU.

We recommended that management strictly observe the pertinent
provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. A list of all
on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year should
be prepared and submitted to the Auditor to facilitate monitoring thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The MDRRMO to formulate an LDRRMF Plan which should consist
of the specific details of the projects, programs and activities to be
funded and furnish the same to the MPDC and MBO for incorporation
in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget of the municipality;
b. The MBO to ensure that charges against the LDRRM Fund are in line
with its identified PPAs; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare and submit the Report
on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF following the format prescribed
in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to implement the use of
biometric machine to all the officers and personnel to log their attendance
to work in order to ensure the correctness and accuracy of their time
attendance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Sanggunian
to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinances with the assistance
of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women
and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and
integrated in local development plans.

Likewise, we recommend that the GFPS should strictly observe the
timelines in the preparation and submission of the GPB and
Accomplishment Reports to DILG and other oversight agencies, and the
endorsed copies thereof be furnished to the Audit Team.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Accountant to follow the format prescribed and purpose of a Subsidiary Ledger.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers for CY 2022 were presented in prescribed form.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the grant of honoraria to national government employees unless provided with an appropriation and until the municipality has fully implemented the Modified Salary Standardization Schedule and other conditions prescribed in the Salary Standardization Law.
observations.managementAction: Management did not grant honoraria to national government employees in CY 2022.

a) Implemented
b) Implemented
c) Implemented
d) Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned officials responsible in the procurement process ensure that the LGU only transact business with the legitimate suppliers/service providers. Likewise, require the committee to completely provide the necessary details in the Purchase Order in their succeeding transactions.
observations.managementAction: The LGU complied with the procurement process required by RA 9184. Likewise, require the concerned officials to submit the lacking documents mentioned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to look into the disposal and segregation of the wastes in the MRF. It should supervise and consider the conduct of trainings and/or workshops on the proper handling and segregation of wastes to its Solid Waste Personnel. We further recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board devise measures consistent with the provisions of the R.A. 9003 and its IRR to prevent open hazards or risks posed to the health of residents, the garbage collectors and the environment.
observations.managementAction: Imposed proper segregation of waste. Discontinued operation of an open disposal facility. Created Project Monitoring Team to regularly monitor the status of the projects.
observations.reason: No solid waste plan created by previous year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer:
a. Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms; and
b. Ensure coordination between the end-users and the accounting office for the submission of required reports and the proper accounting for the inventories.
observations.managementAction:
a) Implemented - Prescribed inventory forms such as RIS and ICS are duly accomplished.
b) Implemented - The Municipal Accountant and end-users particularly RHU regularly coordinate with the submission of required reports.
Bank statements prepared at year-end an submitted to COA with the bank statements.
observations.reason: Absence of details of the items from previous years' submitted in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide additional information to all accounts in the notes, especially those showing significant increase/decrease.  Likewise, the notes should also indicate the nature, composition or any changes thereto pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS Volume I and Section 111 of PD 1445, to provide readers with the information necessary for the full understanding of the operation of the LGU.
observations.reason: The format of the Notes to FS was suggested by the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and all personnel concerned to attach complete documentations on all disbursements to ensure the validity as well as propriety of the transactions. Significantly, management should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.
observations.reason: No action taken. The Accountant was into understanding that the documents attached in each claim were complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184, COA and GPPB issued JMC No. 1, and COA Circular No. 2017-001 to ensure propriety and validity of the procurements, and that the LGU obtain the most advantageous price in its procurements.
observations.reason: The reimbursements for procurements that are Special were resorted. There was no designated Special Disbursing Officer. The Treasurer can no longer handle additional advances for purposes other than payroll due to the volume of transactions and report preparation in the office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all other concerned to: a. Re-evaluate and reassess the programmed priority PPAs embodied in the AIPs, and review if the AIPs are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the Municipality to ensure full utilization of the fund; b. Maximize utilization of the fund for COVID-19-related PPAs to address surging issues on health, safety, and well-being of the residents; and c. Implement immediately all unimplemented PPAs that are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the LGU for the attainment of socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.
observations.reason: Various implementation realignments of the programs under the 20% DF was delayed based on COVID-19 expenditures. The 20% DF was programmed based on the priorities of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer/General Services Officer, and Engineer to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System, to perform their mandated duties and responsibilities in the submission of reports and maintenance of records, and to coordinate with each other. Submit copies of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE), and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC). Draw Journal Entry Vouchers to correct the errors in the books of the LGU relative to the computation of depreciation.
observations.reason: The inventory taking had not been conducted; therefore no report was rendered thereon to be the basis in the computation of depreciation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the identified Persons Responsible and Liable in the Notices of Suspension and Notices of Disallowance, respectively to settle their audit suspension or disallowance. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant send copy of the Notices to the Persons Responsible/Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The details of the Notices of Suspension and Notices of Disallowance were requested from the audit team.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office has no record on the suspended/disallowed disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer: Record regular purchases of inventories using the perpetual inventory method; Conduct physical count and submit the report twice a year inventories on hand; Prepare the required inventory reports, schedules, supplies ledger cards and stock cards; Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms; Ensure coordination between the end-users and the accounting office for the submission of required reports and the proper accounting for the inventories.
observations.managementAction: The supplies were directly issued to end user and/or were only purchased as the need arises. The Treasurer has responsibility for preparation of the report.
observations.reason: There was no preparation of the required report. The report was overlooked due to volume of transactions and lack of designated Supply Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the remaining balance of the LDRRMF to the trust fund at the end of the year pursuant to DILG and Local Government Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; See to it that estimated collection of the Municipality will not be included in the computation of the 5% LDRRMF; See to it that disbursement out of the LDRRMF is allowable under the governing law; See to it that a copy of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan and other relevant reports be submitted on time to the Auditor; and Submit the utilization report in accordance with the prescribed format and submit it on time to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: LDRRM Fund were utilized for the COVID 19 response and the Notes to FS for LDRRM Fund was revised.
observations.reason: Failure to transfer was addressed; the LDRRM Fund was utilized for COVID-19 response. Notes to FS were revised.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point System to strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanisms of the Municipality’s CBMS and GAD databases through preparation of master plan indicating the timelines, process, budget and other necessary actions.



Thereafter, start the implementation in CY 2021 in order to ensure updated, operational and reliable information provided by said databases which will serve in attaining an evidenced-based planning, policy-making, and identification of beneficiaries for relief distribution and financial assistance in the future.

We have recommended further that if necessary, the Management to augment the number of employees responsible for the maintenance and development of the databases, more specifically to update the data contained therein because data gathering and collating mechanism of the GAD Database and CBMS Database is certainly a tedious work.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to update the GAD database, however, the CBMS was not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the deposit slips and disbursement vouchers of the transactions presented as reconciling items for the current year and to exert effort in tracing the disbursement vouchers pertaining to prior year period.  And thereafter, to reconcile their records and make the necessary adjustments in their records as needed in order to reflect the timely and accurate position of the Municipality.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer trace the details on the long outstanding checks particularly as to its date and its whereabouts, and thereafter, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to make the proper adjustment on these cancelled checks.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was still on the process of reconciling books with the Municipal Treasurer.




Reconciliation was still on-going.





The tracing on the stale checks was still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to further look into previous years’ files in order to account for the said receivables.  If after all the possible means were exerted and the claims could not be accounted, the Management could take necessary actions for the proper write-off/derecognition of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to fairly present the Inter-Agency Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was yet to request for an SB resolution requesting COA to write-off/ derecognize the dormant receivable accounts.

To strictly comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting and liquidation of cash advances.

The cash advances from prior years cannot be settled because the responsible person was already deceased.




The provisions in the Circular were strictly implemented.




The Management was yet to request for an SB Resolution requesting COA to write-off/ derecognize the dormant Cash Advance accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality exercise its authority to update the existing Market and Slaughterhouse Code in conformity with the provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991, otherwise known as RA 7160 in order to generate additional income to finance programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The proposals for additional revenue or increase in Fees and Charges were already submitted to the Committee for proper review. Public hearing was yet to be done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive physical count of all types of inventories to give enough time to prepare the RPCI and submit the same on or before January 31 of the ensuing year as prescribed in Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

3.7 	We recommended that the Management undertake the following measures:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive physical count earlier to give enough time to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same on or before January 31 of the ensuing year as prescribed in Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
Instruct the GSO to prepare and maintain property cards for every type of PPEs for proper monitoring and use this as the basis in the conduct of the physical count instead of the accounting records; and
Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies, goods and other items by debiting the appropriate inventory account using the Perpetual Inventory Method and credit the account upon its issuance pursuant to Sections 51, 97, 103 and 121 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the intra-agency transactions from Due to LGUs to Due to Other Funds account and establish its reciprocal account, Due from Other Funds and recognize the income in the appropriate fund. Also, to make an adjustment in order to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts. Henceforth, ensure that the Due to LGUs account will be used solely for inter-agency transactions that involves other LGUs and to refer to the prescribed chart of accounts for proper treatment of unusual transactions.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to incorporate prioritized PPAs in all four thematic areas of the LDRRMF pursuant to Items 3.0 and 4.0 of NDRRM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 to ensure full attainment of the purpose of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking/additional supporting documents necessary to conduct technical evaluation and inspection of the projects. Also, create a written/encoded monitoring for contracts returned or communicated letters to supervise and lessen the risk of non-compliance.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the Statement of Work Accomplishment that coincide with the actual quantities and actual cost of the project. It is also recommended to present the quantities based on the actual structure using the contract unit cost to evaluate if it will compensate the original project cost;
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit the Variation Order, duly approved by the Local Chief Executive, that covers any increase/decrease in quantities, including the introduction of new work items that are not included in the original contract or reclassification of work items pursuant to the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
Remind those officers concerned to exercise prudence even during the pre-engineering phase to ensure that all important details about the projects have been fully covered and considered to avoid constant revision or change of the plan which would delay the implementation of the project.

We recommended that the Management instruct all personnel concerned to exercise due diligence and prudence in the planning phase of the procurement activities. Ensure that all factors affecting the determination of the ABC are considered especially the market price of the items to be procured in order to come up with a reasonable amount of ABC, thereby preventing the incurrence of excessive costing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Reconstitute an Inventory Committee for the conduct of complete and detailed
physical count of all the Municipality’s properties taking into account the
provisions mandated under Section 124 of MNGAS, COA Circular No. 2020-
006, particularly on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts;
a. Require the Inventory Committee, Office of the Municipal Accountant and the
Designated GSO to work coordinately on the conduct and completion of the
one-time cleansing of PPE accounts and include also the inspection of
transferred infrastructure projects from the DPWH to be led by the Municipal
Engineer;
b. Order the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare Property Ledger Cards
for all PPEs;
c. Require the Office of the Designated GSO to prepare Property Cards and ARE
for all PPEs; and
d. Consider the appropriation of funds in the ensuing year for filling up the
position of the GSO to address the recurring issues on property management
of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan consider the request of the
Office of the Mayor for the creation of positions for the General Services Office.

We strongly reiterate that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• prepare and submit the year-end BRS for all bank accounts within the
prescribed period to the Audit Team for review and verification to establish the
correct balances of the bank accounts of the Municipality with its depository
banks and to detect immediately fraud/irregularities in the use of cash
resources, if any and as mandated under Section 122 of PD No. 1445;
• make a detailed verification and effect the necessary corrections on the
discrepancies in the bank accounts appearing in the books with those of the
Treasury and bank records; and
• adjust immediately all reconciling items, either in the books of the Municipality
or the depository banks, which should be properly notified, to avoid
accumulation thereof.
b. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the
stale checks for replacement, if applicable, and comply with the procedures in
the handling of stale/spoiled checks as stated in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual
Volume III.
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to work rigidly and immediately
determine the cause/s of the difference between their records and reconcile
them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to review all recorded units of motor vehicles and
reclassify to appropriate account (e.g., Construction and Heavy Equipment or
Other Transportation Equipment accounts) if warranted.
b. General Services Officer to:
• locate the supporting documents of the 104 units of vehicles and
accordingly verify their specific description, acquisition costs and owner to
tally records with the Accounting;
• present for inspection to the Audit Team the remaining 57 units listed on
the List of Motor Vehicles;
• secure immediately the documents pertinent to the registration of the
municipal vehicles and process the transfer of name to the Municipal
Government, if applicable;
• exert all efforts for the yearly renewal of registration of all motor vehicles;
• prioritized the process of obtaining the corresponding red plate number for
the motor vehicles of the Municipality to ensure proper handling and
maintenance of the property as required by existing regulations; and
• separately report those items that should be taken up under Construction
and Heavy Equipment accounts from the List of Motor Vehicles.
c. Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to:
• reconcile respective records bearing in mind the proper classification and
right details of the items listed on both records;
• provide complete and definite details regarding each item such as brand,
plate number or other distinguished feature of the vehicles being logged on
each record to facilitate ease of reconciliation; and
• locate documents pertaining to the donated motor vehicles and take up its
fair value on the books of accounts for computation of depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal GSO to
coordinate with the Accounting Office in determining the specific descriptions of
the units listed under the Motor Vehicle account to determine which among the
noted unserviceable vehicles were taken up in the books of accounts of the
Municipality.
Moreso, instruct the Disposal Committee to institute measures to properly
dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the above cited
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. MPDC to judiciously plan and specifically identify each of the target recipient’s
location and estimated cost to be presented in the Annual Investment Program
(AIP);
b. Municipal Budget Officer to submit the Sangguniang Bayan approval for the
implementation of a lump sum appropriation and refrain from including projects
which are not intended for general welfare in the appropriation for 20 percent
Development Fund;
c. Municipal Accountant to verify the existence of the Water Supply System and to
make necessary adjustments if warranted; and
d. Municipal Engineer to submit the accomplishment reports with the distribution
list, copy of identification card or cedula of the recipients if the items were
distributed individually and the pictures of “before”, “during” and “after”
project implementation to validate the propriety of the expenditures, otherwise,
the transactions shall be suspended.
Likewise, the Office of the Municipal Mayor to provide explanation/ justification on
the denial of the barangay officials on their signatures and acknowledgement of
the materials appearing in the Acceptance and Inspection Reports to validate the
propriety and legality of the transactions, otherwise, expenditures incurred shall
be disallowed in audit and appropriate action be taken against erring officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
together with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) or a committee created for
the accreditation of May Bukas na Aagapay (MBA) Livelihood Association Inc. to
submit the required documents as discussed in Nos. 1-4. Otherwise, the MBA
shall not be recognized as NGO/PO and therefore all transactions incurred thereto
shall not be allowed in audit.
Moreso, the Municipal Mayor to provide explanation/justification for approving the
continuous transfers of funds to May Bukas na Aagapay (MBA) Livelihood
Association Inc. in spite absence of proof of the legitimacy of the May Bukas na
Aagapay (MBA) Livelihood Association Inc. Likewise, stop the transfer of funds
until submission and evaluation of all the required documents as provided in the
above-mentioned COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide explanation for the noted
inconsistencies on the funding source and the amount stated in the contract.
Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Agriculturist and the
Municipal Treasurer to gather the copy of contracts or other documents to
establish the correct balance of the receivables and demand the immediate
settlement of the account from Rosario Batangas Barangay Councilors
Association Inc. Upon determination of the correct balance, the Municipal
Accountant make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the Due from Non-
Government Organizations/People's Organizations account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
draw adjusting journal entries to recognize expenses and payables of CY 2021 and
to properly utilize the Prior Period Adjustment account.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Vice Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant together with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
immediately submit the PhilGEPS posting, BAC resolutions and other documents
that will show the procurement process undertaken for this activity.
Likewise, submit the billing statement, program of activity, list of the municipal
employees with their signatures who participated in the activity, list of resource
persons, copy of the certificate of attendance signed by the Municipal Vice Mayor
and pictures of the actual conduct of the training showing the resource person/s
and participants to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that the Office of the Municipal Vice Mayor to submit
justification why the four (4) year-end assessment activities were held ahead of
the signing of the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements
under the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring
of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the financial statements in compliance with the requirements of IPSAS
including the required reconciliation of the actual amounts of income and
expenses per Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA)
with the figures presented in the Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to provide
explanation/justification for the Nos. 1-8 abovementioned
lapses/inconsistencies in the procurement process as well as on the defective
documents;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant together with the BAC to furnish the Audit
Team with proper and complete documents for the above transaction to avoid
suspension/disallowance in audit; and
c. Henceforth, the BAC to ensure adherence with the provisions of 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 and the Municipal Accountant see to it that the
disbursements are properly and completely documented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Engineer to submit written justification on the noted
deficiencies in the implementation of the project;
b. require full completion of the project or demand payment of P3,118,518.83
from Mirasol M. Medina Construction and Trading for the cost of
unaccomplished works totaling to P1,746,121.61 and liquidated damages of
P1,372,397.22; otherwise, concerned officials can be held personally liable for
the said amount for failing to take necessary measures to ensure that the
project is implemented in accordance with the approved plans and
specifications; and
c. instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of
projects in accordance with the approved plans and specifications within the
time frame specified in the contract agreement. Impose regular validation of
work accomplishment and identification of contracts which require appropriate
management actions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant together with the Bids and Awards
Committee to submit to the Audit Team the following documents relative to the
procurement of one (1) unit second-hand bulldozer to avoid suspension in
audit:
• computation of Remaining Useful Value (RUV);
• certification/documentation with regards to the general obsolescence of the
heavy equipment, the availability of spare parts, and the quality, degree and
extent of repair and maintenance requirements;
• copy of BAC Resolution expressly stating the alternative mode of
procurement adopted;
• the copy of documents that show the technical, legal and financial eligibility
of the supplier; and
• the enumerated lacking documents above as required in COA Circular No.
2009-001 and in Paragraph 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;
b. Require the Municipal GSO to provide explanation why these heavy equipment
were not stationed in the motorpool or within the compound of the
Municipality and to put proper markings on government-owned heavy
equipment; and
c. Cause the immediate refund of fuel expenses totaling P621,049.93 for the
heavy equipment used in Barangay San Isidro, Rosario, Batangas.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the concerned personnel to indicate in the PR and PO the complete
specifications of the items being requisitioned and all other necessary
information relative to the procurement of supplies/goods/property, to
facilitate the inspection and the determination of the reasonableness of the
prices and quality of the items procured and delivered;
a. instruct the Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
submit the lacking documents such as distribution lists, BAC Resolutions and
evidence of posting on the PhilGEPS, LGU’s website and conspicuous places
or provide justification for further evaluation of the Audit Team;
b. require the BAC to post on the Municipality’s website, PhilGEPS and
conspicuous places all related procurement documents that required to be
published; and
c. Henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that future
disbursement are supported with complete documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant, General
Services Officer and other concerned municipal officials to:
a. See to it that all payments for gasoline, fuel and lubricants shall be supported
with billing statement, gasoline requisition slips, sales invoices and driver’s
trip tickets properly accomplished and serially numbered;
b. At the end of the month, submit to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report
of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as per COA
Circular No. 77-61;
c. Provide explanation on the difference noted between the dates and number of
liters of gasoline stated on the charge sales invoices and driver’s trip tickets;
d. Investigate and determine person liable on the missing charge sales invoices
paid under check no. 559142 dated January 4, 2022;
e. Give explanation on the erasures noted on the charge sales invoices from Algo
Gas Station;
f. Refund the overpayments made due to disbursements not substantiated with
charge sales invoices and driver’s trip tickets totaling P290,710.35,
P1,549,436.68 loaded to private vehicles and P49,070.66 loaded in excess of
the allowable tank capacity; and
g. Provide documentation that will show the propriety and validity of the
utilization of heavy equipment to unknown/various barangays to avoid
suspension/disallowance in audit of P697,190.62 gasoline expenses.
Also, require the Municipal Engineer to provide explanation for non-compliance
with Section 477 (b) (3) of the Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal
Development Council to:
a. Assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current
needs of the constituents of those unimplemented projects; and
b. Ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 percent Development Fund in the
succeeding periods by including development projects that are well-planned
and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01.
Likewise, require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare Annual
Procurement Plan and include the projects funded by 20 percent DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide explanation/justification for the noted deficiency
on Project No. 1;
b. Implementing offices and Bids and Awards Committee to adopt the
appropriate mode of procurement for infrastructure projects that they are not
eligible to implement using the “by administration”; and
Henceforth, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee abide with the
procedures of pakyaw contracting system embodied in the Annex 1 of Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184.

We strongly recommended that Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to:
a. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant as to the proper balance of the
unspent DRRMF recorded under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account; and
b. include on the LDRRMFIP for current year’s appropriation the PPAs to be
charged to unexpended prior years’ balance retained on the Trust Liability
account to ensure maximum and efficient utilization thereof.
We also recommended that that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to provide justification on the checks totaling P519,522.00 disbursed out of the
General Fund.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the MDRRMO, to include all LDRRM funds in the Report on the
Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to fairly
present information to the end users.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
record under the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account the succeeding DRRM funds to
be received from National Government and other sources as mandated by the
above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit to the Audit Team the missing DVs. Otherwise, transactions
shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a suitable size stockroom with
installed racks that will be enough for ten years mandatory document custody and
safeguarding of the Municipality’s accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and
other concerned officials to provide the Audit Team at the beginning of the year
the list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year
with the required details. Submit also to the Audit Team information and evidence
that appropriate signboards and/or public notices were already posted ten days
after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of PPA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer
(GSO) and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to furnish COA copy of the
Purchase Orders not later than five (5) working days from issuance together with
duly accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation and
