to provide notification of deliveries supported with copies of accomplished
Inspection and Acceptance Reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee
composed of adequate number of members to be able to complete the physical
inventory in three months or less, and who shall be temporarily relieved of all their
regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory
taking until the same is completed, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020. After which, it is recommended that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:
a) Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and accordingly
conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and
prepare the corresponding RPCPPE following the guidelines set forth in said
COA Circular; and
b) General Services Officer to update the GSO’s property records based on the
RPCPPE and eventually, together with the Municipal Accountant, performed the
reconciliation of PPE balances between the RPCPPE and General Ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to him immediately the complete
Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2021,
copy furnished the Audit Team;
b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to correspondingly make necessary
adjustment in the books to correct the recorded RPT and SET Receivable; and
c) henceforth, ensure the preparation and maintenance of complete Certified List
of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies to support the balances of the RPT and
SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Office to:
a) prepare the RPCI in the prescribed format under Appendix 48 of MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume II, specifically showing the balances of the inventory items per
stock cards and the resulting variance when compared with the actual count;
and
b) conduct investigation if there are shortages/overages noted in the RPCI and
prepare pertinent report thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
complete the necessary documents and comply with the guidelines prescribed
under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to facilitate the submission to the COA of the
request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts amounting to
P14,660,476.54.
Henceforward, conduct a regular review of all account balances so that dormant
accounts can immediately be identified and acted upon and ensure that all amounts
presented in the financial statements were supported with subsidiary records and
other accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
immediately review all outstanding payables aged over two years and consider
reverting them to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare and submit to him an action plan that will outline and discuss in details the
legal measures and strategies the Municipal Treasurer’s Office will adopt or
undertake to improve the collection efficiency of the Municipality, especially on
delinquent taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that DVs are duly numbered and properly prepared
with complete details in accordance with the prescribed format.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
facilitate a thorough review and verification of records pertaining to outstanding
balance of the Due to GSIS from prior years totaling P591,200.92 so that the balance
could be remitted to the GSIS or adjusted in the books of accounts, if necessary or
applicable. Thereafter, remit all insurance premiums and loan payments withheld to
the GSIS within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain property ledger cards and/or records of the
detailed composition of the PPE accounts, identify the recorded accumulated
depreciation for each type of PPE, allocate depreciation
separately on each items or part of the PPE accounts and effect the
necessary corrections/adjustment thereto.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian
be required to maintain the Stock Cards and require reconciliation between
the records of the Accounting and the Property Custodian which shall be
made regularly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

d. Inventory committee to expedite the conduct of inventory taking and
render a report thereon in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006.

e. Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records
and furnish the inventory committee a copy thereof to assist the
committee in the conduct of the physical count of the PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the supporting details/schedules and to provide
adequate and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements
following the format prescribed in COA Circular 2016-004.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
Reconciliation of the Surplus/Deficit from ordinary activities with the net
cash flow from operating activities and present the same as either part of
the Notes to Financial Statements or as an integral part of the Statement of
Cash Flows.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal
Accountant to follow the format prescribed and purpose of a Subsidiary
Ledger.

We recommended that management stop the grant of honoraria to national
government employees unless provided with an appropriation and until the
municipality has fully implemented the Modified Salary Standardization
Schedule and other conditions prescribed in the Salary Standardization
Law.

This audit recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

a. In issuing the authority to render overtime services, ensure that the
expected output, date of completion/submission of the report, and the
authorized compensation is specifically provided;

b. Consider the provision on the compensatory time/day-off, in lieu of the
overtime pay or non-compensatory remuneration policy for overtime
services pursuant to CSC and DBM JC No. 2;

c. Do not authorize employees holding a position higher than a Division
Chief or with Salary 24 and higher to render overtime services with
compensation;

d. Require the concerned officials to submit the DTR and a detailed
accomplishment during the regular work hours, and accomplishment
report and overtime work plan in all the overtime compensation
claimed; and

e. Require the HRMO to prepare a detailed and specific plan of activities
per Department integrating the volume and frequency of regular and
routine work to be performed and the expected output and deadline.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

a. Designate a Special Disbursing Officer to ensure the availability of cash
for the immediate release of AICS.

b. Instruct the MSWDO to prioritize the preparation of the assessment
report and immediately submit to the concerned officers to facilitate
the outright release of the AICS.

c. Coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office
in formulating the implementing guidelines to regulate the financial aid
or assistance granted to various individuals in crisis situation aligned
with the DSWD-Memorandum Circular No. 11 S of 2019.

d. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all AICS are supported with
complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
officials responsible in the procurement process to ensure that the LGU only
transact business with the legitimate suppliers/service providers. Likewise,
require the officials concerned to submit the lacking documents mentioned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor install control and monitoring
mechanisms to ensure that the use of government vehicles and consumption
of fuel shall strictly be for a valid public purpose. Henceforth, the following
documents and reports should be required on the use of government
vehicles and fuel consumption:

a. Serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets approved by the Municipal
Mayor or his authorized representative to control the use of government
vehicle;

b. Monthly Report of Official Travels prepared by the driver concerned
and Monthly Equipment Utilization Report prepared by the equipment
custodian summarizing in chronological order his trips or use of
equipment, respectively, for the month, duly certified by the driver and
approved by the Administrative Officer; and

c. Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted monthly
to the auditor for verification purposes. The report shall show the
different types of motor vehicles utilized by the agency during the
month, total distance travelled, total fuel consumed and the normal fuel
consumption for each vehicle.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to look
into the disposal and segregation of wastes in the MRF. It should supervise
and consider the conduct of trainings and/or workshops on the proper
handling and segregation of wastes to its Solid Waste Personnel. We further
recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board devise measures
consistent with the provisions of the R.A. 9003 and its IRR to prevent
hazards or risks posed to the health of residents, the garbage collectors and
the environment.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:

a. Impose the forfeiture of the performance security and/or reimburse
from the contractor any amount that will be incurred to correct the
defects/damages found within the defect liability period;

b. Require the posting of the warranty security by the contractors after
the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance by the agency be
strictly enforced. To ensure that the warranty security is posted, the
LGU may make it a requirement to observe the one (1) year period
after completion of the project before returning the retention money
after which the warranty period shall commence;

c. Observe the period requirement provided in Section 62.2.3.2 in the
imposition of the warranty security which is 2 to 15 years after final
acceptance depending on the nature of the infrastructure project; and

d. Require the BAC to maintain a record and monitor the validity of
posted securities so that replacement thereof at times of expiration can
be demanded immediately.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:

a. Instruct the members of MIATF to ensure that environmental surfaces
in the evacuation center (quarantine) facility are regularly and
thoroughly cleaned, sanitized and maintained before, during and after
the quarantine period of the occupants.

b. Require the MPDC to regularly monitor the status/condition of the
infrastructure projects of the LGU and recommend appropriate actions
for its maintenance and/or repairs.

c. Require the Property Custodian to maintain records of all the LGU
properties and monitor its storage.

d. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the
PPE owned by the LGU and prepare a report thereon to be submitted
to the Office of the Auditor;

e. Create an Appraisal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of
the unserviceable properties in order to prevent further deterioration
and loss of value.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:

d. The Municipal Engineer to create a well-planned detailed engineering
and adequately review and evaluate program of works in all aspect of
the construction/phases of the project to ensure that the POW shall
cover at least a usable portion of the project and to see to it that no
construction shall be started for portions of the project that are less than
usable;

e. The concerned offices to identify the reasons and address the hindrances
in the commencement of the work and start of the construction
works/phase for the completion of the project; and

f. The Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit the Program of Work
(POW), plans/drawings and technical specification with detailed
breakdown of the cost up to the completion of the project, separate from
the POW per phases, so as to appropriately allocate funds and to ensure
continuity of the works.

We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Unit and the Property Custodian to conduct regular reconciliation of PPE per books and per count and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective records. Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the PPE accounts under the Trust Fund to the General Fund and provide the corresponding depreciation including those recorded under the SEF.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled and effected adjustment
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of records and non-completion of the physical count of PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management to:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the undistributed rice to the related inventory account. Henceforth, record the purchases of all goods and materials, whether for stock or for distribution, under the inventory account, regardless of its charges pursuant to Section 188 and 134 of the GAM, Volume I and recognize the expense upon receipt of the RSMI duly supported with RIS and other necessary supporting documents; and

b.	Require the Supply Officer and the MSWDO to trace the RDS for the unaccounted goods to support the reported distribution to provide valid basis on the regularity of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: Recorded purchases of goods and materials in the inventory account
observations.reason: Some purchases were still directly debited to expense or Due to NGAs accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management observe the provisions set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-09 dated April 21, 2020 regarding the reporting and accounting guidelines on all donations received and to require the officer concerned to prepare and submit to the NDRRMC, through the OCD the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for all donations received and include thereto the 53 sacks of rice received for PWDs. Instruct the MSWDO to trace the distribution documents of these goods, otherwise the persons concerned shall be held accountable thereof.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required documents to NDRRMC through OCD

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the requirements prescribed in the existing rules and regulations, especially on the creation of special accounts for specific fund receipts to facilitate easy monitoring on the use of the fund and to provide the users of the financial statement of detailed report.
observations.managementAction: Created a Special Account in the GF for the BGCM Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the BAC to secure the lacking supporting documents from the suppliers concerned and submit the same to the Auditor for further evaluation. Henceforth, emergency purchases of the LGU should be supported with the basic documents prescribed under GPPB Circular 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: Emergency procurement were supported with the required documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MEE Manager to submit to the Accounting Office the complete details of the uncollected water billings as of year-end to provide the Accountant with data as basis in recording the income and its corresponding receivables. Also, constantly reminds the consumers of the penal clause under the Municipal Ordinance 17, s. 2018 to promote prompt payment of water bills.
observations.managementAction: Recorded the revenue from uncollected billings.
observations.reason: Imposition of the penal clause still on-going.

observations.managementAction: Conducted investigation which first resulted to suspension of work for 60 days which further led to the accountable officer’s dismissal in government service. Also, demand for restitution of the under remittance was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management stop the inclusion of lump-sum appropriation in the AIP, instead, require the submission of a detailed and specific name of the project with its costing duly supported with program of works. See to it that the development projects being proposed under the 20% DF partakes the nature of capital investment in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in JMC No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer continue to monitor the implementation of all on-going development projects to ensure its completion as planned in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.
observations.managementAction: No lump-sum appropriation in 2021 Annual Budget under the 20% Development Fund

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Health Officer to facilitate the certification requirements by the PhilHealth so that it could avail of the benefits package for its patients, which in turn, would help the Municipality defraying expenses related thereto, and provide better services for its patients and a more conducive working environment to its isolation facility personnel.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record the infrastructure in their books first in order to reflect in the financial records the transactions made and properly reflect them in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to enact a Resolution authorizing the transfer of the Multi-Purpose Halls and Day Care Centers to the different recipient barangays and thereafter, prepare Deeds of Donation for the actual transfer and acceptance by the latter to substantiate transfer of ownership of the said properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to further look into previous years’ files in order to account for the said receivables.  If after all the possible means were exerted and the claims could not be accounted, the Management could take necessary actions for the proper write-off/derecognition of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to fairly present the Receivable accounts; and
Municipal Treasurer to follow-up the payment/liquidation of two NGOs/PO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
reconcile with the records of the Municipal Budget Officer so that the information shown in the Financial Statements will be in conformity with each other;
revert the P33,938.74 to General Fund so that it will be used according to plans and appropriations, and if necessary, trace other Capital Outlay savings or continuing appropriations that were transferred to Trust Liability; and
maintain subsidiary ledger for the Special Trust Fund to facilitate monitoring of transactions under the said fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Inventory Committee to:  i) complete the annual conduct the inventory taking of properties of the Municipality, ii) prepare the RPCPPE by type of PPE, and iii) submit thereafter to the Audit Team within the prescribed period the said report;
the GSO and Municipal Accountant make collaborative procedures in order to sort any record discrepancy/ies, and to update their respective records as well; and
the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties to free up storage spaces, prevent further deterioration of PPE and generate additional income from sale; and
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the availment of the one-time cleansing as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concern heads of departments/units above to submit justification on why the meals and snacks served during their regular conduct of their duties were charged against the Municipal Fund instead of their Representation Allowances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
strictly account transactions with the appropriate account title as recommended in the revised chart of accounts for LGUs as provided in COA Circular No. 2013-002 dated January 30, 2013; and
prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the aforementioned misstatements in the financial reports debiting the Prior Period Adjustments and Representation Expense both by P2,189,991.26; and
Municipal Treasurer to submit the report of paid transactions to the Municipal Accountant within reasonable time in order to timely reflect those in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer:
maintain list of contracts paid to all contractors of the Municipality as basis to collect tax due on the ensuing year;
collect the business taxes assessed on three contractors with corresponding interest, surcharges and penalties as stated on the Revenue Code; and
ensure to strictly apply the provisions of the 2014 Revenue Code of the Municipality in order to increase the revenues of the Municipality.

a) the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office (GSO) to exert more effort to reconcile the difference between the accounting records of PPE and RPCPPE;
b) the General Services Office to provide the details of Land amounting to P4,829,584.72 taken up in the RPCPPE in lump sum; and
c) the Municipal Accountant to verify the accuracy of the computed depreciation expense, and recorded the same in the books and submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Audit Team.
Henceforth, ensure to recognize depreciation of all PPEs on a regular basis in accordance with IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and General Services Office (GSO) has yet completed the reconciliation of the difference between the accounting records of PPE and RPCPPE. The GSO has reconciled P21,313,678.34 of the P40,023,265.68 unreconciled discrepancies Ongoing checking and reconciliation. The GSO is yet to provide details of Land taken up in the RPCPPE lump sum. Reiterated in audit observation no. 1, page 35 of Part II. Verified the accuracy of the computed depreciation expense, and recorded the same in the books and submit the JEV to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Accounting and General Services Office has yet completed the reconciliation of the P40,023,265.68 unreconciled discrepancies; ongoing checking and reconciliation; GSO is yet to provide details of Land taken up in the RPCPPE lump sum.

observations.managementAction: The Management reminded the concerned Municipal officials and personnel to immediately settle immediately their outstanding cash advances with the reminder that under existing laws and regulations, salaries of personnel who unjustifiably fail to settle their cash advances may be withheld accordingly; Demand letters has been sent to the concerned employees for immediate settlement of their accounts. The Management reminded all Municipal officials and employees on the timely liquidation of cash advances. The liquidations for CYs 2010 and 2011 Intelligence Fund Cash Advances were record in the books.
observations.reason: Of the outstanding cash advances, only remaining unliquidated cash advances amounting to P42,040.00.

observations.recommendation:
a) the General Services Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile their respective inventory records and submit the corresponding report, including the adjusting entries made, if any, to the COA Audit Team for further evaluation; and
b) the GSO to submit to the Accounting Office the SSMI duly supported with RIS of the distribution list of the 500 sets of hygiene kits amounting to P538,000.00 to facilitate the adjustment of the Other Supplies and Materials Held for Distribution account in the books.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the report on the reconciliation of inventory records to the Audit Team. Submitted the RSMI duly supported with the RIS of the 500 sets of hygiene kits to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit all unsubmitted CY 2020 DVs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification and post audit, otherwise Notice of Suspensions will be issued. Henceforth, ensure the submission of all reports and accounts within 10 days after the end of the month in accordance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Submitted all unsubmitted CY 2020 DVs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team.

Posting of Notices of Awards, Contracts or Purchase Orders were posted in the Municipality's website. Concerned Municipal officials/personnel submitted the summary of actual receipts and distributions of repacked goods and number of recipients to the Audit Team. Complied with the guidelines of COA Circular No. 2014-002.

observations.recommendation:
a) the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the immediate submission to the Audit Team of the lacking documents such as Income Tax Returns of suppliers, proof of posting of Notices of Awards, among others, and the lists of recipients of food packs from the three barangays, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions; and
b) the concerned Municipal personnel/officials in charge of the receipts and distributions of food packs to submit the lists of recipients from Barangay Tejeros Convention.
observations.managementAction: Concerned personnel/officials submitted the lacking documents and list of recipients immediately. Concerned personnel/officials in charge of the receipts and distributions of food packs submitted the lists of recipients from Barangay Tejeros Convention.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor enjoin the LDRRM Council to identify and incorporate under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP the projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of CYs 2017 and 2018 and prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The projects and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of CYs 2017 and 2018 were identified and incorporated in a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor ensure that the total appropriation for GAD have corresponding PPAs in the GPB and adhere to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. Direct the GAD Focal Point System to ensure that the total appropriation for GAD in the annual budget have corresponding PPAs in the GPB and to strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01, particularly on what expenses could be charged to the GAD Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management ensured that the total appropriation for GAD have corresponding PPAs and adhered to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. The Medical personnel expenses charged to GAD were reviewed and compliance ensured.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the remaining balance of the BGCM fund in the Trust Liabilities account as a Special Trust Account in the General Fund as of December 31, 2020, and correct the Due to NGAs account in order that these accounts will be fairly presented in the Statement of Financial Position.
We likewise recommended that the said grant be adequately disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The Bayanihan Grant was already fully utilized and liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the personnel concerned, especially the Municipal Accountant, to secure complete documentations on all disbursements, to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions. We also recommended for the submission of the lacking supporting documents for the aforementioned transactions.
observations.reason: The required documents were submitted to the audit team. Further, the LGU no longer received Bayanihan Grant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the personnel concerned, especially the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, the submission of PAR for all government property to facilitate the determination of the persons accountable thereof in case of its loss.
observations.managementAction: PAR was prepared and filed in the Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, and the Municipal DRRM Officer to submit the same through the LDRRMC and LDC to the auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Likewise, reflect in the report the cash assistance received from national government, other LGUs, and other sources; and require the Municipal Accountant to make sure that complete documentations are attached on all disbursements to ensure the propriety and validity of the transactions.
observations.reason: LDRRMF Utilization Report was prepared by the Accountant. The LDRRM Fund was allocated and utilized for the COVID-19 response.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities. Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.
observations.reason: The warranty securities were required in the final payment and attached to the disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the LGU officials concerned to comply with the policies and guidelines on the grant of overtime pay to LGU officials and employees. Consider minimization of overtime services and ensure only those that are necessary are paid; and ensure compliance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular.
observations.reason: Overtime services were minimized to only those that are necessary. Lack of personnel and designation of employees to numerous functions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer: a. Review the taxes withheld and remittances to ascertain the correct amount of taxes, and immediately remit the unremitted balance; and b. Strictly adhere to the provisions of BIR Revenue Regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes.
observations.reason: Taxes withheld were remitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct: a. The officers and employees concerned to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances; b. The Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the employees concerned and forward the same to COA for the issuance of final demand, and initiate the filing of cases pursuant to Section 128 of PD 1445; and c. The Municipal Accountant to stop the grant of additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance given to the officers and employees is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, and issue a certification that the employee has no outstanding cash advance for every cash advance granted.
observations.managementAction: The officers and employees only assumed the office in CY 2017. The Municipal Accountant stopped the grant of additional cash advances in CY 2017.
observations.reason: No record of the unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) provided by the Municipal Assessor. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to establish the correct amount of receivables and draw adjusting entries to correct the balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: RPT and SEF Receivable were set up based on the RPTAR provided by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts of funds and record necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement. We also recommended that he shall regularly monitor the transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission, and settle immediately the intra-agency transactions to ensure regular updates and to avoid erroneous account balances.
observations.reason: Reciprocal accounts were reconciled and reflected in the Combined Financial Statements as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant-Designate, Municipal Treasurer, and all responsible officials to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, together with its supporting documents, and cancelled checks. Ensure that all disbursement vouchers, pertinent supporting documents and copies of the checks be submitted on a regular and timely basis. This is without prejudice to any notice of suspension, charges or disallowances that may be issued as a result of our audit.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Vouchers were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to observe the due dates of the remittances of premium and loan amortization to Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS.
observations.reason: The amounts withheld for mandatory contribution and loan repayments of employees were promptly remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and all other concerned shall ensure that all PPAs to be funded by the 20% DF are eligible and implemented during the budget year to attain optimum utilization of the fund. We also recommended that all concerned officials, who are responsible for the preparation of the Municipal Budget shall carefully observe the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to prevent inclusion of ineligible PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The 20% DF was programmed based on the priorities of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all the personnel concerned, especially the Municipal Accountant, to attach complete documentations on claims, to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions. Henceforth shall not process transactions, which are deficient in supporting documents.
observations.reason: The documents required were submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 on prioritization, and training of personnel responsible for planning and implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that they are aligned with GAD-related undertakings, and emerging and/or continuing gender issues and concerns, so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a local GAD code or ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.reason: Various GAD related programs were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council procure the basic equipage prescribed by DILG to ensure that the municipality is prepared for calamities as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.reason: DRRM Equipment were purchased in consideration of the needs and meager funds of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, which is chaired by the Local Chief Executive, should annually update the LGU’s MSWMP. We also recommended that management should strictly observe the provisions of RA 9003, especially in complying with the minimum standards and requirements for the collection of solid waste, and the establishment of a Sanitary Landfill.
observations.managementAction: MSW Plan were prepared and submitted to the concerned oversight agency for approval. An MRF was also constructed.

observations.recommendation: To address the issues, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other local officials concerned to take necessary steps to implement the projects funded out of the fund transfer; and that the Municipal Mayor and other local officials concerned to take necessary steps to implement the projects funded out of the fund transfer; ensure that contracts are submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from their issuance or execution, for transactions to be reviewed as to legality and propriety prior to their payment. Moreover, continued refusal to adhere as required shall subject concerned officers to the legal consequences provided for under the rule in case of non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: Contracts and documents were submitted to the audit team. The contracts and documents were attached to the payment.
observations.reason: The volume of transactions in the BAC.

observations.recommendation: To address the issues, we recommended that the LCE appoint a DRRM Officer who shall work full time. In addition, ensure implementation of all approved PPAs under the DRRMF.

We recommended that the LCE and other local officials concerned to take necessary steps to implement the projects subject in the fund transfer; and to initiate the return of the funds received to the source agency instead of being dormant in the bank.
observations.reason: Implementation of the project funded out of the funds were observed and any unused funds were returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to immediately fill out the vacancy on the mandatory position for LDRRMO of the LGU pursuant to the provision of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 so that disaster risk reduction management will be addressed in times of natural calamities.
observations.reason: LDRRM Officer was designated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with the supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Section 122 of PD 1445 in relation to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two (2) months.
observations.managementAction: Complied. The FS for CY 2021 was submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management properly observe the requirements under COA Circular No. 92-389 in order to maintain a sound internal control in the utilization of government funds. The perforation of these documents shall not be limited only to the Disbursement Voucher but should include all supporting documents to avoid their reuse. A stamp shall be made which shall provide a space for the number of the check issued and date of actual payment and said spaces should be properly filled up.
observations.managementAction: Complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to insure all insurable properties of the LGU with the GSIS in compliance with Republic Act No.656 and Presidential Decree 245 as mandated, and to include the funding requirement of the same in its annual budget.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the    MSWDO    to:         i) coordinate with the MDRRM Office to revisit and revise the exiting plan for the distribution of relief goods to better uphold the necessary promptness and uniformity by convening as often as possible to update the plans according to   current  events;  and  ii) ensure documentation for the release of relief goods to the various Barangay Officials, and see to it that the data contained therein may be used for control purposes.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWDO was coordinating with MPDC for the status of implementation of CBMS. Also, the revision of plan in relief distribution was still on-going.





The documents were prepared for the release of relief goods.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Accountant to:

appropriately recognize the transactions ensuring consistency and accuracy of recording among the journals, ledgers and subsidiary ledgers;

make necessary correcting entries as follows:

Dr  Accounts Payable   P704,572.66
Dr  Due to BIR                686,350.26
Cr       Expenses/Assets    P1,390,922.92 

To record correcting entries on misstatements noted in AP account.

properly recognize Accounts Payable through gross amounts of the corresponding Assets/Expenses incurred;

submit the documents supporting the existence of recognized AP;


furnish copy of AP to the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate monitoring of payment thereof; and

internal control process of settlement of AP and to furnish report as a result hereto which may include provision of additional manpower, among others.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant had recognized the transaction. 



The Municipal Accountant had already made the necessary correcting entries.




The recording of the Accounts Payable was based on gross amounts.



The documents were submitted.



The Municipal Accountant had furnished a copy of AP to the Municipal Treasurer.

The internal control process of settlement was established.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Engineer to furnish copy of Government Report on Programs/Projects/ Activities to Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate;



the Municipal Accountant to:  i) maintain copies of Memoranda of Agreement between the LGU and the National Government Agencies; ii) record completed and on-going projects to the year it occurred regardless if paid or not; 


iii) maintain completed records of the PPEs; and 


iv) reconcile the same with the property cards of the GSO-Designate; and


the GSO-Designate to:  i) complete her property records; and


 ii) conduct annual physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality as basis in the preparation of RPCPPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer had already furnished the Municipal Accountant and GSO designate, a copy of the GPPA.




The copies of MOA were maintained.




The Municipal Accountant was able to record completed and on-going projects.



The Municipal Accountant was able to maintain records of PPEs.

The reconciliation was still on-going.




The property records were not yet complete.




The physical inventory of properties was not completed.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:  i) trace the remaining discrepancy of P294,000.00 between the balances shown in the summary and the table provided, and effect the necessary adjustments in the reports, or even in the Financial Statements, if necessary;


ii) reconcile records with the Municipal Budget Officer so that the information shown in the Financial Statements will be in conformity with each other; and iii) revert the P33,938.74 to General Fund so that it will be used according to plans and appropriations, and if necessary, trace other Capital Outlay savings or continuing appropriations that were transferred to Trust Liability.
observations.managementAction:
The discrepancy was already identified.




The records were not yet reconciled.




The Municipal Accountant had already reverted the amount to General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection on completed projects after the one-year defects liability period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if there are no defects detected or after such were repaired or corrected.

Further, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to oblige the contractor to post warrant security that will address the structural defects that may be discovered form the final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2.

Lastly, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Audit Team copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed contracts to monitor the posting of the said warranty security by the contractors.
observations.managementAction:
The Inspectorate Team had issued the Certificates of Final Acceptance.




The Bids and Awards already required the contractors to post warrant security.





The Municipal Engineer submitted the Certificate of Final Acceptance to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to:

scrutinize the sufficiency of the Contractor’s manpower on all awarded projects especially those that have overlapping implementation period with each other under same contractor and direct the contractors to address the deficiencies noted on manpower utilization;

conduct post-qualification by verifying, validating, and ascertaining all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid and ensure that the Post-Qualification  Report contains in-depth results on the verification of technical requirements; and

determine the applicable liquidated damages on the projects that have been delayed without the corresponding requests for extensions. deficiency.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Secretariat and TWG had scrutinized submitted documents from contractors specifically on the Manpower Utilization.




The Bids and Awards Committee had conducted post qualification within La Union and thru phone calls and emails for bidders outside La Union.




The Municipal Engineer was monitoring the implementation of the projects and impose LD on delayed projects without any request for extension or suspension of work.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to observe the proper procurement process applicable to the provision of financial assistance to the needy in attempt to enter into an agreement with the funeral service provider prospectively, so that remedies and benefits may be availed of in favor of the Government, given that the Covid 19 situation is an on-going concern of the country.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to come up with a certain and clear-cut set of criteria in providing financial assistances to the needy, especially now that the country is economic shambles, and every public fund should be accounted for and should be allocated effectively.
observations.managementAction:
The Bids and Awards Committee had observed the proper procurement process. 






A written policy and guidelines for financial assistance was yet to be prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to review/re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures on AICS Program, and adopt a written policy on the determination as to the appropriateness, procedure and payment of financial assistance to individuals/families in crisis situation, considering the nature, eligibility of beneficiaries, amount of financial assistance and such other measures that would facilitate the effective and efficient implementation of the AICS Program of the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all financial assistance under AICS will undergo the process of assessment by the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office personnel.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer had reviewed and re-evaluated the existing policies and procedures.

Coordinate with the Municipal Mayor with the written policy with regards to the guidelines in giving assistance to the constituents.




The financial assistance was assessed by the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits and properly acknowledge remittances received;


the Collectors to fill-up the Sections B and C in the Report of Collections and Deposits; and



the Municipal Accountant to:            i) complete the data requirements in the Journals and Journal Entry Vouchers and to certify correct them; and


ii) use the General Journal and the Cash Receipts Journal for the purpose these were created.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer and Liquidating Officer had prepared the Report of Collections and Deposit.




The collectors were already instructed to properly accomplish the RCD (Sections B & C) and the Liquidating Officer to prepare the Consolidated RCD.

The Municipal Accountant was yet to provide details in the JEV of collection daily for easy verification of data.



The journals were utilized for their purposes.

The creation of Municipal Building Taskforce thru an Executive Order with the cooperation of barangay officials.




Occupancy permits were required before occupying the structures constructed.


The Building Official had submitted newly issued building permits and occupancy permits to the Municipal Assessor.




The Barangay Officials was enjoined in reporting the construction and completed structures.





A request for additional Job Order or Contract of Service and Motor vehicle for easy monitoring of newly constructed buildings in every barangay was made.




The Municipal Assessor was yet to verify the real properties.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management establish cut-off date as part of their annual closing activity to ensure that all transactions are properly recognized in the correct period.
observations.managementAction: The Management ensured that all the Department Heads have knowledge on the cut-off period such that all their obligations will be properly accounted and recorded to be properly reflected in the Financial Statements at year end.

The ledger of Accounts Payable was submitted.




Adjustments were already made.
Reconciliation of both records were still on going.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving old records hindered the immediate reconciliation of both records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the General Services Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile their respective inventory records and submit the corresponding report, including the adjusting entries made, if any, to the COA Audit Team for further evaluation; and
b. the GSO to submit to the Accounting Office the SSMI duly supported with RIS and complete distribution list of the 500 sets of hygiene kits amounting to P538,000.00 to facilitate the adjustment of the Other Supplies and Materials Held for Distribution account in the books.
observations.reason: The no action was undertaken by the GSO to prepare and submit the complete RIS and SSMI of the Inventory Held for Distribution amounting P538,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Immediately submit the correct lists or schedule of Accounts Payable and documentary requirements to support the Accounts Payable of the Municipality



(and thereafter) Submit the contracts within the prescribed period; and



Make the necessary adjustments to the Financial Statements so that the correct balance of the Accounts Payable and the related asset and/or expenses accounts will be reflected.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted the Schedule of Accounts Payable. However, documentary requirements will only be attached to disbursement vouchers upon payment.

The Management will try its best to comply on the submission of contracts within the prescribed period.



The Management effected necessary adjustments for 2019 transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

•	Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the subsidiary ledgers supplied with complete data as prescribed by pertinent provisions of MNGAS so that relevant information may be provided to internal and external users;




•	Direct the Municipal Treasurer or the GSO, as the case may be, to prepare the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 57 of MNGAS for LGUs and its relevant annexes, so that minimum data required will be supplied, and so that the functions and purposes of the RPCPPE will be attained; and

•	Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the specific items of their records that were not yet reconciled on the initial reconciliation.
observations.managementAction:
The subsidiary ledgers are currently being updated by the accounting unit.





The  GSO-Designate will comply on the said report. 





The Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate are still reconciling their records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to further look into previous years’ files in order to account for the said receivables. If after all the possible means were exerted and the claims could not be accounted, the Management could take necessary actions for the proper write-off/derecognition of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to fairly present the Inter-Agency Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has requested for a Sangguniang Bayan resolution for the write-off of dormant accounts receivables.

The noted cash advances have been settled since.





The Municipality has strictly complied with the COA Circular.






The guidelines set-forth by COA has been considered, and actions were enforced to verify the dormancy of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in the preparation and maintenance of the accounting records, provide the minimum data prescribed by the MNGAS so that these may be processed into relevant information by end-users.
observations.managementAction: The preparation of the accounting records is given more attention and time by Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality exercise its authority to update the existing Market and Slaughterhouse Code in conformity with the provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991, otherwise known as RA 7160 in order to generate additional income to finance programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The system for updating of the Codes are being subjected to rigorous planning so that updates will meet the objectives.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer/General Services Officer, and Engineer to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System, to perform their mandated duties and responsibilities in the submission of reports and maintenance of records, and to coordinate with each other.

Submit copies of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE), and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC)

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Vouchers to correct the errors in the books of the LGU relative to the computation of depreciation.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant overlooked to prepare the Adjusting Journal Entry.

No Physical Inventory was conducted by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts of different funds and record the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement. 

We also recommended that he shall regularly monitor the transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission, and settle immediately the intra-agency transactions to ensure regular updates and to avoid erroneous account balances.
observations.reason: No Subsidiary Ledgers for the prior years’ balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require all the personnel concerned, especially the municipal accountant, to attach complete documentations on all disbursements, to ensure the validity as well as the correctness of the transactions.  Significantly, management should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and officials concerned did submit the required Property Acknowledge-ment Receipt.

Please see related discussion on Part II, Finding No. 6 of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to fill out immediately the vacancy on the mandatory position for LDRRMO of the LGU pursuant to the provision of RA 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 so that disaster risk reduction management will be address in times of natural calamities.
observations.reason: Lack of funds

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to insure all insurable properties of the LGU with the GSIS in compliance with Republic Act No.656 and Presidential Decree 245 to indemnify losses that may arise from fire, theft and other causes, and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include the funding requirement of the same in its annual budget.
observations.reason: The LGU was not able to comply with the requirements of the GSIS.  One of these requirements is the submission of inventory of the contents of the building.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Treasury Office exert additional effort in collecting delinquent realty taxes to generate more income that could be used to finance other development project.
observations.managementAction: Deferment in serving notices, publications of tax delinquency in pursuing Administrative remedies and other activities related to appraisal and assessment of real properties based on BLGF MC No. 010-2020 and DOF Circular No.002-2020

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create an Inventory Committee for the conduct of complete and detailed
physical count of all the Municipality’s properties taking into account the
provisions mandated under COA Circular 2020-006;
b. instruct the Inventory Committee to properly utilize and fill out the required
inventory reports stated on the above-mentioned regulations;
c. order the Office of the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to prepare property cards
and Property Acknowledgement Receipt for all PPEs;
d. require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to work
coordinately on all causes of discrepancies towards the reconciliation of their
respective records to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in the
financial statements;
e. in spite the recommendations herein, strictly enforce Section 122(2) of PD
No. 1445 relative to the withholding of salary of concerned officials for failure
to submit reconciled and accurate inventory reports; and
f. request the Sangguniang Bayan to create the position of a General Services
Officer and at least two support staff positions for the filling up of a
permanent official and personnel in the ensuing year who will assume the
duties and responsibilities in the office and to address the perennial issues
relative to the supply and property management of the Municipality and;
g. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer for the inclusion of appropriate funds
for those created positions in the preparation of CY 2022 Budget.

We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare and submit the year-end BRS for all bank accounts within the
prescribed period to the Audit Team for review and verification to
establish the correct balances of the bank accounts of the Municipality
with its depository banks and to detect immediately fraud/irregularities in
the use of cash resources, if any;
b. make a detailed verification and effect the necessary corrections on the
discrepancies in the bank accounts appearing in the books with those of
the Treasury records;
c. adjust immediately all reconciling items, either in the books of the
Municipality or the depository banks, which should be properly notified,
to avoid accumulation thereof; and
d. properly turnover to the new Municipal Accountant pending works and
other issues and concern especially the reconciliation of records with the
Municipal Treasurer regarding the Cash and Cash Equivalent accounts in
line with her approaching retirement from Office.
• Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of
the stale checks for replacement, if applicable, and comply with the
procedures in the handling of stale/spoiled checks as stated in Section 104
of the GAM for LGUs.
• Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to evaluate and determine
without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect
reconciliation thereof and to perform the same regularly.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of
RPT and SET Receivables and the total delinquency taxes and effect the
necessary adjustments, if any;
b. Municipal Accountant investigate thoroughly the difference noted between
the above receivable accounts and effect the necessary adjustment thereof;
c. Municipal Accountant to adopt the new guidelines in recording the total
amount collectible at the beginning of the year; and
d. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the
current year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all
delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues
on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Inventory Committee to:
a. conduct of complete and detailed physical count of all the Municipality’s
inventory items at year-end; and
b. properly utilize the required inventory reports stated on the above-
mentioned regulations upon completion of the physical count.
• Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:
a. prepare stock cards for all inventory items maintained by the Municipality
and Inventory Custodian Slip for all inventory items to be issued;
b. record in the stock card the inventories received based on the AIR and
submit to the local accountant copies of the AIR, Purchase Order and
delivery receipts for the drawing of Journal voucher (JV) to record the
Inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory
ledger cards; and
c. prepare Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) upon issuance of inventory items
and summarize them monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued (RSMI) which shall be submitted to the local accountant within five
days after the end of the month for the drawing of JV to record expenses.
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting documents such as
Purchase Orders, Delivery Receipts and/or Supplier’s Invoice, draw a
Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal of
Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards; and
b. use weighted average method during the year in costing inventories
carried on stock and measure inventories for consumption and
distribution at the end of the year at the lower of cost and current
replacement cost as stated on the above Manual.
• Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. appropriate funds in ensuing year for enactment of the Sangguniang
Bayan for the filling up of the General Services Officer to have a
permanent official who will assume the duties and responsibilities in the
office and to address the perennial issues relative to the supply and
property management of the Municipality.

We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to exert time and effort to fully coordinate with the Provincial
Accountant and have a direct sit down review and analysis of the accounts to
determine the causes of the long over due deficiencies. If claims are fully
documented and establishes regularity of transactions, then
settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality. In case
exhaustion of all possible remedies and diligent efforts, the issue remain
unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on write-off of receivables and/or
reversion of accounts payables upon presentation of the required documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant:
a. to exert more effort to identify the sources of the unreconciled difference
between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds through
reconciliation of the accounts; and
b. after which, if the issue remain unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on
write-off of receivables and/or payables upon presentation of the required
documentation.

In view of the persistent non-compliance of the Municipality with the provisions
of the abovementioned regulations, we strongly reiterated that the Municipal
Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Engineer to device other methods to be able to provide the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with the complete description
and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger
cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said
property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above
cited regulations;
c. General Services Office/Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain road
networks property cards and to submit Report on Local Road Network;
d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s
Road Network Systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make
a thorough examination of the accounts with abnormal balances and effect the
necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the reliable
balances of the affected accounts.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the MSWDO to:
a. in coordination with the barangay officials, to continuously work hand-in-
hand in maintaining/updating profile of families under its jurisdiction to
properly and readily identify the eligible beneficiaries of various
programs/projects which may be initiated either by government or private
sector; and
b. provide justification on the result of the validation of ten (10) beneficiaries
being able to receive two SAP tranches despite their ineligibilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee, thru its Secretariat
to immediately present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate
aforementioned transactions in Annex H hereof and/or provide written
explanation/justification for the incomplete documentation;
b. Municipal Accountant to submitted immediately the list of lacking vouchers
presented above;
c. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure completeness of signature in the
Obligation Request form to certify the existence of available appropriation for
a project.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
refrain from charging unrelated expenses to the BGCM grant and allow only those
enumerated in Section 3.5 of LBC 125.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
refrain from recording procurements of inventory as outright expense and record
the same in the appropriate inventory account in compliance with Section 188,
Volume I of GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous entry in the recording of the
receipt of the BGCM grant and to properly reflect the balance of the Due to NGAs
account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents pertaining to the
above payments totaling ₱4,921,889.71 as discussed on observations a to c.
Ensure that claims against government funds be supported only with the
required documentations to avoid suspension in audit; and
b. Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the remaining
unsubmitted paid DVs and their supporting documents amounting to
₱10,235,365.22 to the Auditor for verification and audit to avoid the issuance of
notices of suspension and/or disallowance in audit on the subject
transactions.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Mr. Manuel Luis B.
Quizon to liquidate or refund the abovementioned cash advance of ₱59,408.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to deduct the correct amount of premiums and loan
amortizations due to avoid incurrence of penalties and remit the same
immediately to GSIS.
b. Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to ensure compliance with the required
employee’s net pay after deducting the loan amortizations prior to approving
loans.
c. Municipal Accountant and AAO to work hand in hand and coordinate with
the GSIS for the reconciliation of records to determine the correctness of the
unpaid premiums of PS and GS with a total amount of ₱₱297,569.65.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare individual financial statements with complete subsidiary ledgers for each
economic enterprise being operated by the Municipality which should form part of
the schedules of the General Fund.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Development Council to plan and identify specifically the projects
to be included in the Annual Investment Program;
b. Municipal Development Council and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that
PPAs that would be budgeted were the same as that presented per approved
Annual Investment Plan;
c. Municipal Budget Officer to secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan
before obligation and implementation for projects with lump sum
appropriation; and
d. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
monitor closely the implementation of projects and ensure maximum
utilization of the funds available through 100% completion and
accomplishments of programs, projects and activities due the public.
We further recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan refrain from approving the
Annual Budget of the Municipality which was supported with Annual Investment
Program with PPAs showing lumpsum appropriations.

We, therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording procurements of inventory as
outright expense and record the same in the appropriate inventory account
in compliance with Section 188, Volume I of GAM for LGUs; and
b. Municipal Accountant and Bids and Awards Committee, thru its Secretariat
to immediately present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate
aforementioned transactions and/or provide written explanation/justification
for the incomplete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMC to conduct an
in-depth planning of disaster risk management PPAs to be able to prepare and
submit a comprehensive LDRRMFIP with details as to projects name, location
and budget so as to properly guide the implementors in carrying out the listed
specific projects towards the attainment of a disaster resilient Municipality.
We would like to reiterate that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDDRMO to ensure
that the list of projects and activities which are to be charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF in the Trust Fund books is included in the LDRRMFIP to be prepared
yearly.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the MDRRMC and the
MDRRMO to closely coordinate and fully implement the crucial DRRM PPAs for
the immediate benefit of the constituents of the Municipality.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO, to include all LDRRM funds in the
Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund to fairly present information to the end users.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
transfer the unspent QRF and MF (MOOE) for CY 2020 in the total amount of
₱2,694,531.14 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books and observe the
same in the succeeding years to fairly present the financial statements of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
immediately transfer the unspent Mitigation Fund (Capital Outlay) from CY 2018
amounting to₱3,377,098.50 to the General Fund and avoid further transfer of
unspent Mitigation Fund (Capital Outlay) to the Trust Fund in the succeeding
years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to exert more
effort to reconcile the difference of P40,023,265.68 between the accounting
records of PPE and RPCPPE;
b) the General Services Office to provide the details of Land amounting to
P4,829,584.72 taken up in the RPCPPE in lump sum; and
c) the Municipal Accountant to verify the accuracy of the computed
depreciation expense totaling P53,545,187.22, record the same in the books
and submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Audit Team for
validation. Henceforth, ensure to recognize depreciation of all PPEs on a
regular basis in accordance with IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the concerned Municipal officials and personnel to immediately
settle their outstanding cash advances with the reminder that under
existing laws and regulations, salaries of personnel who unjustifiably
fail to settle their cash advances may be withheld accordingly;
b. Institute necessary measures to ensure strict compliance of all
Municipal officials and employees with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD No. 1445, particularly on the timely
liquidation of cash advances; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the books the liquidations
of the CYs 2010 and 2011 Intelligence Fund Cash Advances since the
transmittal letters and the supporting liquidation documents had
already been submitted to and duly received by the ICFAU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the General Services Office (GSO) and the Municipal Accounting Office to
reconcile their respective inventory records and submit the corresponding
report, including the adjusting entries made, if any, to the COA Audit Team
for further evaluation; and
b. the GSO to submit to the Accounting Office the SSMI duly supported with
RIS and complete distribution list of the 500 sets of hygiene kits amounting
to P538,000.00 to facilitate the adjustment of the Other Supplies and
Materials Held for Distribution account in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit all unsubmitted CY 2020 DVs and their
supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification and post audit, otherwise
Notice of Suspensions will be issued. Henceforth, ensure the submission of all
reports and accounts within 10 days after the end of the month in accordance with
Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible Municipal officials to
facilitate the immediate submission to the Audit Team of the proof of posting of
Notices of Award, Contracts or Purchase Orders in the Municipality’s website and
Mayor’s Permits, among other documents required under GPPB Circular No. 01-
2020, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions;
b) the concerned Municipal officials/personnel in charge of the receipts and
distributions of welfare goods to submit a report detailing the quantity and
composition of goods he/she or their office received for repacking, number of
food packs actually repacked and list of beneficiaries with summary, for Audit
Team’s validation; and
c) the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the guidelines provided for
under COA Circular No. 2014-002 by recording the procurement of all
supplies/materials for disaster response and rescue programs and activities
under the appropriate Inventory account and to recognize them only as expense
upon issuance to beneficiaries or end-users and upon submission of RIS and
other proof of distribution of goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible Municipal officials
to facilitate the immediate submission to the Audit Team of the lacking
documents such as Income Tax Returns of suppliers, proof of posting of
Notices of Awards, among others, and the lists of recipients of food packs
from the three barangays, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the
transactions; and
b) the concerned Municipal officials/personnel in charge of the receipts and
distributions of food packs to account for the 1,350 food packs allocated to
Barangay Tejero Convention, to prevent suspension and/or disallowance in
audit.
