We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the LDRRM Council to identify
and incorporate under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP the projects and
activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of CYs 2017 and 2018
and prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to
ensure that the total appropriation for GAD have corresponding PPAs in the GPB
and to strictly adhere to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No.
2013-01, particularly on what expenses could be and could not be charged to the
GAD Fund.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point System to strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanisms of the Municipality’s CBMS and GAD databases through preparation of master plan indicating the timelines, process, budget and other necessary actions.  Thereafter, start the implementation in CY 2021 in order to ensure updated, operational and reliable information provided by said databases which will serve in attaining an evidenced-based planning, policy-making, and identification of beneficiaries for relief distribution and financial assistance in the future.
We have recommended further that if necessary, the Management to augment the number of employees responsible for the maintenance and development of the databases, more specifically to update the data contained therein because data gathering and collating mechanism of the GAD Database and CBMS database is certainly a tedious work.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO to i) coordinate with the MDRRM Office to revisit and revise the existing plan for the distribution of relief goods to better uphold the necessary promptness and uniformity by convening as often as possible to update the plans according to current events; and ii) ensure documentation for the release of relief goods to the various Barangay Officials, and see to it that the data contained therein may be used for control purposes.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Accountant to:
Appropriately recognize the transactions ensuring consistency and accuracy of recording among the journals, ledgers and subsidiary ledgers;
make necessary correcting entries as follows:
Dr	Accounts Payable		P704,572.66
Dr	Due to BIR		   686,350.26
Cr		Expenses/Assets		P1,390,922.92
To record correcting entries on misstatements noted in AP account.
Properly recognize Accounts Payable through gross amounts of the corresponding Assets/Expenses incurred;
Submit the documents supporting the existence of recognized AP;
Furnish copy of AP to the Municipal Treasurer facilitate monitoring of payment thereof; and
Internal control process of settlement of AP and to furnish report as a result hereto which may include provision of additional manpower, among others.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Engineer to furnish copy of Government Report on Programs/Projects/Activities to Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate;
The Municipal Accountant to i) maintain copies of Memoranda of Agreement between the LGU and National Government Agencies; ii) record completed and on-going projects to the year it occurred regardless if paid or not; iii) maintain completed records of the PPEs; and iv) reconcile the same with the property cards of the GSO-Designate; and
The GSO-Designate to i) complete his property records; and conduct annual physical inventory of all properties of the Municipality as basis in the preparation of RPCPPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to i) trace the remaining discrepancy of P294,000.00 between the balances shown in the summary and the table provided, and effect the necessary adjustments in the reports, or even in the Financial Statements, if necessary; ii) reconcile records with the Municipal Budget Officer so that the information shown in the Financial Statements will be in conformity with each other; and iii) revert the P33,938.74 to General Fund so that it will be used according to plans and appropriations, and if necessary, trace other Capital Outlay savings or continuing appropriations that were transferred to Trust Liability.

We have recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection on completed projects after the one-year defects liability period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if there are no defects detected or after such were repaired or corrected.
Further, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to oblige the contractor to post warranty security that will address the structural defects that may be discovered from the final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2.
Lastly, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Audit Team copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed contracts to monitor the posting of the said warranty security by the contractors.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
Scrutinize the sufficiency of the Contractor’s manpower on all awarded projects especially those that have overlapping implementation period with each other under same contractor and direct the contractors to address the deficiencies noted on manpower utilization;
Conduct post-qualification by verifying, validating, and ascertaining all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid and ensure that the Post-Qualification Report contains in-depth results on the verification of technical requirements; and
Determine the applicable liquidated damages on the projects that have been delayed without the corresponding requests for extensions. deficiency.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to observe the proper procurement process applicable to the provision of financial assistance to the needy in attempt to enter into an agreement with the funeral service provider prospectively, so that remedies and benefits may be availed of in favor of the Government, given that the Covid-19 situation is an on-going concern of the country.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to come up with a certain and clear-cut set of criteria in providing financial assistances to the needy, especially now that the country is economic shambles, and every public fund should be accounted for and should be allocated effectively.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to review/re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures on AICS Program, and adopt a written policy on the determination as to the appropriateness, procedure and payment of financial assistance to individuals/families in crisis situation, considering the nature, eligibility of beneficiaries, amount of financial assistance and such other measures that would facilitate the effective and efficient implementation of the AICS Program of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all financial assistance under AICS will undergo the process of assessment by the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office personnel.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Treasurer and Liquidating Officer to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits and properly acknowledge remittances received;
The Collectors to fill-up the Sections B and C in the Report of Collections and Deposits; and
The Municipal Accountant to i) complete the data requirements in the Journals and Journal Entry Vouchers and to certify correct them, and ii) use the General Journal and the Cash Receipts Journal for the purpose these were created.

1.6	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Regularly prepare a correct and complete monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the individual bank accounts maintained in the Authorized Government Depository Bank;
Regularly secure copies of bank statement or bank snapshot, and bank Debit and Credit Memos to facilitate the preparation of BRS; and
Examine the reconciling items and prepare journal entry vouchers for the necessary adjustments in the books of the LGU.

3.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the remaining balance of the BGCM fund in the Trust Liabilities account as a Special Account in the General Fund as of December 31, 2020, and correct the Due to NGAs account in order that these accounts will be fairly presented in the Statement of Financial Position.
3.9	We likewise recommended that the said grant be adequately disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

4.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide additional information to all accounts in the notes, especially those showing significant increase/decrease. Likewise, the notes should also indicate the nature, composition or any changes thereto pursuant to the provisions of IPSAS Volume I and Section 111 of PD 1445, to provide readers with the information necessary for the full understanding of the operation of the LGU.

5.9	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require all the personnel concerned, especially the Municipal Accountant, to secure complete documentations on all disbursements, to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions.  Significantly, management thru the LCE and Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.
5.10	We also recommended for the submission of the lacking supporting documents for the aforementioned transactions.

6.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the personnel concerned, especially the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, the submission of PAR for all issued government property to facilitate the determination of the persons accountable thereof in case of its loss.

7.7	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
a. 	Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, and the Municipal DRRM Officer to submit the same through the LDRRMC and LDC to the auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.  Likewise, reflect in the report the cash assistance received from national government, other LGUs, and other sources; and
b. 	Municipal Accountant to make sure that complete documentations are attached on all disbursements to ensure the propriety and validity of the transactions.

8.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and all personnel concerned to attach complete documentations on all disbursements to ensure the validity as well as propriety of the transactions.  Significantly, management should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.

9.5	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.  This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities.
9.6	Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

10.11	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Administrator, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184, COA and GPPB issued JMC No. 1, and COA Circular No. 2017-001 to ensure propriety and validity of the procurements, and that the LGU obtain the most advantageous price in its procurements.

11.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the LGU officials concerned to comply with the policies and guidelines on the grant of overtime pay to LGU officials and employees.
11.9	This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and Disallowance as a result of our audit.

12.7	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
a.  	Review the taxes withheld and remittances to ascertain the correct amount of taxes, and immediately remit the unremitted balance; and
b. 	Strictly adhere to the provisions of BIR Revenue Regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes.

13.11	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other local officials concerned to:
Re-evaluate and re-assess the programmed priority PPAs embodied in the AIPs, and review if the AIPs are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the Municipality to ensure full utilization of the fund;
Maximize the utilization of the fund for CoViD-19-related PPAs to address surging issues on health, safety, and well-being of the residents of the Municipality; and
Implement immediately all unimplemented PPAs that are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the LGU for the attainment of socio-economic, and environmental management outcomes.

1.8 We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Unit and the Property Custodian to conduct regular reconciliation of PPE per books and per count and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective records. Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the PPE accounts under the Trust Fund to the General Fund and provide the corresponding depreciation including those recorded under the SEF.

We recommended that Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the undistributed rice to the related inventory account. Henceforth, record the purchases of all goods and materials, whether for stock or for distribution, under the inventory account, regardless of its charges pursuant to Section 188 and 134 of the GAM, Volume I and recognize the expense upon receipt of the RSMI duly supported with RIS and other necessary supporting documents; and
Require the Supply Officer and the MSWDO to trace the RDS for the unaccounted goods to support the reported distribution to provide valid basis on the regularity of the transaction.

3.5 We recommended that Management observe the provisions set forth under COA Circular No. 2020-09 dated April 21, 2020 regarding the reporting and accounting guidelines on all donations received and to require the officer concerned to prepare and submit to the NDRRMC, through the OCD the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for all donations received and include thereto the 53 sacks of rice received for PWDs. Instruct the MSWDO to trace the distribution documents of these goods, otherwise the persons concerned shall be held accountable thereof.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the requirements prescribed in the existing rules and regulations, especially on the creation of special accounts for specific fund receipts to facilitate easy monitoring on the use of the fund and to provide the users of the financial statement of detailed report.

We recommended that the Management instruct the BAC to secure the lacking supporting documents from the suppliers concerned and submit the same to the Auditor for further evaluation. Henceforth, emergency purchases of the LGU should be supported with the basic documents prescribed under GPPB Circular 01-2020.

We recommended that Management direct the MEE Manager to submit to the Accounting Office the complete details of the uncollected water billings as of year-end to provide the Accountant with data as basis in recording the income and its corresponding receivables. Also, constantly reminds the consumers of the penal clause under the Municipal Ordinance 17, s. 2018 to promote prompt payment of water bills.

8.7 We recommended that the Management stop the inclusion of lump-sum appropriation in the AIP, instead, require the submission of a detailed and specific name of the project with its costing duly supported with program of works. See to it that the development projects being proposed under the 20% DF partakes the nature of capital investment in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in JMC No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer continue to monitor the implementation of all on-going development projects to ensure its completion as planned in order to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of DILG and DBM.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. create an Inventory Committee for the conduct of complete and detailed physical count of all the Municipality’s properties taking into account the provisions mandated under COA Circular 2020-006; b. instruct the Inventory Committee to properly utilize and fill out the required inventory reports stated on the above-mentioned regulations; c. order the Office of the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to prepare property cards and Property Acknowledgement Receipt for all PPEs; d. require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Municipal Treasurer to work coordinately on all causes of discrepancies towards the reconciliation of their respective records to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in the financial statements; e. in spite the recommendations herein, strictly enforce Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445 relative to the withholding of salary of concerned officials for failure to submit reconciled and accurate inventory reports; f. request the Sangguniang Bayan to create the position of a General Services Officer and at least two support staff positions for the filling up of a permanent official and personnel in the ensuing year who will assume the duties and responsibilities in the office and to address the perennial issues relative to the supply and property management of the Municipality; and g. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer for the inclusion of appropriate funds for those created positions in the preparation of CY 2022 Budget.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 21, 2021, stated that there must be an inventory committee to handle the physical inventory of the PPE. Also, they are using the disbursement vouchers for the procurement of a certain PPE as a reference. Further, no request for disposal has been furnished to the accounting office for the proper disposal of unserviceable properties and equipment.
On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer, on her letter dated June 24, 202, indicated that the recommendations are well taken by the management with regards to the incomplete report on the physical count of Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment. Also, her being the GSO Designate had started conducting actual inventory from different offices of the LGU to update the record of property, plant and equipment. Moreover, a request of record of property, plant and equipment from the Office of the Municipal Accountant was made but due some constraints the properties booked up by the Accounting Office cannot be located.
observations.reason: The GSO-Designate is the yet. Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Accountant to: a. prepare and submit the year-end BRS for all bank accounts within the prescribed period to the Audit Team for review and verification to establish the correct balances of the bank accounts of the Municipality with its depository banks and to detect immediately fraud/irregularities in the use of cash resources, if any; b. make a detailed verification and effect the necessary corrections on the discrepancies in the bank accounts appearing in the books with those of the Treasury records; c. adjust immediately all reconciling items, either in the books of the Municipality or the depository banks, which should be properly notified, to avoid accumulation thereof; and d. properly turnover to the new Municipal Accountant pending works and other issues and concern especially the reconciliation of records with the Municipal Treasurer regarding the Cash and Cash Equivalent accounts in line with her approaching retirement from Office. • Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks for replacement, if applicable, and comply with the procedures in the handling of stale/spoiled checks as stated in Section 104 of the GAM for LGUs. • Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof and to perform the same regularly.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 21, 2021, stated that reconciliation is being made by the accounting and treasury office regarding the differences in the cash account. The adjustments are to be made once the discrepancies have been established. Moreover, she also indicated that there was no turnover yet of accounts, since as of date, no designation for OIC has been made.
On the other hand, the Municipal Treasurer on her letter dated June 23, 2021, pointed out that her Office is currently in the process of evaluating and determining the cause/s of variance between records as evidenced by the aforecited reconciling items provided to the Audit Team. She also stated that the stale checks due to Rosario Hog Raiser and San Isidro MPC amounting to ₱200,000.00 and ₱10,000.00, respectively, were already reverted to Cash in Bank account per JEV Nos. 401-2021-05 and 401-2021-06 dated 05/31/2021 and 06/25/2021, accordingly.
observations.reason: There were still reconciling items for further verification. Reconcilation is currently being made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables and the total delinquency taxes and effect the necessary adjustments, if any; b. Municipal Accountant investigate thoroughly the difference noted between the above receivable accounts and effect the necessary adjustment thereof; c. Municipal Accountant to adopt the new guidelines in recording the total amount collectible at the beginning of the year; and d. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the current year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues on time.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer, on her letter dated June 24, 2021, indicated that their Office will coordinate to the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPT/SET receivables, same with the Municipal Assessor to furnish them corrected and updated taxpayer’s record for reliable RPT/SET Receivables.
On the other hand, she added that her Office continuously conducted a thorough scrutiny and investigation regarding the ballooned real property tax delinquencies. It was found out that some of real properties which should be treated as exempted were indicated taxable. Moreover, coordination with the Municipal Assessor was done on the cleansing of records and an assurance from the Municipal Assessor that such thing will be done. They also conducted an intensive RPT campaign to inform the property owners to pay their taxes on time and even granted discounts when paid before due dates and non-payment of taxes for a very long period as long as they were properly notified can result to higher penalties or losing their properties at all.
The Municipal Treasurer also coordinated with the Barangay Captains of the Municipality to assist them to locate the exact address of the property owners indicated in the tax declaration to enable them to give the owners their statement of tax liabilities. She also added that banners and streamers were posted in barangay halls and other conspicuous place in the municipality the schedules of Real property payments and the discount that will be given to early payees. In addition, they also conduct an information campaign to all property owners of the Municipality with emphasis on granting discounts when paid before due dates and the possibility of losing their property without paying their taxes.
Meanwhile, the Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 24, 2021, stated that regarding the differences noted in the setting up of RPT Receivable, her Office will comply to adopt the new guidelines in recording the total amount collectible at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: Reconciliation to be made; differences to be investigated; measures ongoing including cleansing of records and information campaigns.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Inventory Committee to: a. conduct of complete and detailed physical count of all the Municipality’s inventory items at year-end; and b. properly utilize the required inventory reports stated on the above-mentioned regulations upon completion of the physical count. • Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer to: a. prepare stock cards for all inventory items maintained by the Municipality and Inventory Custodian Slip for all inventory items to be issued; b. record in the stock card the inventories received based on the AIR and submit to the local accountant copies of the AIR, Purchase Order and delivery receipts for the drawing of Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards; and c. prepare Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) upon issuance of inventory items and summarize them monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) which shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month for the drawing of JV to record expenses. • Municipal Accountant to: a. upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting documents such as Purchase Orders, Delivery Receipts and/or Supplier’s Invoice, draw a Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards; and b. use weighted average method during the year in costing inventories carried on stock and measure inventories for consumption and distribution at the end of the year at the lower of cost and current replacement cost as stated on the above Manual. • Municipal Budget Officer to: a. appropriate funds in ensuing year for enactment of the Sangguniang Bayan for the filling up of the General Services Officer to have a permanent official who will assume the duties and responsibilities in the office and to address the perennial issues relative to the supply and property management of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 24, 2021, reasoned out that regarding the year-end balances of the inventory accounts, their Office uses only data available and are attached to the disbursement voucher. No data was furnished by the GSO to be used in the proper recording of ending inventory.
Whereas, the Municipal Treasurer/GSO on her letter dated June 25, 2021, stated that all the audit recommendations are well taken by the management. She added that her Office as GSO had started conducting actual inventory from different offices of the LGU to update its record of property, plant and equipment. Her Office will prepare stock cards for all inventory items maintained by the Municipality and Inventory Custodian Slip for all inventory items to be issued and will record in the stock card the inventories received based on the AIR and submit to the local accountant copies of the AIR, Purchase Order and delivery receipts for the drawing of Journal Voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards and will prepare Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) upon issuance of inventory items and summarize them monthly in the Report of the Supplies and Materials (RSMI) which shall be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month for the drawing of JV to record expenses.
On the other hand, the Municipal Budget Officer on her letter dated June 28, 2021, declared that once the General Services Officer position was requested for creation, her Office will definitely appropriate funds for it. But as of the moment, it is still not created.
observations.reason: Stock Cards are not yet prepared and maintained; GSO has started conducting inventory; funds/position not yet created.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to exert time and effort to fully coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and have a direct sit down review and analysis of the accounts to determine the causes of the long over due deficiencies. If claims are fully documented and establishes regularity of transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the Municipality. In case exhaustion of all possible remedies and diligent efforts, the issue remain unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on write-off of receivables and/or reversion of accounts payables upon presentation of the required documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 21, 2020, indicated that under the Trust Fund, no data was received by the accounting office to recognize receivables from the province. She added that reconciliation is to be made regarding the variances.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is to be made; incomplete information from both offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant: a. to exert more effort to identify the sources of the unreconciled difference between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds through reconciliation of the accounts; and b. after which, if the issue remain unsettled, resort to prescribed procedures on write-off of receivables and/or payables upon presentation of the required documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 21, 2021, stated that they will exert more effort to identify the sources of differences of both accounts through reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: In view of the persistent non-compliance of the Municipality with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations, we strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Engineer to device other methods to be able to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited regulations; c. General Services Office/Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and to submit Report on Local Road Network; d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s Road Network Systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System as of the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 21, 2021, specified that her Office did not received any data from the concerned offices which will be their reference for recording. Only JEV for the transaction is used for reference of acquisition and recording in the books of accounts. She further stated that nevertheless, they maintain schedule for depreciations expenses.
observations.reason: Lack of data from concerned offices; no complete disclosure prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a thorough examination of the accounts with abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the reliable balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, on her letter dated June 24, 2021, expressed that upon analysis, they discovered that those accounts with abnormal balances did not include its beginning balances of the prior year. She promised that they will make the corrections and necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Adjustments to be made to include prior year beginning balances and correct balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. concerned officials to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification or justification; b. clearly state total estimated cost of the projects on the Invitation to Payments and accordingly submit the fully filled out invitation for the payments; c. forward to the Audit Team the aforementioned documents and Annex B of the Agreement and documents as required; d. provide justification on variances; and e. provide explanations/justification on lapses noted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer, on her letter dated June 28, 2021 claimed that upon checking the subject DVs, they have initial signature of the Officer on duty the day the voucher was obligated. She added that her Office believed that the said transaction was in immediate response to Corona Virus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic, that is why, for the payment to be made, said vouchers was processed even though there is only an initial sign with the advice that said voucher needs to be brought back to their Office once the OIC is on duty.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were presented; other required documents remain unsubmitted; justification pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board and Municipal Accountant to ensure that SEF projects are implemented in accordance with laws and to submit justifications for expenses charged to SEF which are ineligible, and to stop practice of splitting of contracts and adhere to RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management as of report. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer were to coordinate and ensure proper documentation; some actions are ongoing.
observations.reason: Some corrective actions undertaken; several transactions still require documentation and justification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a. Heads of each Office to prepare PPMPs and submit them to Budget Office; b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate and prepare APP and ensure procurement compliance; c. Municipal Accountant to check supporting documents; d. BAC to post Notices and ensure posting in PhilGEPS and agency premises.
observations.managementAction: Some PPMPs prepared and submitted for certain departments; BAC and Secretariat to comply; Municipality still resorted to procurement by shopping and emergency methods in some cases. No full compliance yet.
observations.reason: APP/PPMP not fully consolidated; deviations and late postings noted; some corrective actions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to: a. exert active efforts in accomplishing targeted client-focused and organizational-focused PPAs; b. secure Certificate of Endorsement from DILG Provincial Office before incorporating GPB to Annual Budget; c. ensure mainstreaming of GAD activities across all offices; d. coordinate with MHRMO to require personnel to undergo Gender Sensitivity Training.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal GAD Focal Person stated efforts are being exerted; GPB endorsement delayed due to review timelines; integration and training to be pursued; some GAD activities implemented and 63% of GAD budget utilized.
observations.reason: Delayed endorsement and partial mainstreaming; COVID-19 affected conduct of trainings and full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop granting financial assistance for private purposes and require responsible persons to institute legal action for misuse; require preparation of specific guidelines/policies for grants and sponsorships including qualifications, amounts, frequency, mode of payment and documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: No specific guidelines yet established; Municipality continues to provide some assistance; management to prepare guidelines.
observations.reason: No finalized policy; some assistance programs continued pending formal guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. in coordination with the HRMO, reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify affected balances and remit the amounts due; b. ensure immediate remittance of correct amounts; and c. coordinate with GSIS for reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant indicated deductions and withholdings completed by HRMO and statement of remittances being prepared. Reconciliation to be made between accounting and HR offices for correctness.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing between accounting and HR; e-copy differences noted requiring further verification with GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare individual financial statements with complete subsidiary ledgers for each economic enterprise which should form part of the schedules of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant stated they are updating the financial statements of the local economic enterprises; transactions still entered under General Fund ECPAC system.
observations.reason: Consolidation and separation of enterprise accounts still ongoing; schedules not yet fully prepared.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated recommendations that the Municipal Development Council identify specific projects to be included in the AIP; Municipal Budget Officer to ensure PPAs budgeted match approved AIP; secure Sangguniang Bayan approval before obligation for lump sum appropriations; Municipal Engineer and MPDO to monitor implementation to ensure 100% completion and utilization.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Budget Officer indicated efforts to improve AIP and budgeting; some projects still in lump sum; budget officer will ensure SB approval before obligation.
observations.reason: Some projects remain lump sum; SB practice influences itemization; improvements planned for CY 2021 but lapses persisted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMC to conduct in-depth planning of DRRM PPAs to prepare comprehensive LDRRMFIP with project details; ensure unexpended LDRRMF from prior years in Trust Fund are included in LDRRMFIP; Municipal Accountant to include all LDRRM funds in the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant noted preparer of report will be advised to include all funds in reporting; Municipal officials explained that remaining balances were reserved for COVID-19 and other calamities; partial implementations made.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP lacked specific details and failed to include unexpended prior year funds; some projects partially implemented; COVID-19 affected implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure rules, specific guidelines, appraisal or evaluation system and limits (e.g., monetary awards not to exceed 20% of savings generated) are established and used before granting awards; consider individual appraisal or evaluation report to assess performance.
observations.managementAction: No specific system yet designed by the Municipality. They acknowledged issue and shall consider for future grants.
observations.reason: No formal guidelines established; practice was applied previously without full documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned contractors be required to rectify deficiencies or be blacklisted; BAC to ensure compliance with procurement processes; Municipal Engineer to conduct final inspection and require submission of as-built plans, warranties, and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Some contractors rectified noted defects and pictures were submitted to Audit Team, others remain to provide proof of rectification; Municipal Engineer conducted inspection on selected projects.
observations.reason: Some projects rectified; others still pending; pictures/proofs for certain projects not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor compel barangays to establish functional MRFs, ensure projects are prioritized & included in LDRRMFIP, and enforce compliance with COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to include funds in report; LDRRMO to include projects; some PPAs partially implemented; COVID-19 impacted implementation.
observations.reason: Lack of full inclusion of prior year unexpended projects in LDRRMFIP and partial implementation due to COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended BAC observe IRR of RA 9184, post notices and contracts in PhilGEPS and premises, require warranty securities and ensure proper inspection and documentation.
observations.managementAction: BAC to comply; some postings made; Municipal Engineer conducting inspections; some documents submitted late.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of perfected contracts and supporting documents; some warranty/security requirements not enforced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Local School Board to implement projects aligned with laws; stop splitting contracts; BAC to ensure compliance with procurement law; provide justifications for charges to SEF.
observations.managementAction: No action yet taken by management; Municipal Accountant to coordinate and correct; some invoices and DVs under review.
observations.reason: Some corrective actions taken but several supporting documents and justifications remain unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended BAC and concerned offices prepare PPMPs, APP, observe procurement rules, obtain NFA waiver where applicable, and ensure proper posting/PhilGEPS compliance.
observations.managementAction: Some PPMPs prepared; some procurement by shopping and emergency methods still observed; no waivers for rice procured not from NFA were submitted.
observations.reason: APP/PPMP incompletely prepared and consolidated; some procurement irregularities continued; evidence of waivers lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor require GAD Focal Person to actively implement GAD PPAs, secure endorsement prior to budget incorporation, mainstream GAD across departments and require gender sensitivity training for staff.
observations.managementAction: Municipality exerted efforts; some GAD programs implemented (63% expenditures); GPB endorsement delayed; training constrained.
observations.reason: Endorsement delayed; mainstreaming partial; COVID-19 impacted activities and trainings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended preparation and implementation of a detailed plan to establish and operate a sanitary landfill; allocate budget and conduct planning.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to allocate budget and conduct detailed planning on establishment of sanitary landfill; private hauler engaged under contract for collection/disposal.
observations.reason: Detailed planning and budget allocation pending; sanitary landfill establishment not yet achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant and responsible offices to ensure cash advances are used appropriately, liquidated timely, demand letters issued, withhold salaries for unliquidated AOs, refrain from processing new advances until prior are settled, and ensure purchases comply with RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant acknowledged issues, promised adjustments, demanded letters issued; retrieval of documents and reconciliations ongoing; some cash advances liquidated; no withholding yet implemented in some cases.
observations.reason: Some demand letters issued and recoveries initiated; complete liquidation and full compliance pending; documentation gaps remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant ascertain collectability, prepare schedules and supporting documents, pursue write-offs if uncollectible with Sanggunian resolution, and disclose nature/status of receivables in Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant prepared schedules and initiated evaluation; further verification pending; no legal action reported for dormant/closed bank deposits.
observations.reason: Evaluation ongoing; disclosures in Notes to Financial Statements incomplete; recovery/write-off actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor direct: (a) Bids and Awards to ensure procurement compliance and provide documentation; (b) BAC Secretariat to ensure postings and certifications; (c) contractors to provide warranty; (d) MPDC/MC to prioritize and implement projects; and (e) Municipal Engineer to monitor implementation.
observations.managementAction: Some documents submitted late; BAC and MPDC took steps; some projects implemented in latter part of year; improvements ongoing.
observations.reason: Projects were implemented late; documentation and posting deficiencies noted; actions taken but not fully completed.

observations.managementAction: The General Services Office had submitted to the Audit Team the RPCPPE with details of property totalling P7,786,476.50.
The GSO had submitted to the Accounting Office the documents relating to the donated and disposed PPEs to the Accounting Office for recording/dropping from the books;
observations.reason: The recording/dropping in the books of accounts is still on going.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the agency had disposed five unserviceable motor vehicles costing P2,638,700.00
observations.reason: Disposal of unserviceable property is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the non-current portion of the Loans Payable- Domestic to Non-Current Liabilities category in the Statement of Financial Position to ensure fair presentation of liabilities of the Municipality as at year-end. It is further recommended that he require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the outstanding balance of the loan with the creditor bank.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already reclassified and adjusted the non-current portion of the Loans-Payable Domestic to Non-Current Liabilities in the CY 2020 Statement of Financial Position. The Municipal Accountant had already made the adjusting entries in the books and reconciled the loan balance with the creditor bank.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the guidelines provided for under COA Circular No. 2014-002 by recording the procurement of all supplies/materials for disaster response and rescue programs and activities under the appropriate Inventory account and to recognize them only as expense upon issuance to beneficiaries or end-users and upon submission of RIS and other proof of distribution of goods; b) the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the pertinent resolution declaring the Municipality or the Province, as the case maybe, under state of calamity to support the use of QRF to procure rice costing P3,700,000.00, otherwise the amount will be suspended in audit; and c) the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the MDRRMF Monthly Utilization Report duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO had submitted to the Audit Team the resolution declaring the Municipality or the Province, as the case maybe, under state of calamity to support the use of QRF to procure rice costing P3,700,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the guidelines provided for under COA Circular No. 2014-002 by recording the procurement of all supplies/materials for disaster response and rescue programs and activities under the appropriate Inventory account and to recognize them only as expense upon issuance to beneficiaries or end-users and upon submission of RIS and other proof of distribution of goods; b) the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the pertinent resolution declaring the Municipality or the Province, as the case maybe, under state of calamity to support the use of QRF to procure rice costing P3,700,000.00, otherwise the amount will be suspended in audit; and c) the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the MDRRMF Monthly Utilization Report duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Officer had prepared and submitted to the Audit Team the MDRRMF Utilization Reports on a quarterly basis.
observations.reason: The MDRRM Officer still unable to prepare the Utilization Report monthly as required by the regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSB strictly adhere with the guidelines provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. of 2017, particularly on the formulation/preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 and the three-year program and the preparation and submission of the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization to the concerned offices.
observations.managementAction: Municipal School Board already prepared and submitted the SEF Budget Preparation Form 1 and the three-year program on the preparation of SEF Budget.

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials and personnel to strictly adhere to Section 93 of PD No. 1445 which requires government agencies to make direct payments to suppliers and ensure that the provisions of RA 9184 on the appropriate method of procurement and BIR Revenue Regulation 2-98 on the withholding of taxes are not circumvented.
observations.managementAction: The agency had reduced the payments through reimbursement from 113 Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) amounting ranging from P22,087,847.65 of P760,397.56 to last year to 43 DVs aggregating P11,722,915.65 for CY 2020.
observations.reason: The agency had not fully reduced the amount of payments through reimbursement as transactions still recorded as outright expense.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the affected 2018 financial statements pertaining to the afore-mentioned observations for fair presentation and prepare necessary adjusting entries on the erroneous recording of collection and reversion of disallowances.

We also recommended that the Management establish cut-off date as part of their annual closing activity to ensure that all transactions are properly recognized in the correct period.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant effected the necessary adjustments to restate the financial statements.




The Management established a cut-off date to comply with the deadlines set by the Bureau of Local Government Finance and Provincial Government. The cut-off date in CY 2019 was December 18, 2019 while in CY 2020, it was on December 23, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the deposit slips and disbursement vouchers of the transactions presented as reconciling items for the current year 

And to exert effort in tracing the disbursement vouchers pertaining to prior year period.  



And thereafter, to reconcile their records and make the necessary adjustments in their records as needed in order to reflect the timely and accurate position of the Municipality.




We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer trace the details on the long outstanding checks particularly as to its date and its whereabouts, and thereafter, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to make the proper adjustment on these cancelled checks.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer submitted the deposit slips, while the Municipal Accountant submitted the disbursement vouchers.



The Municipal Accountant had already recorded P4,114,819.52 pertaining to CY 2019 transactions under General Fund. However, tracing of the disbursement vouchers pertaining to CY 2018 transactions amounting to P456,364.91 is still on-going.


The Management prepared reconciliation of records only as of November 2020.





The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer had already traced the details of the long outstanding checks totaling to P2,889,221.84. Adjustments for the stale checks and cancelled checks will be made in 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:

•	The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and

•	The Municipal Accountant prepare necessary journal entries to correct the balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts as well as the Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income in order to fairly present said accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer has furnished the Municipal Accountant the duly certified list.


The Municipal Accountant has prepared the necessary and correct journal entries in January 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to submit immediately the 32 contracts amounting to P36,855,914.16, including all documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation to facilitate timely conduct of contract review of the Auditor in order to immediately communicate deficiencies noted and for the management to address the weaknesses.  

Likewise, submit the lacking documentary requirements as stated above.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality has submitted the 32 contracts.





The lacking documentary requirements for the other noted transactions were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management ensure existence of an appropriation before obligation and consequently, payment be made in conformity with Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.  

Likewise, adhere to the posting requirements of Section 21.2 and Annex H of Republic Act No. 9184 prior to acceptance of procured goods in line with the attainment of transparency and competitiveness of government procurement, and sound internal control.
observations.managementAction:
The recommendation has been applied prospectively.





The posting requirements are now prospectively being adhered to.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in order attain government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality now strictly complies with the requirements of the law, and has ceased to make similar transactions to the one noted in the observation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management, through the Municipal Development Council ensure that development funds are utilized through proper prioritization and planning the PPAs to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management for the benefit of the constituents.  


The local finance committee shall likewise consider availing credit facilities without needing to allocate an enormous portion of their Internal Revenue Allotment to repay loans leaving a lesser portion for the delivery of essential public service.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Development Council now ensures that the development funds are utilized according to the plans and priorities.





The local finance committee now considers other financing methods in their activities.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to maintain Community-based Monitoring System to facilitate the LGU addressing appropriately and accurately the probable measures before, during and after calamities;




require the MDRRMO to include in the propose LDRRMFIP which is to be approved by the MDRRMC the procurement of forklift, jack hammer, shoring and concrete cutter needed for disaster preparedness, response and recovery of the municipality.  Thereafter procure through the Bids and Awards Committee, the needed tools and equipment; and

capacitate the MDRRMO and his staff through seminars like preparation of Business Continuity Plan, Hazardous Materials Management, Management of the Dead and the Missing, Local Climate Change Action Plan Formulation, Pre-disaster Recovery Planning and Rapid Damage Assessment and Needs Analysis.
observations.managementAction:
Initial actions were made, but the implementation is still on going.





The proposed LDRRMFIP has been submitted, and is subject for approval.




In-house capacity building is being made. Meanwhile, outside trainings are halted due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local School Board cease the practice of budgeting and charging expenditures related to training of its teachers, Alternative Learning System and prizes under ECCD programs against Special Education Fund. Thereafter, ensure that all charges under SEF be confined to expenditures compliant with the guidelines set forth in Sections 272 and 100(c) of RA 7160, Section 7(b) of RA 10410 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. of 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has ceased the noted practice.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanism of the Municipality’s GAD Database to ensure an updated, operational and reliable database, which serve the GAD Focal Point System in attaining an evidence-based GAD planning and policy-making. The retroactive effects of the lack of data provided by the GAD Database may be theoretical in nature, but the prospective outcome of improving the database will most likely be positive, however difficult to measure; and

comply with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the establishment of a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system through the creation of GAD M&E Team, and the subsequent performance of its functions to evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Management ensures that data gathering and collating mechanisms are being subjected to improvements and upgrade.





The Management complies with the Memorandum Circular.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

•	submit other documents related to the said GAD-related Seminar/Workshop for a more thorough audit procedures, and for possible considerations;

•	limit the approval of the attendance to seminars/workshops that are held too far from the locality, especially for seminars that are not specialized; and


•	in cases of necessary, specialized and uncommon seminars/workshops, select capable individuals among the municipal personnel to represent the Municipality.  These officials/employees must be specifically tasked to gather information, thereafter, sharing it to other concerned individuals.
observations.managementAction:
The required documents were submitted by the Management, and were made available for audit and review.

Attendees to seminars/workshops had been limited by the Municipality.


The Management ensures that the selection process has been made stricter, so that only capable individuals will be made to attend.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management stop the practice of spending too much public funds on meals and snacks for entertainment or reception of staff and guests. Public funds must be used prudently at all times to ensure economy and propriety by limiting the allocation to P150.00 per meal for each individual.
observations.managementAction: The practice of spending too much on meals and snacks has been stopped altogether.

The average material allocation has been identified by the Management and is being implemented.




The Management conducts more rigorous post-qualification. 




The Management installed control mechanisms.




Expenditures are put under strict scrutiny as to efficiency and economy.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to devise a control mechanism that will secure compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act No. 11260, thus, ensuring employee productivity and satisfying their families’ basic needs. We also recommend that loans of employees from other financial institutions such as Land Bank of the Philippines will be reflected as deduction to their payroll in order to reflect the actual net take pay of the employees.
observations.managementAction: The Management installed control mechanisms. The net take home pay is being computed before granting of loan to employees.

observations.recommendation:
After the provision of information and fair warnings, it is the Building Official’s responsibilities to impose the corresponding fines and penalties for non-compliance. 

Also, in connection to these, the Local Chief Executive is also recommended to encourage the Municipal and Barangay Officials alike to coordinate with the Municipal Building Official, information about structures being built within their jurisdiction, primarily, in order to assure the safety and security of the community from possible effect of defective construction.

Additionally, we have recommended that the Municipal Assessor conduct frequent physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real properties within the province are properly listed in the assessment rolls and to exercise the functions of appraisal and assessment primarily for taxation purposes of all real properties in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Fines and penalties are still being subjected to confirmation. Fair warnings are being provided.

The Barangays have been told of their duties and responsibilities.





The procedures and strategies for the conduct of surveys are being planned and perfected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to properly prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the bank accounts maintained by the Municipality, and immediately prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books for the reconciling items.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statements were submitted but with undetermined reconciling items
observations.reason: Due to the reconciling items of the prior years

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant deducted some of the unliquidated cash advances from the claims of Terminal Leave Benefits of retired LGU employees.

Did not grant additional cash advance to employees with existing cash advances.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers are no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to establish the correct amount of receivables and draw adjusting entries to correct the balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020 RPT/SET receivable was set up.
observations.reason: No Adjusting Journal Entry was prepared to adjust the RPT/SET Receivable for the prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant-Designate, Municipal Treasurer, and all responsible officials to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, together with its supporting documents, and cancelled checks.  This is without prejudice to any notice of suspension, charges or disallowances that may be issued as a result of our audit.

Ensure that all disbursement vouchers, pertinent supporting documents and copies of the checks be submitted on a regular and timely basis.
observations.managementAction: The management submit some of the subject disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: There were still unsubmitted DVs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal              Accountant to verify the account to determine the correctness in the                   charging to the account.   

It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant to be wary in the recording of transactions so as not to                     misrepresent the accounts in the financial statements.
	
Also, the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant should 	coordinate to prevent the incurrence of expenditures beyond the approved budget.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had verified the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the required submission of contracts to the Audit Team within five (5) days from their   issuance or execution, for transactions to be reviewed as to legality and propriety prior to their payment. Moreover, continued refusal to adhere as required shall subject concerned officers to the legal consequences         provided for under the rule in case of non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: Some contracts were submitted within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The management failed to observe the timely submission of contracts to COA.

observations.managementAction: Purchases of supplies were directly recorded as expenses.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to prepare ledger cards and stock cards, physical inventory report, among others.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2020 the LGU was able to implement some of the recommend-dations.
observations.reason: In CY 2020 no transfer of unutilized LDRRM fund was made to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, which is chaired by the Local Chief Executive, should annually update the LGU’s MSWMP.

We also recommended that management should strictly observe the provisions of RA 9003, especially in complying with the minimum standards and requirements for the collection of solid waste, and the establishment of a Sanitary Landfill.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had submitted its MSWMP to DENR Regional Office for approval.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not established a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation:
Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with the supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month.

Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Section 122 of PD 1445 in relation to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two (2) months.
observations.managementAction: The submission of the accounts was delayed.
observations.reason: There was a delay in the submission of the accounts from the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly observe the requirements under COA Circular No. 92-389 in order to maintain a sound internal control in the utilization of government funds.  The perforation of these documents shall not be limited only to the Disbursement Voucher but should include all supporting documents to avoid their reuse. A stamp shall be made which shall provide a space for the number of the check issued and date of actual payment and said spaces should be properly filled up.
observations.managementAction: Some disbursement vouchers and their pertinent supporting documents were stamped “PAID”, but some were not.
observations.reason: This control was not strictly implemented by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1145
observations.managementAction: Reconciled and effected adjustment in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture Officer, Accountant and Treasurer do concerned effort in collecting this amount in order to safeguard government resource against wastage.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled the records of individual accounts. Sent notice of collection to loaners concerned.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with provision of Section 1, rule of the IRR of RA 9679 and to discontinue remitting HMDF employer share above the mandated amount to preclude audit disallowance
observations.managementAction: Discontinued the 2% of salary for employer share.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Award Committee and its Technical working Group live by heart the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR to preclude occurrence of same conditions in the ensuing year and of impediments that may arise due to non-adherence of the law.
observations.managementAction: Conducted procurement orientation on RA 9184 on August 13, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accounting Office take another effort to follow up its liquidation, otherwise require then to refund the amount drawn.
observations.managementAction: Prepared demand letters to be served to personnel concerned

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Create an Inventory Committee, who shall conduct an actual and complete physical inventory of all properties; • Instruct the GSO to prepare a report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and reconcile with the Municipal Accountant; • Instruct the GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of records between their two Offices to come up with an accurate and fairly presented reconciled balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.; and • Instruct the Municipal Accountant to debit the account Construction-in-Progress for payment under progress billing and credit this account upon completion of project. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned LGU officials to ensure that the road networks be properly recorded in the Registry and be transferred to the books of accounts within the target dates or period of four years which ends in CY 2019.
observations.managementAction: Only RPCPPE for Transportation Equipment for CY 2019 was submitted. No reconciliation was conducted. The GSO Designate declared that her Office had already started the conduct of actual inventory count on different offices of the LGU to update their PPE records. Likewise, the schedule of PPE from the Municipal Accountant was already received and a disposal committee was now created.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer- is also the GSO Designate, hence not given priority due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure adherence to the provisions of Section 93 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and limit payments for the purchases or other payment of claims through reimbursement to the amount not exceeding P15,000.00. Otherwise, payment checks should be made directly to the supplier’s net of taxes due the government.
observations.managementAction: Management assured us that they will implement the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Expenses totaling P14,505,031.49 were still made on reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the affected 2018 financial statements pertaining to the afore-mentioned observations for fair presentation and prepare necessary adjusting entries on the erroneous recording of collection and reversion of disallowances.
We also recommended that the Management establish cut-off date as part of their annual closing activity to ensure that all transactions are properly recognized in the correct period.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Immediately submit the correct lists or schedule of Accounts Payable and documentary requirements to support the Accounts Payable of the Municipality, and thereafter submit the contracts within the prescribed period; and
Make the necessary adjustments to the Financial Statements so that the correct balance of the Accounts Payable and the related asset and/or expenses accounts will be reflected.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the deposit slips and disbursement vouchers of the transactions presented as reconciling items for the current year and to exert effort in tracing the disbursement vouchers pertaining to prior year period.  And thereafter, to reconcile their records and make the necessary adjustments in their records as needed in order to reflect the timely and accurate position of the Municipality.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer trace the details on the long outstanding checks particularly as to its date and its whereabouts, and thereafter, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to make the proper adjustment on these cancelled checks.

Based on the foregoing observations we have, therefore, recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the subsidiary ledgers supplied with complete data as prescribed by pertinent provisions of MNGAS so that relevant information may be provided to internal and external users;
Direct the Municipal Treasurer or the GSO, as the case may be, to: (1) prepare the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 57 of MNGAS for LGUs and its relevant annexes, so that minimum data required will be supplied, and so that the functions and purposes of the RPCPPE will be attained; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the specific items of their records that were not yet reconciled on the initial reconciliation.

We have recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:
The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare necessary journal entries to correct the balances of Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts as well as the Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income in order to fairly present said accounts.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to further look into previous years’ files in order to account for the said receivables.  If after all the possible means were exerted and the claims could not be accounted, the Management could take necessary actions for the proper write-off/derecognition of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to fairly present the Inter-Agency Receivables.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to submit immediately the 32 contracts amounting to P36,855,914.16, including all documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation to facilitate timely conduct of contract review of the Auditor in order to immediately communicate deficiencies noted and for the management to address the weaknesses.  Likewise, submit the lacking documentary requirements as stated above.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to submit immediately the 32 contracts amounting to P36,855,914.16, including all documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation to facilitate timely conduct of contract review of the Auditor in order to immediately communicate deficiencies noted and for the management to address the weaknesses.  Likewise, submit the lacking documentary requirements as stated above.

We have recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in order attain government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process.

We have recommended that Management, through the Municipal Development Council ensure that development funds are utilized through proper prioritization and planning the PPAs to be implemented to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management for the benefit of the constituents.  The local finance committee shall likewise consider availing credit facilities without needing to allocate an enormous portion of their Internal Revenue Allotment to repay loans leaving a lesser portion for the delivery of essential public service.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to maintain Community-based Monitoring System to facilitate the LGU addressing appropriately and accurately the probable measures before, during and after calamities;
