require the MDRRMO to include in the propose LDRRMFIP which is to be approved by the MDRRMC the procurement of forklift, jack hammer, shoring and concrete cutter needed for disaster preparedness, response and recovery of the municipality.  Thereafter procure through the Bids and Awards Committee, the needed tools and equipment; and
capacitate the MDRRMO and his staff through seminars like preparation of Business Continuity Plan, Hazardous Materials Management, Management of the Dead and the Missing, Local Climate Change Action Plan Formulation, Pre-disaster Recovery Planning and Rapid Damage Assessment and Needs Analysis.

We have recommended that the Local School Board cease the practice of budgeting and charging expenditures related to training of its teachers, Alternative Learning System and prizes under ECCD programs against Special Education Fund.  Thereafter, ensure that all charges under SEF be confined to expenditures compliant with the guidelines set forth in Sections 272 and 100(c) of RA 7160, Section 7(b) of RA 10410 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. of 2017.

We have, therefore, recommended that Management:
strengthen the data gathering and collating mechanism of the Municipality’s GAD Database to ensure an updated, operational and reliable database, which serve the GAD Focal Point System in attaining an evidence-based GAD planning and policy-making. The retroactive effects of the lack of data provided by the GAD Database may be theoretical in nature, but the prospective outcome of improving the database will most likely be positive, however difficult to measure; and
comply with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the establishment of a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system through the creation of GAD M&E Team, and the subsequent performance of its functions to evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects.

Based on the foregoing observations, we have, therefore, recommended that the Management:
submit other documents related to the said GAD-related Seminar/Workshop for a more thorough audit procedures, and for possible considerations;
limit the approval of the attendance to seminars/workshops that are held too far from the locality, especially for seminars that are not specialized; and
in cases of necessary, specialized and uncommon seminars/workshops, select capable individuals among the municipal personnel to represent the Municipality.  These officials/employees must be specifically tasked to gather information, thereafter, sharing it to other concerned individuals.

We have recommended that Management stop the practice of spending too much public funds on meals and snacks for entertainment or reception of staff and guests. Public funds must be used prudently at all times to ensure economy and propriety by limiting the allocation to P150.00 per meal for each individual.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the concerned officials to devise a control mechanism that will secure compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act No. 11260, thus, ensuring employee productivity and satisfying their families’ basic needs. We also recommend that loans of employees from other financial institutions such as Land Bank of the Philippines will be reflected as deduction to their payroll in order to reflect the actual net take pay of the employees.

Based on the foregoing observations, we have, therefore, recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in the preparation and maintenance of the accounting records, provide the minimum data prescribed by the MNGAS so that these may be processed into relevant information by end-users.

Also, in connection to these, the Local Chief Executive is also recommended to encourage the Municipal and Barangay Officials alike to coordinate with the Municipal Building Official, information about structures being built within their jurisdiction, primarily, in order to assure the safety and security of the community from possible effect of defective construction.
Additionally, we have recommended that the Municipal Assessor conduct frequent physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real properties within the province are properly listed in the assessment rolls and to exercise the functions of appraisal and assessment primarily for taxation purposes of all real properties in the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality exercise its authority to update the existing Market and Slaughterhouse Code in conformity with the provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991, otherwise known as RA 7160 in order to generate additional income to finance programs and projects beneficial to the constituents.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to see to it that all the required supporting documents be attached to the disbursement vouchers before payment is made to avoid disallowance or suspension in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. General Services Officer-Designate (GSO) together with the Inventory Committee to
conduct a complete physical count of all the PPEs of the Municipality, work on the
necessary disposal of unserviceable properties, exert efforts toward reconciliation
with the Accounting Office and submit the RPCPPE in the prescribed format to the
Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts regarding the over depreciation charged to Other Machinery and
Equipment account and properly classify the Textbooks and Instructional Materials
for Distribution under the inventory accounts of the Municipality.

Considering that PPE accounts signifies 49% of the total assets of the Municipality, we
also recommended to the Municipal Mayor the filling up of a permanent official on the
Office of the General Services who will assume the duties and responsibilities of the
said Office and who will address the perennial issues relative to the supplies and
property management of the Municipality.

We strongly reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare and submit the year-end BRS for all bank accounts within the
prescribed period to the Audit Team for review and verification to establish the
correct balances of the bank accounts of the Municipality with its depository
banks and to detect immediately fraud/irregularities in the use of cash
resources, if any;
b. make a detailed verification and effect the necessary corrections on the
discrepancies in the bank accounts appearing in the books with those of the
Treasury and bank records; and
c. adjust immediately all reconciling items, either in the books of the Municipality
or the depository banks, which should be properly notified, to avoid
accumulation thereof.
• Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the
stale checks for replacement, if applicable, and comply with the procedures in the
handling of stale/spoiled checks as stated in Section 59 of the NGAS Manual
Volume III.
• Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to work rigidly and immediately
determine the cause/s of the difference between their records and reconcile them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property ledger cards
for Local Road Networks and disclose the information for the said property in the
Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above cited regulations;
b. General Services Office to prepare and maintain road networks property cards and
to submit Report on Local Road Network;
c. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory count of all the Municipality’s road
network systems and accordingly submit a Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System as year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to verify and tally its records pertaining to the noted
(₱1,883,500.00) variance between its General Ledger and Schedule of
Depreciation.
• Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer-Designate to:
a. locate documents pertaining to the donated three motor vehicles and take up its
acquisition cost on the books of accounts for computation of depreciation;
b. cause the immediate disposal of the 17 unserviceable/junked vehicles through
proper disposal procedures of government property pursuant to COA Circular
No. 92-386;
c. locate the supporting documents of the remaining 14 units of vehicles out of the
34 unrecorded items in the books of the Municipal Government and accordingly
record the same in the books of accounts if these vehicles are in existence and
being used in the operation of the Municipal Government;
d. verify the 64 units of transportation equipment being computed with depreciation
by the Municipal Accountant which are not found on the Report on the Physical
Count of the GSO through supporting the same with procurement documents and
acknowledgement receipt for equipment to establish their identity for reconciliation
with the records of the GSO;
e. establish the description of items totaling ₱1,009,000.00 for addition/disclosure of
such to the appropriate motor vehicle unit; and
f. provide complete and definite details regarding each item being logged on each
record to facilitate ease of reconciliation.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Assessor to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the updated and duly
verified assessment roll of the Municipality’s taxable properties;
b. Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation of the duly certified list showing
the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each of them at the start of the year
and cause submission to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording in the
books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and the equivalent Deferred Income
accounts;
c. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the current year
and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property
taxes and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues on time. Conduct verification
on those outstanding balances to determine the existence and completeness of the
receivables and its collectability; and
d. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPT, SET Receivables and their
reciprocal account and total delinquency tax as prepared by the Municipal
Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal GSO-Designate to:
a. number each type of supplies and maintain Stock Cards per stock number;
b. forward copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) to the Municipal
Accountant for the preparation of the DV and recording of deliveries in the
appropriate ledger cards;
c. utilize and consolidate weekly the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for which
supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant together with the
original copies of RIS; and
d. use the prescribed format in reporting the physical count of inventories and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of
each year in coordination with the Inventory Committee.
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. maintain Supplies Ledger Cards per stock number and record the purchases in
the appropriate ledger cards upon receipt of the AIR and other supporting
documents;
b. use the appropriate inventory account in recording the purchases and
corresponding expense account upon receipt of SSMI submitted by the GSO; and
c. use the moving average method in costing the inventories.

Similar with that of the PPE, we repeated our recommendation with the Municipal Mayor
with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan that the Budget Officer appropriate
funds in the ensuing year for the filling up of the General Services Officer, together
46
with its staff to have permanent officials who will assume the duties and
responsibilities of the Office, and to address in totality the recurrent issues relative to
the supply and property management of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant to:
a. reconcile its records on receivables/payables in the books of the Municipality for
Intra-agency accounts;
b. continue the coordination with the Provincial Accounting Office until such time that
the accounts be reconciled. If claims are fully documented and establishes
regularity of transactions, then settlements/collections should be made by the
Municipality. For those undocumented collectibles, where after all diligent efforts
that had been exhausted by the Municipal Accountant still disclosed zero basis,
then proper procedures leading to write-off of these accounts should be
considered; and
c. seek approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of undocumented
payables to surplus accounts after conferring with the Provincial Accountant
showed the absence of any documents to prove validity of claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make
a detailed review of the accounts with abnormal balances, determine the cause/s of the
same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to
present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in
recording, analyzing and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to reflect
the correct balances of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Municipal employee,
Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit the Approved plans/drawing, ABC and the list of tools or equipment to be
provided by the Municipality to the Audit Team to avoid being suspended in audit;
b. clearly state the total estimated cost of the projects on the Invitation to Pakyaw
Group and accordingly submit the fully filled out invitation for the payments audited
or justification why such portion was left without any amount;
c. forward to the Audit Team the aforementioned Annex B of the Pakyaw Agreement
and the documents required to be submitted to the Municipal Engineer per Section 5
of the said agreement;
d. provide justification on the noted variances on the duration of the implementation of
50
the projects audited and ensure that succeeding reports will depict uniform number of
days; and
e. provide explanation/justification on the lapses noted on the attached quotation of
Adven P. San Miguel for the supply of labor for the concreting of brgy. roads at
Brgy. Maalas-as to Mavalor, Maalas-as to Bulihan, Mabato, Natu and construction
of covered canal of Brgy. Putingkahoy.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer-
Designate to:
a. comply with the regulations that procurement of ordinary and regular office
supplies and equipment involving amounts below the threshold should first be
procured from DBM. Shopping from private suppliers should be resorted to, only
for items not available in the PS-DBM. Thus, in case shopping is resorted to, the
claim should be supported with a Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks from the
PS-DBM; and
b. prudently and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter as consolidated and
incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in order to adopt
competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best quality at
the lowest price is availed.

Like on the issues of PPE’s, we persistently recommended to the Municipal Mayor
and Sanggunian Bayan to concur the filling up of a permanent official and staff on the
Office of the General Services who will assume the duties and responsibilities of the
Office and who will address the perennial/recurring issues relative to the property and
supplies management of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly comply with the provisions of Joint
Circular No. 2015-01 of the COA, DBM, DILG, GCG, and DND on the use of Confidential
Funds. He should require the:
• concerned Municipal officials in charge of the Confidential Fund and the Municipal
Accountant to submit the original cash advance disbursement voucher to the Audit
Team complete with details as to the date, serial number, date signed and the
required certification on face of the DV as stated above.
• concerned Municipal officials in charge of the Confidential Fund to:
a. ensure that subsequent cash advances for CF be fully liquidated at the end of the
year;
b. safeguard the submission of the cash advance voucher together with its complete
attachments to the Audit Team within the period prescribed;
c. submit the amended Physical and Financial Plan for CY 2018 ample in details;
d. submit AIP for CY 2018 showing the allocation/budget for POP of the LGU and
54
guarantee the attachment of such on the next cash advance voucher for CF;
e. request the concerned PNP Chief in the locality to highlight in concrete details the
circumstances which required the urgency of allocating CF; and
f. to immediately forward to the Audit Team the transmittal letter duly received by
ICFAU for proper monitoring of the same.
• OIC, Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. itemized the POP of the LGU for CY 2018 then submit the same to the Audit Team
and ensure that subsequent reports to be forwarded will be such detailed; and
b. make certain that obligation requests are duly numbered and dated.
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. make the necessary adjustment regarding the Confidential Expense account to
properly present the correct balance of the said account at year-end;
b. use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer instead of Due from
Officers and Employees account when accounting for cash advance for CF; and
c. ensure that JEVs are properly numbered and dated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to
a. to review the completeness of supporting documents before funds are
transferred /released to the barangays (Implementing Agency) Require the
submission by the Barangay Treasurer of all the documentary requirements
aforementioned initially from the Memorandum of Agreement down to Copy of
56
the OR issued by the IA to the Source Agency acknowledging receipt of funds
transferred; and
b. record fund transfers in the books of accounts as receivable from the
concerned barangays subject to liquidation to ensure that funds are used only
for the intended purpose. Make the necessary correcting entries “Due from
LGUs (1-03 -03-030)”, instead of Donation and or Due to NGAs and
subsequently credit this account upon submission of the liquidation papers by
the concerned barangay for a fair presentation of accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare individual financial statements with complete subsidiary ledgers for each
economic enterprise on Public Market, Slaughterhouse and Cemetery being operated
by the Municipality and as well as its 20% Development Fund which should form part
of the schedules of the General Fund in accordance with the above cited regulations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Head of each Office/Department to formulate its respective Project Procurement
Management Plans (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities and projects
(PPAs) and submit the same to the Budget Office for evaluation to ensure
consistency with the Municipality’s budget proposal;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to forward the PPMPs included in the budget proposal to
the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat for consolidation into APP, and
to the BAC for final recommendation of the appropriate procurement modality
wherein budget proposal/appropriations should be itemized per projects; and
c. BAC with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat to prepare the Annual Procurement
Plan, duly supported by the Project Procurement Management Plan, (PPMP) of each
department/office and the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as required per
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Development Council to plan and identify specifically the projects to be
included in the Annual Investment Program;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to avoid/refrain preparation of budgets for projects in
lumpsum appropriation. For projects with lumpsum appropriation, secure the
approval of the Sangguniang Bayan before obligation and implementation; and
c. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Development Coordinator to monitor closely the
implementation of projects and ensure maximum utilization of the funds available
through 100% completion and accomplishments of program, projects and activities
due the public.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly adhere with the provisions of Joint
Circular No. 1, series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and
Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government in order to ensure that
Special Education Fund will be utilized for the intended purpose or according to the
identified priority areas taken from the DepEd-approved School Improvement Plan (SIP)
and DEDP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• LDRRM Officer:
a. to ensure that the list of projects and activities which are to be charged to the
unexpended LDRRMF under the Trust Fund books, is included in the Annual
LDRRMF Investment Plan to be prepared yearly; and
b. to make certain the maximum utilization of current Mitigation Funds, continuing
appropriations and unexpended balance in the Trust Fund and accordingly
coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator for the
implementation of planned programs/projects of the LDRRM Office.
• Municipal Accountant, LDRRM Officer and Budget Officer to effect the recognition
and transfer of cash from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books of the
unutilized QRF and MF-MOOE for CYs 2018 and 2019, in the total amount of
₱12,708,523.52.

We appreciate the persistent efforts of the Municipality to support gender equality and
women empowerment, however to address the aforementioned findings, we
recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive Committee
together with the Gender Focal Point System and the Technical Working Group to:
a. exert active efforts in accomplishing all the targeted and planned client-focused
and organization-focused PPAs;
b. secure first the Certificate of Endorsement of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget from
the DILG Provincial Office before incorporating to the Municipality’s Annual Budget
the planned PPAs to properly ascertain the relevance of the gender issues identified
70
and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB;
c. ensure the proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all
departments/offices of the Municipality; and
d. coordinate with the Municipal Human Resources Management Officer to require all
personnel to undergo training on gender sensitivity, human rights and gender
responsiveness especially those holding positions involving protection and
defense of women against gender-based violence, counselling and trauma healing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to exert more effort to
reconcile the difference of P18,003,705.54 between the accounting records of PPE and
RPCPPE;
b) the General Services Office to provide details of the property totaling
P7,786,476.50 taken up in the RPCPPE in lump sum;
c) the General Services Officer to submit documents relating to the donated and
disposed PPEs to the Accounting Office for recording/dropping from the books; and
d) the General Services Offices to undertake proper disposal of unserviceable
property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the non-current portion of the Loans Payable-Domestic to Non-Current
Liabilities category in the Statement of Financial Position to ensure fair presentation
of liabilities of the Municipality as at year-end. It is further recommended that he
require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to
reconcile the recorded outstanding balance of the loan with the creditor bank’s
records and henceforth, to ensure that documentary stamps and insurance expenses
on succeeding loan releases are properly recognized in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Office to:
a) submit the complete RIS and SSMI pertaining to the issuance of 500 sets of
hygiene kits to the Audit Team and thereafter submit the same to the Office of the Municipal
Accountant for proper recording in the books; and
b) conduct a complete physical count of all inventories and submit the corresponding
Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Office of the Auditor within the
period prescribed under Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the guidelines provided for
under COA Circular No. 2014-002 by recording the procurement of all
supplies/materials for disaster response and rescue programs and activities
under the appropriate Inventory account and to recognize them only as expense
upon issuance to beneficiaries or end-users and upon submission of RIS and other
proof of distribution of goods;
b) the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the pertinent resolution declaring the
Municipality or the Province, as the case maybe, under state of calamity to
support the use of the QRF to procure rice costing P3,700,000.00, otherwise the
amount will be suspended in audit; and
c) the MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the MDRRMF
Monthly Utilization Report duly reconciled with the accounting records and
certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the MSB strictly adhere with the guidelines provided under
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. of 2017, particularly on the formulation/
preparation of SEF Budget Preparation Form No.1 and the three-year program
indicating strategic prioritization policies in the allocation of SEF and preparation
and submission of the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization to the
concerned offices.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all concerned officials and
personnel adhere strictly to Section 93 of PD No. 1445 which requires government
agencies to make direct payments to suppliers and ensure that the provisions of RA
9184 on the appropriate method of procurement and BIR Revenue Regulation 2-98 on
withholding of taxes are not circumvented.

1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the reciprocal
accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and
present reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No.
1445.

2.7 We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture Officer, Accountant and
Treasurer do concerted effort in collecting this amount in order to
safeguard government resources against wastage.

3.5 We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provision of
Section 1, Rule VI of the IRR of RA 9679 and to discontinue remitting
HMDF employer share above the mandated amount to preclude audit
disallowance.

4.6 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and its Technical
Working Group live by heart the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR to
preclude occurrence of same conditions in the ensuing year and of
impediments that may arise due to non-adherence of the law.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipal Treasury Office exert additional
effort in collecting delinquent realty taxes to generate more income that
could be used to finance other developmental project.

6.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office take another
effort to follow up its liquidation, otherwise require them to refund the
amount drawn.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the updated and duly verified assessment roll of the Municipality’s taxable properties; b. Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each of them at the start of the year and cause submission to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables and the equivalent Deferred Income accounts; c. Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the current year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payers to pay their dues on time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the existence and completeness of the receivables and its collectability; and d. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of RPT, SET Receivables and their reciprocal account and total delinquency tax as prepared by the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer will take the observation with utmost importance and coordinate with Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor to reconcile differences. The Municipal Assessor stated her Office is in continuous coordination and will provide cooperation. Municipal Accountant committed to find reconciling items and present reconciled RPT and SET Receivable and Deferred accounts.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous transaction and lack of personnel at the Accounting Office, reconciliation could not be completed. Some differences still remain and reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit year-end BRS for all bank accounts within the prescribed period; make a detailed verification and effect the necessary corrections on the discrepancies in the bank accounts appearing in the books with those of the Treasury and bank records; and adjust immediately all reconciling items, either in the books of the Municipality or the depository banks. Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks and comply with handling procedures.
observations.managementAction: Several reconciling JEVs were made and some reconciling items were booked-up by the Municipal Accountant. Municipal Treasurer accounted for ₱6,414,409.50 difference in Trust Fund books. The Municipal Accountant is still reconciling bank records.
observations.reason: There are still unreconciled items due to voluminous transactions and ongoing reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to provide the Municipal Treasurer with updated assessment roll; Municipal Treasurer to facilitate preparation of certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and taxes due at start of year and submit to Municipal Accountant; Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment and intensify collection of delinquent taxes; and Municipal Accountant to reconcile balances of RPT/SET receivables and their reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer coordinated and committed to reconcile records; Municipal Accountant to reconcile and find reconciling items.
observations.reason: Discrepancy remained; detailed reconciliation ongoing and some corrections already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned contractors to immediately rectify the deficiencies and submit pictures as proof of correction; Inspectorate Team to conduct regular and strict monitoring and supervision to ensure that all contractors undertake the regular monitoring and supervision of projects; LDRRM Officer to refrain from charging against 20% LDRRM Fund programs related to construction of evacuation centers without basic amenities since charging thereof could not be justified; and require concerned officials to submit the required supporting documents to the Audit Team for validation.
observations.managementAction: Local Government officials and Inspectorate Team were instructed for regular monitoring. Some projects were already rectified by contractors and pictures submitted to Audit Team. Municipal Engineer instructed Inspectorate Team for regular monitoring and supervision. Some documents on CY 2019 projects were not provided as of report.
observations.reason: There were still infrastructure projects where proof of rectification was not provided. Verification of documents is still ongoing and some lacking supporting documents remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop granting financial assistance for private purposes and comply strictly with provisions of RA No. 7160; establish clear policies and guidelines on the grant of financial assistance including qualifications, amounts, frequency, mode of payment, documentation requirements; and require proper internal controls for grant processing.
observations.managementAction: Municipal officials acknowledged the finding. No fully established system yet. The Municipal Government is to consider the recommendations and is gradually moving to comply. For CY 2019, PRAISE was not granted.
observations.reason: No specific policies were submitted; the municipality is gradually complying and preparing guidelines but not yet finalized. Some grants/incentives remain subject to policy formation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the focal person to strictly adhere to the set guidelines of the SIA including full compliance on days of implementation and refund of unused funds; submit justifications for deviations and refund unused funds to DSWD; ensure proper liquidation and submission of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-MSWDO submitted documents showing that the purchase of NFA rice worth ₱376,237.00 was distributed to beneficiaries and consumed. The focal person explained program was for 65 days instead of 120 days and that purchased goods were consumed; MSWDO submitted reports.
observations.reason: Some documentation and refunds were handled; the number of days and budget sufficiency issues arose because program implementation was only 65 days. Some matters were resolved but others require further justification and documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended periodic review by concerned officials to ascertain attainment of collection targets, analyze reasons for shortfalls and adopt measures to avoid future misses in projections; careful planning and realistic revenue collection targets be set.
observations.managementAction: Municipal officials undertook measures and better collection efforts; total actual collections for CY 2019 exceeded estimates. The Municipality implemented collection strategies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended MSWDO prepare an updated master list indicating economic status of each senior citizen; conduct quarterly validation and monitoring of beneficiaries to ensure entitlement and propriety of utilization; and ensure all eligible senior citizens are dully selected per DSWD guidelines.
observations.managementAction: MSWDO submitted a Master List of citizens and started distribution of monthly social pension. National government (DSWD R-IV A) assisted in distribution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Human Resource/Payroll and Accounting Offices reconcile with GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG; identify causes of over/under remittance and remit amounts due; institute procedures to avoid incurrence of penalties; and check monthly payroll deductions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal HRMO coordinated with GSIS and identified causes; some overremittances were due to employees renewing loans but not informing payroll; Municipal Accountant and HRMO reconciled and remitted amounts per Section 14.2. Management held meetings with concerned agencies.
observations.reason: Timing differences and personnel issues caused delays; some reconciliation and remittances completed but some items still outstanding and under reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Treasurer and Accountant discontinue allowing deductions that reduce net take-home pay below ₱5,000 and comply with GAA provision; ensure deductions for loans do not exceed limits; and review payroll practices.
observations.managementAction: Municipality revised payroll practices and now carefully evaluates status of net pay before authorizing deductions. Some exceptions noted and being addressed.
observations.reason: The municipality is evaluating and implementing changes; some exceptions remain and monitoring continues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to regularly conduct physical count; prepare report and reconcile with accounting; determine existing/missing/unserviceable properties; prepare registry for Road Networks and ensure transfer to books within the period; Municipal Accountant to be cautious in preparation of financial statements and see that supporting schedules are tallied with amounts; and to debit Construction-in-Progress upon payment under progress billing and credit upon completion.
observations.managementAction: Only partial actions undertaken; RPCPPE for Transportation Equipment for CY 2019 was reported. No full reconciliation conducted due to voluminous transactions and lack of personnel. Registry for Road Networks is being maintained and some items taken up in accounts.
observations.reason: Unavailability of personnel and voluminous documents prevented full reconciliation and completion of required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconcile bank and book records regularly; adjust reconciling items with supporting documents; prepare JEVs to record valid reconciling items; coordinate with depository banks for adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation is being made; several JEVs were booked. Some reconciling items remain and reconciliation is ongoing. The Municipal Accountant continues coordination with Treasurer.
observations.reason: Voluminous transactions and lack of personnel resulted in unresolved reconciling items; process is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor and concerned officials: (a) ensure procurements follow RA 9184 and avoid using cash advance as procurement alternative; (b) ensure cash advances are liquidated within prescribed time, demand letters be issued, withhold salaries of accountable officers if not liquidated; (c) refrain from spending for non-urgent or personal expenses and ensure proper liquidation and justification; (d) accept liquidation only when fully supported; (e) draw JEVs for recording adjustments; and (f) require Municipal Agriculturist to justify transfers of biological assets.
observations.managementAction: Some improvements made: some cash advances liquidated or withheld; demand letters issued in some cases; JEVs prepared for some adjustments; some refunds made (e.g., refund of ₱238,106.51 for double payment). However, some unliquidated cash advances remained as of year end and supporting documents for some claims remain incomplete.
observations.reason: Some issues resolved; others remain due to incomplete documentation, delayed demand letters and continued exceptions in certain disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Treasurer furnish certified list of taxpayers and amounts to Municipal Accountant at start of year and coordinate with Assessor to reconcile records; Municipal Accountant to analyze unreconciled entries and effect adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer and Assessor are coordinating and Municipal Accountant will reconcile records. Some adjustments were made but discrepancy remains and reconciliation ongoing.
observations.reason: Discrepancy persisted due to voluminous transactions; reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Treasurer design effective tax strategies to intensify collections for current and delinquent taxes, enforce tax remedies under Local Government Code and coordinate with Provincial Government for collections and enforcement.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer intensified tax collection efforts and coordinated with Provincial Government; delinquent taxes reduced compared to prior year though still significant.
observations.reason: Additional efforts needed; delinquent taxes remain albeit reduced; continuous collection strategies ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant ascertain collectability of receivables; pursue legal action where necessary; prepare schedules and supporting documents; submit disclosure of nature and status of receivables in Notes to Financial Statements; and submit request for write-off to COA when collectability is nil with supporting Sanggunian resolution.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant initiated evaluation of receivables and started preparation of schedules. Some documents were booked-up under Other Receivables. No full legal action yet.
observations.reason: Evaluation of dormant receivables is ongoing; some supporting documents lacking for old receivables and legal actions not yet pursued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Accountant reconcile records with Provincial Accountant and furnish copies of JEVs as basis for recording collections; continue coordination until accounts reconciled; if claims are undocumented consider write-off with Sanggunian approval.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant coordinated with Provincial Accounting Office; meetings held and some reconciliations performed though discrepancies remained.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is ongoing; differences still being resolved and some documentary proofs missing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Development Council to plan and itemize specific projects in AIP; Municipal Budget Officer to avoid lump-sum appropriations and secure Sanggunian approval for itemized projects; ensure procurement compliance with RA 9184; require submission of supporting documents for contracts; ensure expenditures charged to 20% DF are related to development investment and part of prioritized projects.
observations.managementAction: MPDC and Budget Officer committed to include itemized AIP and budgets in succeeding year; some procurement lapses were addressed; supplemental AIP prepared for a small portion but large unutilized balances remained. Municipal Engineer and BAC instructed to provide supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some improvements made; however many projects were still in generic terms and large unutilized balance existed. Procurement supporting documents for some CY 2017 projects were not submitted; remedial actions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended LDRRMO prepare annual LDRRMFIP including list of projects charged to unexpended balances; ensure maximum utilization of current Mitigation Funds, continuing appropriations and unexpended Trust Fund balances; Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to effect recognition and transfer of unutilized QRF and MF-MOOE to Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMO prepared LDRRMFIP but it did not include list of projects to be charged to unexpended Trust Fund balances. Municipal Accountant effected transfer of some balances in March 2020 but over-transfer occurred and adjustments were to be made. LDRRMO committed to maximize utilization and coordinate with MPDC.
observations.reason: LDRRMFIP lacked listing of projects for Trust Fund balances; transfers were effected later than year-end; some unutilized funds remained and implementation of planned PPAs was insufficient.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor as chairperson of Executive Committee together with GFPS and TWG to: (a) exert efforts to accomplish planned PPAs; (b) secure DILG endorsement of GPB prior to incorporation into Annual Budget; (c) ensure mainstreaming of GAD-related activities in all departments; and (d) require all personnel to undergo GST and other gender-related capacity development.
observations.managementAction: GFPS Secretariat acknowledged shortcomings and committed to implement recommendations; GPB was submitted but endorsement was deferred due to timelines; some GAD activities implemented though many partially implemented and attribution not used.
observations.reason: Voluminous workload and timing issues prevented full implementation and timely endorsement; many GAD PPAs were only partially implemented and GAD mainstreaming incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor provide sufficient storeroom/office building for safekeeping and accommodation of increasing volume of documents and ensure preservation period of ten years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: Municipality acknowledged the need and committed to allocate budget and find space for storage; however as of report space remained insufficient.
observations.reason: Unavailability of adequate storeroom/space; budget and planning for storage building required and still in process.

observations.recommendation:
the Municipal Accountant and the General Services were still on the going.
The GSO had submitted RPCPPE in RCPPE in prescribed format prescribed in Section 7 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II and to prepare the same inventory properly and not as mere subsidiary records of PPE accounts;
 the GSO provide details of the property totaling Implemented
