The Municipal Treasurer send demand letter to those delinquent stall owners so that additional funds could be used to finance projects, programs that would benefit its constituents.

We have further recommended that the receivables be taken up in the books of account of the municipality so as not to understate the asset and income accounts.

We have recommended to management to see to it that all the required supporting documents be attached to the disbursement vouchers before payment be made to avoid disallowance or suspension in the future.  We further recommended to management enforce the immediate settlement of all disallowances by the person’s held liable under the notices of disallowances.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer continue to find ways and means to address the cash deficit by exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.

the Municipal Accountant maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each Inventory items to record the receipts and issuances of inventory items, the Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all inventory items and prepare and submit the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council identify and incorporate, under a separate caption, in the LDRRMFIP the programs and activities to be charged out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the management and utilization of the said Fund.
We also recommended that the Municipal Government stop circumventing the guidelines on proper management and utilization of the LDRRMF funds in order to fully achieve the purposes for which the funds were allocated.

Municipal Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit the following documents:
Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts for the preceding calendar year or quarter; and
Certified photocopy of Income Tax Returns (ITR) to be submitted on or before April 30 of each year to determine if a deficiency in business tax collected exists arising out of the difference in gross sales or receipts as declared in the Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts and in the ITR.
Municipal Treasurer or his duly authorized representative/s to examine the books of accounts and other pertinent records of the business establishments doing business within the municipality to ascertain, assess and collect the true and correct amount of the tax due from the taxpayer concerned.
Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipal Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year.
Municipal Treasurer to verify/assess the gross sales or receipts of the contractors/suppliers and validate the noted unassessed/uncollected amount of ₽214,367.79 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the Municipality’s Revenue Code. Absence of documented action or information to counter the uncollected may result in the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator to clearly define the works or activities which could only be accomplished through hiring of consultants, include them in the contracts and made them the bases to justify the need for consultancy services.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately to the Auditor concerned the five disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents involving a total amount of P71,625.00.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen the control over releasing of checks to the claimants.  Releasing of checks should be done inside the Treasurer’s Office as much as possible, payments to service providers and other establishments should be done by a designated employee and disbursement vouchers brought out of the Treasurer’s Office should be returned immediately.

We recommended that the approving authority submit written justification for allowing the grant of assistance in excess of the maximum amount provided in the guidelines.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the submission of medical certificate or clinical abstract for medical assistance granted which were not duly supported with these documents.
Henceforth, we recommended that management adhere to the established guidelines and policies on the granting of assistance/donations to its constituents for consistency and uniformity, see to it that all claims are duly supported with the required documents to prove its validity, necessity and propriety of the amount granted, and stop the grant of assistance when there are no more funds available.

We recommended that the Municipal Government evaluate the operations of the Rosario Water System and determine its viability. They should consider increasing the water rates being charged to concessionaires to a level that will commensurate the cost of water sold and the operating expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Administrator require the submission of Accomplishment Reports from the Job Order employees and Evaluation Report from the Department Heads of the offices where the JOs are assigned prior to the renewal of contract to assess the necessity of their rehiring.
We also recommended that the Municipal Government evaluate the contribution of the JO employees to the overall accomplishment of the Municipality and if found to be dispensable, limit the hiring to what is prudent and necessary in the exigencies of the service.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statement together with its supporting documents in the prescribed method and format in order to prove the correctness of the balances reported in the financial statements and to comply with Section 74 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and National Guidelines on Internal Control System circularized in DBM Letter No. 2008-8 dated October 23, 2008.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to closely monitor and enforce the liquidation of travel cash advances through issuance of demand letters to concerned officials and employees. Otherwise, withhold payment of any money due them.

To address the deficiency, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation to all Property, Plant and Equipment. In compliance with COA Circular No. 2017-004 dated December 13, 2017, the formula to compute for the depreciation is as follows:
The cost of Property, Plant and Equipment less the estimated residual value which is usually 5% of the cost of the asset, then divide the balance by the estimated useful life which is to be determined based on the judgment of the management pursuant to COA Circular No. 2017-004 dated December 13, 2017.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unused balance of the disaster fund in the General Fund to a special Trust Fund.  Moreover, he should instruct concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan and Report of Sources and Utilization of the fund in accordance with the prescribed format.

We recommend that management should workout to have an approved Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to the provision of Section 17 of RA 9003. Likewise, we recommend that management strictly enforce the other relevant provisions of the said law to attain systematic and comprehensive solid waste management for the protection of public health and the environment. Moreover, optimally utilized the fund to help achieve desirable results in the implementation of programs relative to solid waste management.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to instruct the:
GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit a copy of Annual GAD Plan and Budget together with its GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with Annex D and Annex E respectively of the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 2016; and
See to it that disbursement relevant to GAD Fund are legitimate and in accordance with the approved Annual GAD budget.

We recommend that the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) be established in compliance with the mandate of Republic Act 9344 to oversee the implementation of the programs to address the welfare and interest of children as provided in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
We recommend that proper documentation, sound management and fiscal administration should be observed in order to attain efficiency in the use of government funds.

Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with the supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month.
Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Section 122 of PD 1445 in relation to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two (2) months.

We recommend that Management properly observe the requirements under COA Circular No. 92-389.  The perforation of these documents shall not be limited only to the Disbursement Voucher but should include all supporting documents to avoid their reuse. A stamp shall be made which shall provide a space for the number of the check issued and date of actual payment and said spaces should be properly filled up.
We recommended that Management direct all officers and employees to strictly adhere with rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended for management to make the appropriate action to preclude the existence of the same condition in the future. Proper monitoring of remittance of collections should be made by the immediate supervisor/liquidating officer.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to record the withheld amounts to its appropriate accounts and remit such amounts directly to the concerned institutions to refrain from creating confusion in the records or books.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and the concerned employees to submit the lacking documents as specified in the attached schedule and for the Budget Officer to be conscious in the use of appropriation exclusively for its specific purpose to avoid incurrence of same audit suspensions and possible disallowances, if not supported with appropriate documentation, in the future.

We recommended that management comply strictly with the provisions of Sec. 21.1 of RA 9184 as to the proper posting of Invitation to Bid in the PhilGEPS to preclude the incurrence of possible negative consequence due to non-compliance of the law.
We recommended that the LGU appropriate funds, of what is required by law, for the strengthening and implementation of programs of the LCPC.
We recommended that management follow the procedural steps in the budget preparation and eventual authorization to preclude the existence of the same condition in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary correcting entries. The 20% of IRA should be recognized as income in the books of the 20% DF. Receipt of the 20% of the IRA in the GF Proper, prior to actual transfers to 20% DF, should be recorded as Due to Other Funds and correspondingly recognized Due from Other Funds in the books of the 20% DF.

We recommended to the Local School Board to include only expenditure items which are feasible for funding under SEF and which are needs of the schools and learning centers so the purpose of the creation of the fund will be achieved.
Likewise, please submit the following documents to enable us to make a decision in audit: School Improvement Plan (SIP) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDP)

We recommended that management direct the Local Finance Committee to monitor and evaluate the physical and financial implementation of the projects so programmed projects will be implemented within the scheduled time frame and for the constituents to enjoy the beneficial use of it.
Further, that management direct the contractor/supplier to refund the excess payments of the above mentioned projects with over claims as well as the service provider of the claim for Php24,000.00 since the project was under contract by a certain construction firm.

We recommended that management give preferential attention to this concern by prioritizing the budget allocation for the final waste disposal/processing facility to prevent the incurrence of possible health hazards the existing dumpsite might cause to the constituents.

We recommended that management undertake extensive planning related to disaster risk reduction and management to preclude the existence of same conditions mentioned above and to avoid unfavorable observation in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated General Services
Officer to:

e regularly conduct the physical count of Property, Plant and
Equipment, prepare a report thereon showing the details of the
equipment and property and reconcile with the Municipal Accountant
on the recorded amount in the financial statements in order to arrive
with reliable inventory report; and

e determine those property and equipments which are physically
existing, missing and unserviceable for proper identification and
appropriate action as required by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to be cautious in the preparation of financial statements and see
to it that all supporting schedules be tallied with the amount shown therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant debit the account
Construction-in-Progress for payment under progress billing and credit this
account upon completion of project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Concerned LGU
officials to ensure that the road networks be properly recorded in the Registry and
be transferred to the books of accounts within the target dates or period of four
years which ends in CY 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

* Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
to reconcile their records on a regular basis;

e Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry
Voucher (JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment in the books of
the Municipality in order to reflect the correct and reliable
balances of the Cash accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to give immediate attention and adjust in the books the reconciling
items that are duly supported with valid/complete documents, and coordinate
with the authorized depository bank regarding the bank reconciling items that
are need to be adjusted in the bank books in conformity with Section 3.3 of COA
Circular No. 96-002 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

- Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the
certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due
and collectibles for the year as provided in Section 20, Volume I of
the NGAS Manual in order to ensure reliability of the balances of Real
Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts; and

- Municipal Accountant to analyze and record the adjusting entries
pertaining to the unreconciled RPT Receivables and Deferred RPT
Income accounts and take up the corrections of the differences
noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to:

o design effective tax strategies to intensify collection of Real Property
Taxes, not only current taxes but also the delinquent taxes in order to
generate more income; and

o enforce the tax remedies provided for under Book 1I of the Local
Government Code in the collection of taxes due to the government.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all concerned municipal
officials and employees to institute legal actions against responsible persons in the
deposit of municipal funds to private banks.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statements the nature and status of unrecovered deposits which was
booked up under Other Receivables account to present fairly the Municipality’s
financial position.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
ascertain the collectibility of these receivables and if found that collection is nil a
request for write off can be forwarded to the Commission on Audit which must be
supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution approving the write off and other
documents evidencing that efforts were exerted by the Municipality for its recovery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

o prepare schedules for the aforementioned accounts to be supported in the
financial statements and see to it that all recorded receivables be
substantiated with documents in order present a more reliable accounts
balances; and

o properly disclose the nature and status of these receivables in the Notes to
Financial Statements to inform its users on the actual condition of these
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records with
the Provincial Accountant and settle discrepancies between the balances of
reciprocal accounts so that the Municipality will be able to present the correct
balances of inter-agency receivables and payables in the financial statements.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should furnish the Provincial Accountant
with the copies of the JEVs showing the amount due to LGU as the basis for
recording the collections made by the Municipality in the books of the Province and
if adjustment/correction was made in the province regarding the collections made by the
Municipality. Also, the Provincial Accountant should furnish the Municipal
Accountant with copies of their JEVs as the basis of the latter to draw the JEV
taking up the income of the Province.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a) direct Municipal Planning and Development Officer and/or the Municipal
Engineer to optimally utilize the 20% Local Development Fund in order to
deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental
outcomes to the constituents which is essential in the attainment of the
constitutional objective;

b) require the Municipal Development Council to design a feasible and
workable Annual Investment Plan towards the socio-economic development
and environmental protection of the Municipality and ensure
accomplishment of the programs, projects and activities which are on-going
and yet to be implemented so as not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of
the immediate benefits that could be derived therefrom; and

¢) submit justification/explanation on the delay/non-implementation of the
priority development projects listed in the CY 2017 20% Development Plan
in spite of the availability of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Development Council to clearly specify the detailed descriptions, locations and
cost of priority programs, projects and activities funded out of 20 percent
Development Fund and ensure that such projects partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures. For projects that are presented in generic
terms and with lump-sum appropriation, secure the approval of the
Sangguniang Bayan before the implementation of each project and
disbursements of the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant to avoid the charging of expenses to the
20% Development Fund (DF) which are not related to and do not partake the
nature of investment/capital expenditures and do not contribute to the
attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes and strictly adhere to the guidelines set under DILG-
DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in the appropriation
and utilization of the 20% DF to ensure its optimal utilization.

We further recommended the Municipality’s adherence to the rules and
regulations set forth in R.A. No. 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform
Act and to its Revised IRR so as to promote transparency, accountability,
equity, efficiency and economy in the procurement process to achieve good
governance and to refrain/stop the processing of claims without complete
documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned department
heads to submit the required supporting documents to COA for audit purposes:
and direct the concerned departments/personnel to submit justification for the
noted tampering of supporting documents. Tampering is highly discouraged and
should be discontinued.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that succeeding transactions be properly supported with
complete documentation in order to establish the validity of claims and
processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented be
discontinued.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

* concerned officials to submit the lacking eligibility documents to the Audit
Team for further evaluation and ensure completeness of the bidding
documents to establish the propriety of the bidding process and as basis for
the next audit action and to check, verify and validate first all the eligibility
documents submitted by the bidders before awarding contracts to bonafide
contractors. Otherwise, enforce strictly the sanctions against the erring
contractors, BAC/TWG and other concerned officials for lapses committed
on ensuing procurements;

o the BAC to strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of Section 13 of the
IRR of R.A. 9184 in inviting observers in all stages of the procurement
process and indicate in the letter invitation with clarity the procurement
stages to be attended by the observers such as opening of bids, bid
evaluation, pre-bid conference, etc.;

¢) the BAC to observe the provisions of the IRR of R.A. 9184 in the posting of
Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract in the agency
website and PhilGEPS and require the BAC Secretariat to issue certification
on the posting of aforementioned documents in any conspicuous places in
the premises of the Procuring entity; and

66

d) the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to refrain/stop the
processing of claims without complete documentation.

e) the contractors to post the Warranty Securities of the completed
infrastructure projects to assure for the protection/guarantee against possible
structural defects/failures that may occur during the applicable warranty
period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a) the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere
strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009 on the prompt submission of the copies of perfected
contracts and their supporting documents within five (5) working days
after execution/issuance thereof, for timely auditorial and/or technical
review/ evaluation thereof of the contracts and for the Municipality to
be informed early of any deficiencies noted in audit and provide the
Audit Team with the copies of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)
as basis for the proper validation of the implementation of the
programs;

b) the BAC, BAC Secretariat and Technical Working Group to explain in
writing, the reason for pushing thru with the proceeding/bidding
72

despite “ineligibility” of the bidder and the concerned officials to
submit the lacking eligibility documents to the Audit Team for further
evaluation and ensure completeness of the bidding documents to
establish the propriety of the bidding process;

¢) the BAC to strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of Section 13
of the IRR of R.A. 9184 in inviting observers in all stages of the
procurement process and indicate in the letter invitation with clarity
the procurement stages to be attended by the observers such as
opening of bids, bid evaluation, pre-bid conference, etc.;

d) the BAC to observe the provisions of the IRR of R.A. 9184 in the
posting of Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved
contract in the agency website and PhilGEPS and require the BAC
Secretariat to issue certification on the posting of aforementioned
documents in any conspicuous places in the premises of the Procuring
Entity;

¢) the Municipal Accountant to deduct the ten percent (10%) retention
money from each progress payment for infrastructure projects up to
fifty percent (50%) completion of the works, if the project is within
schedule otherwise, it should be continuously imposed. Moreover, the
total retention money shall be due for release only upon final
acceptance of the project or upon the request of the contractor to
substitute the same with an irrevocable standby letter of credit or
surety bond callable on demand or other acceptable documents
prescribed in Section 6.2, Annex E of Republic Act No. 9184; and

f) the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to conduct final inspection
of infrastructure projects, one (1) year from its completion and
require submission of warranty security in accordance with Section
62.2.3 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the
financial statements in accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA
Circular No. 2016-004 to assists users in understanding the financial statements and
to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledger for each
special account and to submit the trial balances and financial statements pursuant
to Sections 105 and 110 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to inform users of
the result of the operations as well as financial condition for decision making
purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of
the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for
the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those
for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of
ten (10) years as mandated by law.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and the management agreed to reconcile their records on Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as required under the above-mentioned regulation in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The reconciliation between the records of GSO and Accounting is on-going.
observations.reason: The lack of manpower in the office of the Municipal Accountant caused the delay in the implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to insure all properties owned by the municipality to the GSIS as required under the above-cited regulation to enable the municipality to recover the cost of properties in case of loss or damage.
observations.managementAction: The management is in the process of completing the documents required by the GSIS so as to avail of the insurance program provided by the GSIS.
observations.reason: The Office of the MPDC is still locating some of the records.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the preparation and submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements as required under the above-cited rules and regulations inorder to establish the correct balance of Cash-in-Bank account.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant was able to prepare the bank reconciliation statements as of cash exam date.
observations.reason: Preparation of bank reconciliation statements were delayed due to late delivery of bank statements and also due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to appoint a Municipal Assessor as required under the above-mentioned regulation to ensure the smooth delivery of services to the constituents considering that the Offices of the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor are essential in the operation of the local government.
observations.managementAction: The OIC Municipal is completing the required work-year experience to be able to be appointed as Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and he agreed that the Municipal Accountant, being the one certifying to the completeness of documents in the disbursement voucher to see to it that all claims are accompanied with the required supporting documents before a disbursement is charged against the government funds to prove the validity of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: The Office of Municipal Accountant is doing its best to comply with this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, we required management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Management is making use of the ACRAS as their guide in the documentation of their procurement.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 13.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001.  In addition, we required management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity of the procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: Management is making use of the ACRAS as their guide in the documentation of their procurement.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Finding No. 13.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to formulate plans and strategies to enhance collection of its delinquent real property taxes.  We further recommended that the Municipality consider sending demand letters to delinquent taxpayers to remind of their obligations and avail of the administrative and legal remedies provided by law to enforce collection of delinquent taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is conducting tax campaign.
observations.reason: Some property owner could not be located.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and the management agreed to comply with the above-mentioned provisions so that the Audit team will be able to conduct timely evaluation/review of items purchased and any defects/deficiencies noted thereon will be immediately communicated to the management.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the submission of Copies of Contract, Purchase Orders and their supporting document, however still not within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower, thus the delay.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the officials concerned to observe the provisions cited above on the prescribed date of submission of documents for audit and evaluation of the transaction of the agency.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Accountant is trying its best to meet the prescribed period in rendering the accounts of the municipality.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower in the accounting department.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management strictly comply with the submission of accounts and reports as required under the above-cited rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Accountant is trying its best to meet the prescribed period in the submission of the financial reports of the municipality.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower in the accounting department.

observations.managementAction:
The Accounting and General Services Offices continue to reconcile their records.





Inventory taking was already conducted in 2017 and the corresponding RPCPPE submitted.






The GSO coordinated with the Accounting Office to determine the details of PPE.


Disposed motor vehicles in 2017 were already droppedfrom the books. 




Preparation of PPE Ledger Card had been stated by the Accounting Office.

The GSO started preparing the Property Card.
observations.reason: Lack of adequate records pertaining to acquisition of PPE prior to 2004 hinders the full implementation of the recommendations.

observations.managementAction:
Initial actions had been undertaken by the GSO by following up the registration of motor vehicles with the LTO.





IIRUP had been prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.





Disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles had been undertaken by the Municipality.


Proper maintenance of motor vehicles was undertaken by the Municipality


Registered vehicles were covered with insurance.
observations.reason: Inadequate records pertaining to acquisition of the vehicles caused the delay in the registration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments in the Accumulated Depreciation of the depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality and in Government Equity.  Henceforth, he should use the revised residual value which is 5% of the cost, in line with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation of PPE had been adjusted to conform with the PPSAS standards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measures:

The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officers maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, to record the receipts and issuances of inventory items;

The General Services Officer accomplish the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for the issuances of inventory items and consolidate the same in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI).  The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS should be submitted to the Chief Accountant for the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances; and

The General Services Officer conduct the periodic physical count of the inventory items and prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO prepared and maintained stock cards starting 2017.  


RIS and SSMI were already prepared by the GSO.





Inventories stored in the GSO Office were physically counted and reported in the RPCI.
observations.reason:
Voluminous workload in the Accounting Office hindered the preparation and maintenance of Supply Ledger Cards. 

Only the inventories stored in the GSO was physically counted and reported at year-end of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all collections for the Share of the Municipality from Economic Zones are turned over daily by the Collectors to the Liquidating Officer deposited intact the government authorized depository bank.
observations.managementAction: Collections for the share of the Municipality from Economic Zones were remitted promptly by the Collector to the Liquidating Officer starting February of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer find ways and means to address the cash deficit the municipality had been experiencing for several years now by exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Actions had been taken to bring down the cash deficit toP62,622,714.40 as of year end of 2017.
observations.reason: The extent of the cash deficit from previous years had a material impact in the operations of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to allocate funds out of the regular collections in the General Fund for the transfer of the unexpended balances LDRRMF from Calendar Years 2012 to 2016amounting to P29,316,748.19 to the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The previous years’ balances of LDRRMF had been transferred to the Trust Fund

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the guidelines formulated by the Municipality on the availment of various social services and donations be submitted to the Sanguniang Bayan for its ratification.

We also recommended that the approving authority and recommending approval submit written justification for allowing the grant of assistance in excess of the maximum amount provided in the guidelines and those grants for financial assistance which are not supported with letter request and case study from the Municipal Social Worker and Development Office. 

Further we recommended that management adhere to the established guidelines and policies on the granting of assistance to its constituents for consistency and uniformity, see to it that all claims are duly supported with the required documents to prove its validity and the necessity of the request, and stop the grant of assistance when there is no more funds available.
observations.managementAction:
Guidelines had been ratified by the Sanguniang Bayan. 



The Municipality had justified the granting of assistance in excess of the maximum amount provided in the guidelines in certain instances.
observations.reason: Strict compliance to the formulated guidelines was not strictly adhered to by management.

observations.managementAction:
Campaign programs were conducted by the Municipality in schools to promote awareness among the students. 


Schools were commissioned to be the MRF of the Barangays.
observations.reason:
Strict implementation of the policy is not yet enforced due to lack of intensive information campaign in the barangay level.

Intensive information campaign of the Ordinance in the barangay level was not yet undertaken by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the that the concerned department heads of the Municipality, as end-users of the procuring entity, be required to prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to serves as a guide document in the procurement and contract implementation process, as well as a vital reference in monitoring procurement, and to facilitate the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

We also recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, based on the PPMP, prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in the prescribed form, indicating therein among others, the mode of procurement and the schedule for each procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had prepared the PPMP and the APP in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials adhere to the cited provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to enable the Audit Team to undertake prompt validation of the projects and inform management of any deficiencies noted in audit for appropriate remedial measures.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office submitted the reports on government projects regularly in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office to furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to facilitate the prompt review and evaluation of the contracts and the early communication to management on the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts for infrastructure projects were already being submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross sales/ receipts (not only from the Municipal Government of Rosario) for the preceding Calendar Year and other pertinent records/ information for the determination of gross receipts.

Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipal Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year.

Municipal Treasurer to verify/assess the gross receipts of the contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling ₽140,865.18,and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the Municipality’s Revenue Code. Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.

Municipal Treasurer to properly classify and bill taxpayers their business tax in accordance with Section 2A.02of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

Bids and Awards Committee examine the eligibility documents submitted by the bidders and determine if the latter had paid the correct business taxes taking into consideration the payments for transactions made with the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Submission off sworn statements were now being required by the BPLO. 




The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Municipal Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractor/ suppliers for the year 2017. 



Additional taxes had been collected by the Treasurer’s Office from suppliers/ contractors.  




Proper classification and billing of taxpayers were undertaken. 

BAC had examined the documents submitted by the bidders.
observations.reason: There were payments inadvertently not included in the list submitted to the Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the grant of additional cash advances to officials and employees with previous balances and demand for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances through the issuance of demand letters copy furnished the Audit Team. In case of non-compliance, instruct the Accounting Office to withhold the salaries or any money due them as provided in Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002. In addition, require the Municipal Accountant the immediate submission of payrolls and liquidation of travels.
observations.managementAction: Management accepted the deficiency and will try to deduct from the employee’s salaries their outstanding balances and will request for writeoff from COA to those employees who are no longer connected to the LGU
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant still grants cash advances to those who have outstanding balances.  Some employees are no longer connected to the LGU.

See related discussion in Finding No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere with the pertinent provisions of COA Circulars No. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the proper documentation of transactions. Likewise, the Accountant should rectify the deficiencies noted on the variance per LDRRMF utilization report and per audit and to submit the disbursement vouchers/liquidation reports and the lacking supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Management vowed to comply with the audit team’s recommendation
observations.reason:
. | Certain deficiencies were still noted in the LGU’s management of the LDRRM Fund. 


See related discussion in Finding No. 5 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management publish all their procurements in the PhilGEPS website. Generally, public bidding should be used for all procurement  and all alternative modes of procurement be resorted to only if the conditions which allows the use of such alternative method were met pursuant to the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184. Moreover, stop the practice of procurement thru reimbursements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already posts their procurements in the PhilGEPS website.
observations.reason: Management still fails to comply with certain provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board actively participate in the gradual implementation of the approved 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to protect the constituents and the environment against health and environmental hazards brought about by improper solid waste disposal.
observations.managementAction: Management is exerting efforts on the gradual implementation of its 10-year SWM Plan.
observations.reason:
The LGU is still waiting for the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan by the National Office.

See related discussion in Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the basic documentary requirements to support the payment of wages to job order personnel. The Accountant should see to it that all claims should be supported with complete supporting documents before payments are made.  We further recommend that management stop the charging of wages for garbage collectors, office staffs/aides and street sweepers to 5% LDRRMF and 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management will gather the necessary supporting documents needed by the audit team.
observations.reason:
Vouching of documents revealed that no contracts were attached to the paid DVs for payment of wages to job order employees.


See related discussion in Finding No. 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant establish/set up Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables based on the assessed value of real property as required under Sections 233(a) and 235 of RA 7160. Likewise, require the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Accountant copies of certified list of taxpayers and the corresponding assessed values of the taxable real property units to facilitate the setting up of receivables at the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20, Volume 1, of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management admits its failure to set-up RPT/SET receivable at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: Establishment of the said receivables at the beginning of the year for CY 2017, was made only to the SET (SEF Fund). No RPT receivables was established under the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to exert earnest efforts to intensify collections in order to generate more income for the Municipality. Likewise, look into the cause of the decrease of income in order to improve operations.
observations.managementAction: To revise Municipal Revenue Code; to hire additional collection personnel; and to impose more collection strategies on the tax collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should (1) demand for the immediate settlement of outstanding advances; (2) strictly adhere with the governing laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and (3) to refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers unless his/her previous cash advance is liquidated or a proper accounting thereof has already been made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management already liquidated the cash advances but not the full amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Accountant to control the recording of appropriate account codes based on actual nature of services rendered in conformity with the provisions of the PPSAS to present fairly subject expenditures in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management already acted on the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with Section 34 of the IRR-A of RA 9184 so bona fide bidder could be awarded the contract.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management implemented to some of the suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the withheld amount so as not to deprive the government officials and employees of the benefits and privileges due them. The Municipal Treasurer must see to it that remittances of the contributions and withheld premiums and loans to the GSIS and PAG-IBIG shall be given priority over and above the payment of any and all obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees. Also, the Municipal Accountant should check the attached supporting documents and ensure that original copies are submitted to COA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management already acted on the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly scrutinize the transactions and require valid and legal complete documents to support the claims pursuant to Sec. 4(6) 0f P.D 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management submitted some of the vouchers with complete documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize those programs, projects and activities stipulated in RA 7160 and refrain from charging expenditures not chargeable against the fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management already acted on the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to direct the Municipal Planning Officer to supervise more closely and to monitor the project accomplishments by following time frame and target date so as not to deprive end users of its beneficial use.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management already implemented some of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the conversion of the semi controlled dumpsite to a sanitary landfill as the final disposal facility of the LGU’s solid waste in compliance to RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | The management already appropriated fund for the implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the  Local Chief Executive: a. Create an Inventory Committee who will expedite the conduct of the physical count of properties of the government including acquired and donated. After which, the Committee should come up with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) by type which shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit; b. Require all department/office heads to conduct Physical count of all the properties in their respective department/office immediately based on the signed MRs and AREs on file at their office and to submit RPCPPE and TIRUP, as applicable, to the General Services Office for consolidation and likewise, to prepare separate report for loss of government property to the Committee copy furnished the Auditor’s Office. c. Enforce the provision of Section 122 (2) of PD 1445 against concerned officials who continuously failed to submit the required reports necessary in the decision making by the end-users of the reports.
observations.managementAction: An Inventory Committee who will expedite the conduct of the physical count of properties of the government including acquired and donated. After which, the Committee should come up with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) by type which shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit; b. Require all department/office heads to conduct Physical count of all the properties in their respective department/office immediately based on the signed MRs and AREs on file at their office and to submit RPCPPE and TIRUP, as applicable, to the General Services Office for consolidation and likewise, to prepare separate report for loss of government property to the Committee copy furnished the Auditor’s Office. c. Enforce the provision of Section 122 (2) of PD 1445 against concerned officials who continuously failed to submit the required reports necessary in the decision making by the end-users of the reports.
observations.reason: The inventory report was just lately done by the present GSO  wherein the  previous records where not  properly submitted. However, the were noted findings as mentioned in Part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the designated General Services Officer to initiate immediately the processing of the insurance policies of the various property of the Municipality, to ensure indemnification or compensation by the Municipality for any damage to, or loss of, its properties due to fire, earthquake, storm or other casualty. Likewise, we recommended that the concerned municipal officials furnish the General Services Office copy of the latest inventory report with complete list of all insurable properties for indorsement to the GSIS so that the appraisal thereof and the computation of the amount of insurance premiums payable by the Municipality could be readily  made. Henceforth, strict compliance with R.A. No. 656 should be observed to ensure that the Municipality will be compensated or indemnified for property damages or losses caused by fire, earthquake and other covered calamities.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Report was accomplished  but without the complete details as mentioned in the PPE finding specially the values which can be the basis of the insured amount.
observations.reason: Several under  the  Inter-Agency Payables and assets accounts show abnormal  balances, thus the accuracy and reliability  of  the  financial  statements could not be ascertained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality secure the necessary documentary requirements as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to legitimize ownership on the land purchased and that proper titling of the above-mentioned lots be facilitated to avoid exposure of the same to third party claim. We also advised to take extra care in entering into contract in order to protect the interest of the municipal government. Further, the Municipal Accountant stop processing the claims if the documents are incomplete.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had submitted only one from all the lacking  necessary documents to legitimate ownership on the land purchased and for the proper titling of the said lot. However, it must noted that the said lot is already transferred in the name  of  the Municipality of Rosario per tax declaration no. 19-0008-01286.
observations.reason: No reason offered by the municipal officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply strictly with COA  Circular No. 2013-004 on the information and publicity of all on-going government projects / programs / activities to be implemented during the year, and the posting of project signboards/public notices within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity.
observations.managementAction: The BAC were submitting to the Auditor’s  Office the Monthly/Quarterly Report of Publicized projects but not the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities that are to be implemented during the year at the beginning of the year and the information on the posting of project signboards/public notices within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity.
observations.reason: No reason given by the Office of the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the LDRRM Officer to meticulously plan the programs and projects and ensure that programs and projects under the Mitigation Fund be implemented immediately to achieve intended purposes; b. submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month so that timely review and evaluation in terms of compliance with the requirements could be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The said long outstanding were not yet reverted to unappropriated surplus since the account had not been removed yet in the financial statements. The Municipality had submitted the utilization report for CY 2017 but not in the right format.
observations.reason: The municipality submitted the report but not in the right format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the focal person to implement immediately the required activities on Supplementary Feeding Program based on the set timeline in order to improve and sustain the nutritional status of the target children beneficiaries and ultimately contribute to the success of the program as a whole.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality implemented the BUB projects as of December 31; 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality implement immediately the program/projects under BUB and comply with DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 6 for BUB implementation and ensure required documents are provided.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had implemented BUB projects as of December 31, 2017. However, there were still lacking required documents in their disbursement.
observations.reason: For 2017, the Municipality had implemented BUB projects out of the Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities Fund. There were still lacking required documents in their disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the payment of annual dues to various organizations not in conformity with RA 7160 for lack of legal basis; and instruct the Municipal Treasurer that payments made to duly recognized leagues under the law be acknowledged with the prescribed government Accountable Form No. 51 or official receipts duly registered with the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had stopped the payment of annual dues to various organizations not in conformity with RA 7160. For the payments made to duly recognized leagues under the law, the management assured that it was acknowledged with the prescribed government Accountable Form No. 51 or official receipts duly registered with the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledger for each special account and to submit the trial balances and financial statements pursuant to Sections 105 and 110 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to inform users of the result of the operations as well as financial condition for decision making purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant said that they are currently updating their records to provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledger for each special account and to submit the trial balances and financial statements pursuant to Sections 105 and 110 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to inform users of the result of the operations as well as financial condition for decision making purposes.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to further review and analyze the details of those payables. Reconcile the unremitted amount and immediately remit the same to the concerned agencies. Adjusting entries should be made where appropriate to reflect accurate balances of the accounts in the books as well as in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still undergoing the review and analyzation of the old balances which are not easily identified. Also, old balances due to BIR.
observations.reason: These are old balances which cannot be easily identified. Also, manpower constraint.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the long outstanding payables be reverted to Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to RA No. 3526 as implemented by EO No. 109, 5.1993. Also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately remit the collections due to national government agencies to augment their resources for programmed projects and to avoid possible misuse of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The said long outstanding were not yet reverted to unappropriated surplus since the account had not been removed yet in the financial statements. The Municipal Treasurer had done the partial remittance of the said collections totaling unremitted P871,584.60 on July 31, 2018.
observations.reason: These are old balances which were not reverted yet. The Municipal Treasurer remitted partial amounts and needs further action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant said that they started their reconciliation of these reciprocal accounts on 2017. But it was not reconciled yet.
observations.reason: Lack of time and manpower.

observations.managementAction: Follow-up on the lacking documents as soon as possible to be able to apply for the insurance of the buildings.
observations.reason: Reiterated Implemented under No. 3

observations.managementAction: Designate a particular driver for particular vehicle. Further, require the driver his daily trip ticket before issuance of gas slip/gas allowance.
observations.reason: Reiterated Implemented under No. 8
Reiterated Observation No. 10

observations.managementAction: Waiting for e-NGAS roll out. Further, the Municipal Accountant is requesting for additional manpower, if unavailable, requesting for overtime if possible.
observations.reason: Implemented under Observation No. 16

observations.recommendation: We again recommended    that the Municipal Mayor|create a team to study    and   re-evaluate the     facts and incidents of   the case for the          appropriate actions.  In |case all actions have    been taken to recover     the deposit and still     remain futile, authority   for the possible         write-off of the said     receivable accounts from|the Philippine Deposits  Insurance Corporation  should be requested from|the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: This finding was|reiterated in   Part II of this |report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the   Municipal Agriculturist |and other concerned LGU  officials properly monitor the|LGU’s dispersal program  and submit the report on the|initial dispersal of the  breeding stocks indicating the|name and address of the |recipients, description,   quantity, unit cost and    total amount of the     breeding animals received.|Also prepare and submit  semi-annually the inventory|and status of these breeding|animals to the Auditor    concerned and Accounting |Department for proper     recognition in the books|of any increase or        decrease on the quantity   and value of these assets.
observations.reason: The concerned   official has not|submitted the   required  reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal  Engineer to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of copies of contracts and their supporting documents forming part thereof to the Auditor, to facilitate the prompt review and evaluation of the contracts and the early communication to management on the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.reason: Management gave no reason for their non- compliance to the audit recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct physical inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon duly categorized by type of the property and equipment. We also recommend that the required property cards and PPE Ledger Cards be maintained and the accounting unit to provide depreciation for all depreciable assets, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 111 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Not Acted Upon
observations.reason: More time is needed in reconciling the reports of actual inventory and book records, it failed to conduct a complete inventory at the end of the year. Likewise, the incoming Municipal Accountant assumed office early 3rd quarter of 2017 and was unable to update his records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management immediately implement the projects financed by the 20% development fund, limit the use of such fund for purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and optimally utilize the fund to achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: Those programs/ projects/ activities that were not implemented in CY 2016 were treated as continuing appropriations and will be implemented in 2017.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Finding No. 2 of this report. | As cited during the exit conference, those projects/ programs/ activities that were not in conformity with the guidelines set forth under DILG-DBM Joint Circular were charged to 20% DF due to insufficient funds of the MOOE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local government unit strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121 dated May 27, 2010 and DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the use of the 5% LDRRMF. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspensions/Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management vowed to comply with the audit team’s recommendation
observations.reason: See related discussion in Finding No. 3 of this report. | Certain deficiencies were still noted in the LGU’s management of the LDRRM Fund especially on disbursements not related to the intended purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the procurement processes as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and submit the lacking procurement documents. Likewise, be advised to submit to the audit team copies of all infrastructure contracts/purchase orders within five (5) days after their perfection as required in COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials promised to comply with the audit team’s recommen-dation .
observations.reason: See related discussion in Finding No. 4 of this report. | Management still fails to comply with certain provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting entries on the net understatement totaling ₱65,757.84 and to submit the required supporting documents. Moreover, require the Budget Officer to strictly incur obligation within the budgeted appropriation
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials promised to comply with the audit team’s recommen-dation .
observations.reason: Required documentation was not yet submitted. No adjusting entries were made by the Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management is exerting efforts to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Finding No. 8 of this report. | As cited during the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant explained that it was due to the delay on the transmittal of paid vouchers together with the supporting documents in the accounting office that caused the  delay of submission to the auditor. Likewise, he also wanted the accounts be complete before submitting them to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare and submit to the auditor the AAPSI within 60 days from receipt of the audit report copy furnished the agencies concerned pursuant to the provisions of Section 93 of RA 10651 to ensure that all recommendations are implemented and to provide enough time for the auditor to validate the same.
observations.managementAction: Not Acted Upon
observations.reason: No AAPSI for CY 2015 was submitted as of this date.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant establish/set up Real Property Tax (RPT) and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables based on the assessed value of real property as required under Sections 233(a) and 235 of RA 7160. Likewise, require the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Accountant copies of certified list of taxpayers and the corresponding assessed values of the taxable real property units to facilitate the setting up of receivables at the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20, Volume 1, of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management admits its failure to set-up RPT/SET receivable at the beginning of the year.
observations.reason: Management reasoned out that no RPT/SET was established/ set-up for CY 2015 because of the substantial amount of receivable balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officials be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase order within five (5) days after their execution/issuance and copies of delivery documents within 24 hours from acceptance of the delivered goods and services as provided for in COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-02. This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management accepted the lapses and deficiency on submission of copies of approved procurement contract for infrastructure projects, goods and services.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Finding No. 5 of this report. | Copies of approved procurement contract for infrastructure projects, goods and services were only submitted together with the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to insure all insurable properties of the LGU with the GSIS in compliance with Republic Act No.656 and Presidential Decree 245 to indemnify losses that may arise from fire, theft and other causes, and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include the funding requirement of the same in its annual budget.
observations.managementAction: Management already requested from GSIS for appraisal of the insurable properties of the LGU.
observations.reason: The LGU is still waiting for the action to be undertaken by the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management secure approval from DOF to maintain depository account with Philippine National Bank, otherwise affected depository account may have to be closed pursuant DOF Department Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005.
observations.managementAction: The LGU still maintains the same depository bank for its SEF accounts.
observations.reason: Management still maintains the Philippine National Bank-Catarman Branch as depository bank of the SEF accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to instruct the employee or official concerned to strictly adhere to the due dates on submission of documents and reports to avoid delays. The failure on their part shall be considered a ground for the automatic suspension of payment of his/her salary until he/she shall have complied with the aforesaid requirements without prejudice to any disciplinary action that may be instituted against him (Sec. 122, P.D. 1445).
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to fully implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all collectors shall prepare the RAAF to facilitate review by the Municipal Treasurer as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issuances and ending balances on hand.  In turn, the Municipal Treasurer should prepare the CRAAF which should be submitted to the Auditor for review and verification every month
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to fully implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit to the GPPB the procurement monitoring report within the time frame stipulated in Section 12.2 of the Amended IRR-A of RA 9184 so that monitoring and evaluation of the LGU’s compliance to the provisions of the Procurement Act can be made.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to fully implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management to coordinate with the Local School Board to prepare and submit Local School Board Plan as basis for utilizing the SEF and to prioritize those programs, projects and activities stipulated in RA 7160.
Utilities, Office and Medicine supplies for District use and travel of School Heads as well as the teachers will be charged to their respective appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The Management failed to fully implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and the management
agreed to reconcile their records on Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) as
required under the above-mentioned regulation in order to present fairly the
financial statements of the municipality.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the
Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to take up the transfer of said
property to the book of the barangay inorder to present fairly the financial
statements of the municipality. Consequently, require the Barangay Record
Keeper to take up said transfer to the book of accounts of the barangay.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to insure all
properties owned by the municipality to the GSIS as required under the above-
cited regulation to enable the municipality to recover the cost of properties in
case of loss or damage.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to comply with the preparation and submission of the
Bank Reconciliation Statements as required under the above-cited rules and
regulations inorder to establish the correct balance of Cash-in-Bank account.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and he agreed that
attendance to seminar and trainings should be enjoyed only by regular or
permanent employees and staff in doing so the municipality will be benefited
from such seminars and trainings. We further recommended that the BAC
appoint a member of TWG who is not on a Job Order Contract to avoid the
wastage of government resources and for the BAC appoint a member of TWG
who can handle the different aspects of the procurement, namely, the technical,
financial and legal aspects so that the bids submitted will be rigorously
evaluated.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and they agreed to
comply with the documentary requirements on the release of cash assistance
under the AICS Program of the DSWD as required under the above-mentioned
regulation to prove the propriety and validity of the transactions. We further
recommended that the amount of cash assistance to be given to indigents be
based on the Social Workers assessment of the case of the client.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to appoint a
Municipal Assessor as required under the above-mentioned regulation to ensure
the smooth delivery of services to the constituents considering that the Offices of
the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor are essential in the operation of
the local government.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to refrain from
granting honoraria to observers to ensure transparency in the bidding process.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and he agreed that
the Municipal Accountant, being the one certifying to the completeness of
documents in the disbursement voucher to see to it that all claims are
accompanied with the required supporting documents before a disbursement is
charged against the government funds to prove the validity of the transaction.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to comply with the regulations cited above so that the
implementation of projects/programs and activities appropriated under SEF will
not be hampered/delayed.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC
Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to
comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001. In addition, we required
management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity
of the procurement of infrastructure projects.

As of this writing, some lacking documents were already submitted by the
municipality.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the BAC
Members, the BAC Secretariat, the TWG, and the Municipal Accountant to
comply with the above-cited provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations and COA Circular No. 2012-001. In addition, we required
management to submit the deficiencies noted to ensure the legality and validity
of the procurement of goods.

As of this writing, some lacking documents were already submitted by the
municipality.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to formulate plans
and strategies to enhance collection of its delinquent real property taxes. We
further recommended that the Municipality consider sending demand letters to
