delinquent taxpayers to remind of their obligations and avail of the administrative
and legal remedies provided by law to enforce collection of delinquent taxpayers.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Budget
Officer to comply with the guidelines on the utilization of 20% Development
Fund as provided under Department of Budget and Management-Department of
Interior and Local Government Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated
April 13, 2011.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive and the management
agreed to comply with the above-mentioned provisions so that the Audit team
will be able to conduct timely evaluation/review of items purchased and any
defects/deficiencies noted thereon will be immediately communicated to the
management.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the
officials concerned to observe the provisions cited above on the prescribed date
of submission of documents for audit and evaluation of the transaction of the
agency.

We have recommended that the management strictly comply with the
submission of accounts and reports as required under the above-cited rules and
regulations.

Create an Inventory Committee who will expedite the conduct of the physical count  of the properties of the municipal government  including acquired and donated.  After which, the Committee should come up with the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) by type to be submitted to the Municipal  Mayor’s office copy furnished the  Auditor’s office.
Require all department/office heads  to conduct Physical count of all the properties in their respective department/office immediately based on the signed MRs and AREs on file at their office  and to submit RPCPPE and IIRUP, as applicable,  to the General Services Office for consolidation and likewise, to prepare separate report for loss of government property to the Committee copy furnished the   Auditor’s Office.
Enforce the provision of Section 122 (2) of PD 1445 against concerned officials who continuously failed to submit the required reports necessary in the decision making by the end-users of the reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instructs the designated General Services Officer to initiate immediately the processing of the insurance policies of the various property of the Municipality, to ensure indemnification or compensation by the Municipality for any damage to, or loss of, its properties due to fire, earthquake, storm or other casualty.
Likewise, we recommended that the concerned municipal officials furnish the General Services Office copy of the latest inventory report with complete list of all insurable properties for indorsement to the GSIS  so that the appraisal thereof and the computation of the amount of insurance premiums payable by the Municipality could be readily made.  Henceforth, strict compliance with R.A. No. 656 should be observed to ensure that the Municipality will be compensated or indemnified for property damages or losses caused by fire, earthquake and other covered calamities.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant initiate a thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause/s of the abnormal balances and make proper adjustments/corrections. Moreover, ensure that all accounts are in their normal balances and fairly presented in the resulting financial statements.
Further, the Municipal Accountant must exercise due diligent and care in the performance of her duties and responsibilities to ensure the non-recurrence of the abnormal balances.

We recommended that the Municipality secure the necessary documentary requirements as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to legitimize ownership on the land purchased and that proper titling of the above-mentioned lots be facilitated to avoid exposure of the same to third party claim.  We also advised to take extra care in entering into contract in order to protect the interest of the municipal government. Further, the Municipal Accountant stop processing the claims if the documents are incomplete.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to design effective tax strategies to intensify collection of Real Property Taxes, not only current taxes but also the delinquent taxes in order to generate more income.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce the tax remedies provided for under Book II of the Local Government Code in the collection of taxes due to the government.

We recommended the BAC in coordination with the Municipal Engineer comply strictly with the requirements of semestral submission of the PMR pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and GPPB Circular no. 03-15.
We recommended that the Municipality comply strictly with COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the information and publicity of programs/projects/activities to be implemented by the agency for the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board ensure that the Special Education Fund be utilized only on programs/projects or activities  that are included among the list of priorities to be funded by SEF pursuant to the provision of Joint Circular No. 01 s 1998 of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM), Department of Education, Culture and Sports (DECS) and Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG), and Section 272 of RA 7160 in order to promote quality education in every public schools of the Municipality of Rosario.

Instruct the LDRRM Officer to meticulously plan the programs and projects necessary in the prevention, mitigation and preparedness of the Municipality in case of calamity and ensure that the programs and projects under the Mitigation Fund be implemented immediately to serve the purpose for which the fund was created; and
Submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month so that timely review and evaluation in terms of compliance with the requirements could be undertaken.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the focal person to implement immediately the required activities on Supplementary Feeding Program based on the set timeline in order to improve and sustain the nutritional status of the target children beneficiaries and ultimately contribute to the success of the program as a whole.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute the membership of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to conform to the membership requirements of the RA 9003 (Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000) and to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned in the law; and require the 26 barangays to re-establish and effectively maintain their materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed waste for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill.  Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal SWM Board strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of RA No. 9003 and its IRR.

We recommended that the Municipality implement immediately program/projects under BUB projects for the development of its constituents.  We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the requirements set-forth in the DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 6 for all the required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to stop the payment of annual dues to various organizations not in conformity with RA 7160 for lack of legal basis; and instruct the Municipal Treasurer that payments made to duly recognized leagues under the law be acknowledged with the prescribed government Accountable Form No. 51 or official receipts duly registered with the Bureau of Internal Revenue.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide and maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledger for each special account and to submit the  trial balances and financial statements pursuant to Sections 105 and 110 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to inform users of the result of the operations as well as financial condition for decision making purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to further review and analyze the details of those payables.  Reconcile the unremitted amount and immediately remit the same to the concerned agencies.  Adjusting entries should be made where appropriate to reflect accurate balances of the accounts in the books as well as in the financial statements.

We reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Mayor and all concerned officials take appropriate actions immediately to come up with a final decision. Further, the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to FS the position of the subject unrecovered deposits which is included in the Other Receivables account to present fairly the financial position of the municipality.

We recommended that the long outstanding payables be reverted to Unappropriated Surplus in pursuant to Republic Act (RA) No. 3526 as implemented by Executive Order (EO) No. 109, s.1993.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately remit the collections due to the national government agencies to augment their resources for programmed projects that will directly benefit the intended beneficiaries, and to avoid possible misuse of the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments in the Accumulated Depreciation of the depreciable Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality and in Government Equity.  Henceforth, he should use the revised residual value which is 5% of the cost, in line with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17.

We recommended the following measures:
The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officers maintain Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively, to record the receipts and issuances of inventory items;
The General Services Officer accomplish the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for the issuances of inventory items and consolidate the same in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI).  The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS should be submitted to the Chief Accountant for the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to record the issuances; and
The General Services Officer conduct the periodic physical count of the inventory items and prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all collections for the Share of the Municipality from Economic Zones are turned over daily by the Collectors to the Liquidating Officer deposited intact the government authorized depository bank.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer find ways and means to address the cash deficit the municipality had been experiencing for several years now by exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to allocate funds out of the regular collections in the General Fund for the transfer of the unexpended balances LDRRMF from Calendar Years 2012 to 2016amounting to P29,316,748.19 to the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that the guidelines formulated by the Municipality on the availment of various social services and donations be submitted to the Sanguniang Bayan for its ratification.

We also recommended that the approving authority and recommending approval submit written justification for allowing the grant of assistance in excess of the maximum amount provided in the guidelines and those grants for financial assistance which are not supported with letter request and case study from the Municipal Social Worker and Development Office.

Further we recommended that management adhere to the established guidelines and policies on the granting of assistance to its constituents for consistency and uniformity, see to it that all claims are duly supported with the required documents to prove its validity and the necessity of the request, and stop the grant of assistance when there is no more funds available.

We recommended the that the concerned department heads of the Municipality, as end-users of the procuring entity, be required to prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to serves as a guide document in the procurement and contract implementation process, as well as a vital reference in monitoring procurement, and to facilitate the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

We also recommended that the Secretariat, Bids and Awards Committee, based on the PPMP, prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in the prescribed form, indicating therein among others, the mode of procurement and the schedule for each procurement activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials adhere to the cited provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to enable the Audit Team to undertake prompt validation of the projects and inform management of any deficiencies noted in audit for appropriate remedial measures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office to furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof by reference or incorporation within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to facilitate the prompt review and evaluation of the contracts and the early communication to management on the deficiencies noted, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Treasurer’s Office to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross sales/ receipts (not only from the Municipal Government of Rosario) for the preceding Calendar Year and other pertinent records/ information for the determination of gross receipts.
Municipal Accountant to submit to the Municipal Treasurer a List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipal Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes in the current year.
Municipal Treasurer to verify/assess the gross receipts of the contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling ₽140,865.18, and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the Municipality’s Revenue Code. Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.
Municipal Treasurer to properly classify and bill taxpayers their business tax in accordance with Section 2A.02of the Municipality’s Revenue Code.
Bids and Awards Committee examine the eligibility documents submitted by the bidders and determine if the latter had paid the correct business taxes taking into consideration the payments for transactions made with the Municipality.

We recommend that management stop the grant of additional cash advances to officials and employees with previous balances and demand for the immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances through the issuance of demand letters copy furnished the Audit Team. In case of non-compliance, instruct the Accounting Office to withhold the salaries or any money due them as provided in Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002. In addition, require the Municipal Accountant the immediate submission of payrolls and liquidation of travels.

We recommend that management adhere with the pertinent provisions of COA Circulars No. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF and COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the proper documentation of transactions. Likewise, the Accountant should rectify the deficiencies noted on the variance per LDRRMF utilization report and per audit and to submit the disbursement vouchers/liquidation reports and the lacking supporting documents.

We recommend that management publish all their procurements in the PhilGEPS website. Generally, public bidding should be used for all procurement  and all alternative modes of procurement be resorted to only if the conditions which allows the use of such alternative method were met pursuant to the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184. Moreover, stop the practice of procurement thru reimbursements.

We recommend that management strictly observe the submission of contracts/purchase orders within five (5) working days from execution/issuance and furnish delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of deliveries of goods and services, in line with COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 002. Likewise, payment of various purchases should be supported with complete documentation as mandated in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for propriety and legality.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board actively participate in the gradual implementation of the approved 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan to protect the constituents and the environment against health and environmental hazards brought about by improper solid waste disposal.

We recommend that management submit the basic documentary requirements to support the payment of wages to job order personnel. The Accountant should see to it that all claims should be supported with complete supporting documents before payments are made.  We further recommend that management stop the charging of wages for garbage collectors, office staffs/aides and street sweepers to 5% LDRRMF and 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to observe strictly Section 347 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of financial accounts and reports including the liquidation/payrolls for salaries and wages and traveling expenses. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial statements on or before February 14 of each year.

We recommend that management limit the grant of overtime services only when extremely necessary, within its appropriations, and supported with proper documents in accordance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s 2015 on the policies and guidelines on overtime services of government personnel, Section 4(1) of PD 1445 and Section 5.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for its basic documentary requirements.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Notices of Disallowance as the case may be.

We recommend that Management should (1) demand for the immediate settlement of outstanding advances; (2) strictly adhere with the governing laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and (3) to refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers unless his/her previous cash advance is liquidated or a proper accounting thereof has already been made.

We recommend that Management instruct the Accountant to control the recording of appropriate account codes based on actual nature of services rendered in conformity with the provisions of the PPSAS to present fairly subject expenditures in the financial statements.
We recommend that the general services office attend to the immediate disposal of these unserviceable equipment and motor vehicles to recoup a much higher value.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Conduct public bidding in the purchases of goods and shopping should only be resorted to in exceptional cases as provided in the IRR.

We recommend that Management directs the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly complies with the provision on mandatory posting of procurement opportunities of the LGU and be reminded that EO 662, as amended by EO 662-A expressly provides that non-compliance with posting obligations shall render the accountable officials liable for dereliction of duty.
We recommend that management requires the suppliers, manufacturers and distributors to establish warranty deposit in accordance with Section 62.1 of Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
We recommend strict compliance with Section 34 of the IRR-A of RA 9184 so bona fide bidder could be awarded the contract.

We recommend that the General Services Office complete the conduct of the inventory taking and submit the complete report to COA.
Require the Accountant also to prepare the adjusting entries on the donated properties which are still recorded in the book of accounts of the LGU.

We recommend that management directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the withheld amount so as not to deprive the government officials and employees of the benefits and privileges due them. The Municipal Treasurer must see to it that remittances of the contributions and withheld premiums and loans to the GSIS and PAG-IBIG shall be given priority over and above the payment of any and all obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees. Also, the Municipal Accountant should check the attached supporting documents and ensure that original copies are submitted to COA.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant properly scrutinize the transactions and require valid and legal complete documents to support the claims pursuant to Sec. 4(6) 0f P.D 1445.

We recommend that management strictly follow the standard that payroll deductions shall be limited following the order of preference provided in the GAA and ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed take home pay which for 2016 is Php3,500.00 as provided under Section 52 of RA No. 10717.
We recommend that Management prioritize those programs, projects and activities stipulated in RA 7160 and refrain from charging expenditures not chargeable against the fund.

The Municipal Planning and Development Council should initiate the proper planning.  Make sure that all conditions and requirement are already in place before the projects are included in the plan for ready implementation. Time frames and target dates should be followed so as not to deprive the constituents to enjoy the developmental projects and the end users of the immediate beneficial use.
Direct the Municipal Planning Officer to supervise more closely and to monitor the project accomplishments by following time frame and target date so as not to deprive end users of its beneficial use.
We recommend that Management prioritize the conversion of the semi controlled dumpsite to a sanitary landfill as the final disposal facility of the LGU’s solid waste in compliance to RA 9003.

observations.managementAction: The General Service Officer has not yet submitted the partial inventory report because because she was claimed that there are still some corrections/adjustments to be done.
Additional manpower, if none, request for overtime if possible.
observations.reason: Reiterated Implemented under No. 4

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department is maintaining a special account for the municipality but was not being submitted to COA. The claimed that they are not aware that this reports were to be submitted to COA.
Provide financial assistance to indigent people in the form of AICS.
Require the contractors or suppliers to put up signboards once they have received their notice of award before proceeding with the project.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the |Municipal Mayor         instruct:              
The Municipal           GSO-Designate to: a)    forward copy of the     acceptance and          Inspection Report (AIR) |to the Municipal        Accountant and maintain|stock cards for supplies|and materials to account |for the receipt and      disposition; b)         consolidate weekly the  Requisition and Issue     Slip (RIS) using the    Summary of supplies and |Materials Issued (SSMI); |and c) submit to the     Municipal Accountant the|SSMI together with the   original copies of RIS;  
The GSO and other       concerned official to   conduct physical count  of inventory items every |semester and prepare the |Report on the Physical  Count of Inventory; and    
The Municipal Accountant|to: a) record the       purchased as inventory  and the issuance based  on the AIR and SSMI     submitted by the GSO; b)|maintain the supplies   ledger cards (SLC) for  each commodity/stock.   Such ledger cards shall |contain the details of  the supplies in the       inventory control       account in the general  ledger; and c) use the    moving average method of |costing inventories.
observations.managementAction: Office Supplies and  Other Supplies and  Materials were treated as Inventory account.
observations.reason:
Only two Job    Order personnel |were assigned to|assist the GSO.
Lack of         personnel.
Maintenance of  ledger cards for|each item was   not yet done by|the accounting  office. Lack of|personnel was   also the reason.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Mayor:        Create a group that     would undertake a       thorough study and      evaluation on the actual|Municipality for each    department/ offices to |minimize government     spending.              Require all department  heads to submit the     unit’s workload and     require number of        personnel particularly   those in the Sangguniang|Bayan, Office of the    Mayor and Administrator.|Instruct the Accountant  to verify the           deficiencies noted in   the payroll and examine |the completeness of     supporting documents    before processing for    payment.              Instruct the concerned  officials to pay only    those claims for wages   with appropriate and    duly approved individual  accomplishment reports. |Moreover, stop charging |these JOs to Salaries   and Wages – Casual      account and use the     appropriate account for  the JOs.
observations.managementAction:
A group was created  by the Mayor.
Complied with the recommendation.
Require all department heads to submit the unit’s workload and require number of personnel particularly those in the Sangguniang Bayan, Office of the Mayor and Administrator.
The Accountant is complying with the recommendations.
Instruct the concerned officials to pay only those claims for wages with appropriate and duly approved individual accomplishment reports.
The Municipal Treasurer-ICO is complying with the recommendations.
Moreover, stop charging these JOs to Salaries and Wages – Casual and use the appropriate account for the JOs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Mayor instruct   the Municipal Treasurer |and other concerned     officials to adopt measures|and strategies to improve|collection efficiency.    Enforce the remedies    provided under Section    254, 256 and 259 of RA|7160 in order to improve  the financial position,    operation and development|of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management sent demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and also sought the assistance of barangay officials to locate delinquent taxpayers in their respective barangays. Several accounts were also transferred to the Provincial Treasurer Office for collection.
observations.reason: Tax             delinquencies   increased       substantially.

observations.recommendation: We respectfully         recommended that the    Sangguniang Bayan of    Rosario stop the        practice implementing the|program/s and projects of |the LGU and focus on their|mandates of enacting      ordinances, approving    resolutions and appropriating|funds for the general     welfare of the municipality|and its inhabitants. Limit|disbursements for the     necessary and crucial     expenses needed for the  performance of its        legislative function only. |We also recommended that|the concerned SB        officials submit written  justification for the     continuous disregard of   prior year’s audit        recommendation which could|be a good for disallowance|/refund of the total      amount disbursed beyond  their mandated functions.
observations.managementAction: Sangguniang Bayan had limited their disbursements for only the necessary and crucial expenses for the performance of their legislative function.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the  |concerned elected        officials refund the     total amount of P112,030|and follow strictly the   provision under CSC and  DMB Joint Circular No. 01 |in giving step increments|elected officials.
observations.managementAction: The total amount of P103,440.00 and P8,620.00 representing overpayment of salary and 13th month differentials of the three councilors were already refunded

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Treasurer     prioritize the release of |the barangays’ shares and |release it within five days|after end of each quarter, |pursuant to Section 271 (d)|of RA No. 7160 to provide |fund augmentation on the  finances of the barangays.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer-ICO released the shares of the barangays within five days after end of each quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Mayor:        Reconstitute the        Municipal Solid         Management Board (MSWMB)|to conform to the       membership requirements |of the RA 9003            |(Ecological Solid Waste  Management Act of 2000) and|to optimize the environment|of civil society as envisioned|in the law;              The Municipal Solid Waste|Management Board (MSWMB) |10-years Solid Waste      |Management Plan has       |already been approved.    Review and if warranted,   revise or update the      Municipality’s 10-Year   Solid Waste Management Plan|to conform to the minimum|components end elements  required under RA 9003|consistent with the       National Solid Waste    Management Framework and|to ensure its sustainability,|relevance and effectiveness; and|Require the barangays to  re-establish and         effectively maintain their|materials recovery facility|that will receive the mixed|waste for final sorting, |segregation, composting |or recycling before       transferring the residual |wastes to sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor had reorganized its Municipal Solid Management Board (MSWMB) through Administrative  Order No.  5 s. 2016.
The Municipality’s 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan has already been approved.
The Municipal Mayor had required all the 48 instructed the barangays to re-establish and maintain their Materials Recovery Facility (MRF).
observations.reason:
Still the       composition of  the Board was|not in          accordance with|the requirement of Section 12 of|RA No. 9003.
Several         barangays had  only started|complying in 2nd|quarter of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Mayor:        Ensure that the 20%     Development Fund is      fully utilized for the   intended purposes to help |achieve desirable socio-  economic development and |environment outcome.     Procure lot that is free  from hindrances for the  immediate implementation|of the project.
observations.managementAction: A survey was done by the MPDC to the barangays to determine the need for the necessary development projects direct from the people.
observations.reason: Some projects   were still      on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Mayor require |the Focal Point/Person to |plan and implement       projects and programs    that will directly        benefit women. Consider |the potential and        inherent skills of women |in the formulation of     strategies and bases that |will be use in designing  priority projects and      programs for a GAD Plan  responsive to its prescribed|thrust and vision.
observations.reason: This observation|was reiterated  in Part II of|this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the |Municipal Disaster Risk   Reduction and Management|Officer (MDRRMO)        together with other      concerned local          officials meticulous plan |the program and projects  necessary in the prevention,|mitigation and           
preparedness of the LGU  in case of calamity, for|incorporation in the LDRRMF|Investment purposes     enumerated in the approved|investment plan. Likewise, |submit the Report on the  Sources and Utilization of the|Disaster Risk Reduction   Management Fund (DRRMF) to |the office of the Auditor on |or before the 15th day    after the end month so that|timely review and       evaluation in the terms of|compliance with the       requirements could not be hampered.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had filled up the vacant position for a Local DRRM Officer.
observations.reason:
The LDRRM       Officer had only|assumed office  in the last |quarter of 2016.
The LDRRM       Officer was not aware of the|regulation on   submission of|the Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Treasurer and   other concerned official|observe the provision of  Section 337 of the Local|Government Code of 1991 |and limit the expenses    within available income |and see to it that the   expenses are among the   priority expenditures under|SEF.
observations.reason: This observation|was reiterated  in Part II of|this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the |Municipal Mayor instruct   all concerned a official   to grant cash advances   for payment of wages to   Job Order Personnel     equal to the net amounts  of the payrolls for the pay |periods and   its liquidation report be|supported by required     documents pursuant to the|provisions of Section 4.2.1,|4.2.2 and 5.10.1 of COA No.|97-002 dated February 10,|1997.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances granted for payment of wages to Job Order Personnel were equal to the net payrolls.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that |the Municipal Accountant  submit immediately all the 95|the Disbursement Vouchers|with the supporting documents and Journal Entry|Vouchers, and strictly    observe the provisions of|COA Circular 94-001.
observations.managementAction: The 95 Disbursement Vouchers were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the:  Municipal Accountant refrain from recording |under Account 969 those  expenses with specific|accounts provided in the  Chart of Accounts under   COA Circular 2004-008; |Concerned Budget and     Accounting Personnel observe the proper|classification and       recording of expenses   incurred as provided for |under COA Circular No.   2004-008 dated September  |20, 2004; and            The Municipal Accountant |reclassify expenses       erroneously charged to    Other Maintenance and    Operating Expenses to    its appropriate expense   account.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant is recording expenses to its proper account.
The Municipal Accountant already charged the said expenses to their proper expense accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the   MSWD Officer intensify  collection of overdue     accounts from the         beneficiaries to maintain|source of funding and keep|the program moving. On   the other hand, the agency |should further strengthen|the monitoring  and     coordination among the   different departments in |volved in the            implementing of the MBA|projects to have a       successful program of the|municipality.           We also recommend that   official concerned revisit|the program and institute  measure to strengthen and |improve its implementation  and collection.
observations.managementAction: Concerned personnel tried to locate the beneficiaries with overdue accounts.
observations.reason: Still some      beneficiaries   could not  be   found.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the   Municipal Mayor and     Accountant exert extra    effort to collect all past |due receivables from      officers and employees   concern by sending demand|letters to settle their    cash advances immediately.|Management could also    avail of other remedies  such as withholding of their|salary or any amount due  to them or initiate filing of   appropriate charges       against defaulting        officials and employees.  The Municipal Accountant|monitor closely the     liquidation of all        current advances to      prevent the further       accumulation of unliquidated|cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Prior years cash advances has been fully liquidated as of December 31, 2015
Close monitoring of liquidation of prior years’ cash advances has been effected.
The Municipal Accountant monitor closely the liquidation of all current advances to prevent the further accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We respectfully         recommend that the     Sangguniang Bayan of    Rosario should stop       disbursing funds for the |above mentioned          activities/programs.  Their function is to enact ordinances,|approve resolutions and  appropriate funds for the   general welfare of the    municipality and its       inhabitants, thus, only   necessary and crucial     expenses needed for the  performance of its        legislative function are|allowed.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan has stopped disbursing funds beyond its mandate.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that       Management strictly     adhere to the law on the  granting of Productivity   Enhancement Incentive to|its officials and         employees and employees |which should be uniformly |given to all government   personnel including those|from the local government |units. Cause the immediate |refund of the excess PEI   received in 2014 in the    amount of P30,000.00    each.
observations.managementAction: Management had decided to file a Request for Reconsideration from COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to prioritize the complete reconciliation of the balances of the PPE accounts to make certain the reliability, existence and correctness of the  Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and General Services Offices continue to reconcile their records and had able to reduce the difference between the balances of RPCPPE and General Ledger to P112,119,901.91 in 2016.
observations.reason: Lack of adequate records pertaining to acquisition of PPE prior to 2004 for infrastructure projects and prior to 2007 for all other PPEs.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer exercised diligence in the disposition of funds.



Remittances of contributions to GSIS, Pag-Ibig and other agencies are updated. 





Remittances to BIR for taxes withheld and Provincial Treasurer for share in property taxes are up to date.
observations.reason: Cash overdraft still exists due to expanding social services being provided by the LGU to its marginalized constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to allocate funds out of the regular collections for the transfer of the unexpended balances of CYs 2012, 2013, 2014 and 2015 LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction:
In November 2016, management formulated and finalized the five-year plan for the utilization of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF.  

The amount of P2.8 Million was transferred to DRRMF in Trust Fund books representing unexpended balances of 2015  and the same was utilized for the purchase of two units of dump truck.
observations.reason: The Municipality still experienced cash deficit in 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to stop the practice of charging prior years obligations against current appropriation. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that all claims for obligation at the end of the year are booked up as Accounts Payable. Compliance to Section 350 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and Section 4 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs) must be strictly adhered to for fair presentation of the financial statements.

Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the completed project valued at P1,439,421.02 from  Construction in Progress to its appropriate PPE account.
observations.managementAction:
The infrastructure projects were charged to Continuing Appropriation of the particular year.  The Municipal Accountant booked up all unpaid obligations of the Municipality as of December 31, 2016 as Accounts Payables.  









Journal Entry Voucher No. 06-20 dated June 30, 2016 was drawn by the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the completed project to its appropriate PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the withheld taxes due the BIR amounting to P2,823,612.06, and henceforth, remittances shall be within ten (10) days following the end of the month the withholding was made  in compliance  with Section 9(c) of BIR Regulation No. 6-97.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had analyzed the balance of Due to BIR as of December 31, 2015 and reclassified the portion thereof representing refund of taxes withheld to Due to Officers and Employees.  Remittances to BIR had been updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Service Officer to:

exert more effort to obtain records/documents of all green plated motor vehicles owned by the Municipality for a complete database of information and to facilitate registration and insurance thereof; and

insure all government motor vehicles and other transportation equipment under the Property Insurance Fund administered by the Government Service Insurance System.
observations.managementAction:
The General Services Office had coordinated with the Land Transportation Office for the registration of the second hand motor vehicles still in the name of its previous owners.  


The General Services Office insured all vehicles in the name of the Municipality with the GSIS Property Insurance Fund and still awaiting registration/ transfer of ownership of those with private plates.
observations.reason: The Municipality took time in gathering the documentary requirements of the LTO and still awaiting confirmation from LTO Manila East Branch

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials/employees to refund the excess travelling allowance totaling P927,473.46 and henceforth, follow the provisions of Executive Order No. 298 in claiming said allowance to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction:
Concerned employees refunded the excess claims in travelling allowance.

Claims for travelling expenses were now based on the prescribed rates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adopt a written policy setting the rule on the officials who are entitled to mobile expenses and the monthly limit that each qualified official may be entitled.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum No. 038 s of 2016 was issued to the Department Heads by the Municipal Administrator allowing the former to avail of only one mobile line in their account. In like manner, Memorandum No. 03 s of 2016 was issued to the Sanguniang Bayan Members by the Vice Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the accounting policy under Section 4(j) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I, which prescribed that regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory account for fair presentation of the affected account.
observations.managementAction: Food supplies purchased by the Municipality intended for distribution to its constituents were booked up in Food Supplies Inventory Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to enable the Audit Team to undertake prompt validation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the 2016 fourth quarter report for government projects charged to 20% Development Fund only.
observations.reason: There is a need for the concerned department head to revisit COA Circular No.2013-004 for clear understanding of the requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the 220 disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents involving a total amount of P14,334,562.30 to determine the validity, regularity and propriety of the covering transactions. 

Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).
observations.managementAction:
Out of 220 subject disbursement vouchers, 219 had been submitted by the Accounting Department to the Office of the Auditor and the remaining one was refunded by the claimant for his inability to locate the same. 




Disbursement Vouchers pertaining to 2016 transactions were all submitted to the Office of the Auditor as of February 15, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the ICO Municipal Accountant to record all purchases as inventories and the custodian to render a report of all issuances from stock. Likewise, physical inventory should be conducted at year-end.
observations.managementAction: In 2016, the Accountant already recorded its inventory items as inventories but issuance from stocks were not still accounted for due to non-preparation of RIS and SSMI.
observations.reason: Small value inventory items were recorded as outright expense and were distributed to the end-users shortly after their delivery.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of equating the LGU share on the monthly premium contributions to HDMF with the personal share of employees whose contributions exceed the maximum amount of ₱100.00 per employee per month. We also recommend that management make representations with the management of HDMF to consider the over-remittance of HDMF premiums as advance payment on premium contributions of the LGU, otherwise, recover the over-remittance of employer’s contribution from the LGU employees thru refund.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the practice of equating the municipality’s share on monthly contributions to HDMF with the personal share of employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the immediate liquidation of unliquidated cash advances and stop the grant of additional cash advances to those with previous unsettled balances in accordance to COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Management accepted the deficiency and will try to hold salaries to those accountable officers with outstanding balances.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Finding No. 1 of this report. | Significant amount of cash advances were still outstanding as of year-end but was liquidated in the ensuing year. However, liquidation reports for the current year were not submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to exert earnest efforts to intensify collections in order to generate more income for the Municipality. Likewise, look into the cause of the decrease of income in order to improve operations.
observations.managementAction: To revise Municipal Revenue Code; to hire additional collection personnel; and to impose more collection strategies on the tax collection.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in monitoring the remittances of collections of all revenue collectors to preclude misappropriation of the meager resources of the government, otherwise he shall be jointly and solidarily liable with them for any loss of government funds under his control laboratory supplies.
observations.managementAction: The management still remit the collections late but not up to 88 days.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002:
Advice the Municipality Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
To compel all regular and special disbursing officers to liquidate their outstanding cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated
The Municipal Accountant should strictly observe the rules and regulations regarding the grant and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The management already liquidated the cash advances but not the full amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to always adopt the perpetual inventory method of recording the procurement of drugs and medicines and the medical, dental and laboratory supplies
observations.managementAction: The Management already acted on the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct those concerned officials and employees to properly allocate expenditures to what is required for the medical care and health benefits of the public.

We recommended to direct the Municipal Planning Officer to supervise more closely and to monitor the project accomplishments by following time frame and target date so as not to deprive end users of its beneficial use
observations.managementAction: The management implemented some of the programs/projects.
